name: Gran Hotel Costa Rica, Curio Collection by Hilton
review_text:
I’m giving the Gran Hotel in Costa Rica 2 stars because while there were a few positives, several issues made our stay disappointing.

The room itself appeared mostly clean, and the room service food was actually very good and arrived quickly, which we appreciated. I also had a positive experience with the afternoon valet service. They were friendly and brought my vehicle up to the front of the hotel like you would expect.

Unfortunately, there were several problems during our stay. The biggest issue was finding a huge spider in our room that was literally the size of an orange. It completely freaked my wife and me out and made us paranoid for the rest of the stay. After that, we were constantly checking around the room and honestly almost decided to leave and book a different hotel.

Another issue was the chargers/outlets beside the bed. Some of them didn’t work, which I didn’t realize until the next morning when I woke up and my phone was only at 12%. Not the best way to start the day when you’re traveling and relying on your phone.

The WiFi in the room was also extremely poor. It only worked when standing right near the door. Anywhere else in the room the network couldn’t even be found, which made using the internet frustrating.

The morning valet service was another disappointment. Instead of bringing my car up to the front of the hotel like the afternoon valet did, they simply handed me my keys and told me where it was parked. When I went to get my vehicle, I couldn’t even drive out because the other valet vehicles were parked extremely close around it. I had to ask the valet to move multiple cars just so I could get out, which was difficult due to the language barrier.

We also tried to eat at the hotel restaurant around 7pm. We stood by the sign that said “wait to be seated” for quite a while while several employees walked by and seemed to avoid acknowledging us. After waiting long enough with no help, we eventually left the restaurant and ended up leaving the hotel to get McDonald’s instead.

On top of that, I tried to ask the front desk a few questions through the hotel’s app multiple times, but the messages were never answered.

Overall, while the room was mostly clean and the room service and afternoon valet were good, the giant spider, poor WiFi, broken chargers, frustrating morning valet experience, lack of response from the front desk, and being ignored at the restaurant made the stay disappointing.

name: Gran Hotel Costa Rica, Curio Collection by Hilton
review_text:
Decepção com este hotel:

A experiência foi bastante aquém do esperado. A conexão de internet é instável e pouco confiável; desistimos de utilizar o Wi-Fi e fomos obrigados a permanecer em roaming 4G, ainda assim com sinal fraco (em torno de três barras).

As mini-geladeiras de ambos os quartos ocupados não funcionavam, o que gerou um problema logístico sério para o armazenamento da insulina do nosso filho, que é diabético tipo 1.

Além disso, não é um hotel adequado para quem está de carro. O serviço de manobrista é caro, ineficiente e demorado, impactando negativamente a mobilidade durante a estadia.

Quando se encontram estabelecimentos onde itens básicos não funcionam adequadamente, a impressão que fica é de uma clara deficiência de gestão.

Não recomendo este hotel, a menos que haja uma necessidade específica de se hospedar nesta localização do centro histórico, próxima ao teatro. Fora isso, o padrão geral é medíocre.

Restaurante.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text:
Après avoir lu certains avis 1 étoile, il devient évident que le problème ne vient pas du restaurant, mais du niveau de compréhension de ceux qui évaluent.

On ne juge pas une table gastronomique comme on note un comptoir de fast-food. Comparer ce type d’établissement à des standards basiques, c’est simplement démontrer une absence totale de repères en matière de restauration.

Ici, tout est maîtrisé : précision du dressage (légumes posés un à un à la pince), assiettes à température contrôlée, viandes maturées en cuisine… Chaque détail reflète un vrai savoir-faire et des coûts bien réels.

Et parlons franchement du prix : le rapport qualité-prix est excellent pour ce niveau d’exécution. Critiquer les tarifs sans comprendre ce qui se trouve dans l’assiette relève plus de l’ignorance que d’un avis objectif.

Si cela ne convient pas, il existe de nombreux endroits touristiques standardisés qui correspondent mieux à certaines attentes — mais dans ce cas, il faut rester cohérent dans ses évaluations.

Le service est irréprochable : rapide, fluide, attentif sans être intrusif. Mention spéciale à Romeu, dont le professionnalisme et l’efficacité ont contribué à rendre l’expérience encore plus agréable.

Au-delà de l’assiette, le lieu lui-même mérite d’être souligné : un bâtiment ancestral, mis en valeur avec respect, où chaque détail témoigne de la fierté d’un patrimoine local et de son histoire. Pour un visiteur, c’est un véritable lieu chargé de mémoire, qui rappelle la richesse simple d’un pays et le chemin qu’il a parcouru. C’est exactement le type d’endroit où un touriste international peut mesurer le cachet, l’élégance et la beauté propre à ce pays.

Pour ma part, expérience parfaitement alignée avec les standards d’un établissement haut de gamme. Rien à reprocher.

Merci à toute l’équipe pour  cette  merveilleuse expérience!!

Eric et Marie ✨

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Es un lugar de gusto exquisito, desde la decoración hasta los platillos y la forma en que los presentan. La música de ambiente, y la decoración de la terraza te hace sentir en algún rinconcito de Europa. El personal es muy atento, y la relación precio-calidad es muy buena. Se ha convertido en mi lugar favorito en San José 🤗

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: One of my favourite restaurants in San Jose, reasonably priced for what it seems to be a high end restaurant. Beautiful atmosphere. Servers were attentive and professional. Got the lamb chop and it was soooo delicious. My girlfriend really enjoyed her beef risotto as well.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text:
Incredibly good food. The kitchen takes *excellent* care of every portion of each dish. The blackened tomatoes with dill were a side and could have easily been the star of the show.

Their Marquis de Chocolate was incredible. I would not have paired dark chocolate with gorgonzola, but they did and I love them for it.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Buen servicio

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Una lástima que un lugar tan hermoso tenga un menú de comidas tan “extraño”. Desde mi punto de vista es un restaurante demasiado caro y su comida no es de admirar. Adicionalmente desde la entrada su menú indica que es comida costarricense, pero sus platos no tienen nada que ver con la cocina “tica”. El lugar es hermoso, pero un domingo a la 1 de la tarde completamente vacío, desde ahí vimos que algo andaba mal.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: El mesero Alberto (o Álvaro) tiene pésimo servicio al cliente. Llegué a las 5 p.m. y sólo quería un postre y un café. Alberto nos recibió de mala manera, apenas nos dirigió la palabra. Le dije que quería un brownie y se rió de forma burlona diciendo que ahí no venden brownies. Después se fué sin decir nada y cuando volvió nos dijo que no estaban atendiendo a, aún cuando solamente había una mesa ocupada. Yo trabajo en turismo de lujo y nunca me habían recibido tan mal.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Solo comimos en el restaurante, como siempre el servicio y la atención excelente, la comida espectacular, no ha ninguna observación sobre la comida, es uno de los mejores restaurantes de Costa Rica

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: El restaurante es muy bueno, buena atención, buena comida. Diría que un clásico para los que les gusta comer bien.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Definitely more of a Fine dining establishment vibe, when we saw how highly rated it was we just went and were not dressed to impress. Even so the staff was giving us every courtesy and made sure everything was to our liking. The Fruit smoothie was great, the Ravioli de la Casa with the Pumpking sauce was delicious. And desert was amazing.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: I didn’t like this hotel. The staff is simply rude and just plain obnoxious. The lack of good manners is avoidable if you stay somewhere else. There is nothing luxurious about it. I don’t know if it was because we were a gay couple or something else. We are a couple over 60 and have traveled the world and this place has nothing charming about it. The restaurant staff in the breakfast service is the worst I have ever encountered. I’m giving my room a 4 start because it was cleaned and it was quiet.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: The restaurant we needed in San Jose - after some pretty terrible experiences with food here this place redeemed everything. Great decor amazing food

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Il posto è suggestivo e meraviglioso. Sembrava di essere tornata nella bella epoque. Il cibo me lo aspettavo meglio considerando le aspettative che avevo per come si presentava il tutto ma comunque buono. Non ho dormito nell hotel.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Restaurant is top notch, comparable to any high quality EU or USA restaurant. Decoration is adorable , the food is gourmet , so expect a small dish but the quality of the food is 10/10. Service is also great. I would recommend this place hands down. Keep up the good work.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text:
I’ve visited this place more than 15 times over the past 10 years, but it no longer amazes me. The service has become arrogant and slow, showing clear preference toward older local customers or foreign-language-speaking guests.
I always order the same dishes, yet this time the portions were noticeably smaller. The tarte Tatin lacked proper caramelization and tasted bland—and unfortunately, the service matched that same lack of sweetness.
With so many great restaurants in Costa Rica offering better quality and value, this one isn’t worth your time or money anymore. Perhaps stop by just for a drink if you want to admire the beautifully preserved old building—but that’s really all it has going for it now.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Café delicioso, repostería riquísima, atención excelente, espacio agradable, una linda vista

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Buscaba un lugar bonito, elegante y privado para una celebración y encontré que manejan unas salas aparte para este tipo de casos, muy recomendado. El servicio fue excelente, brindaron platos vegetarianos para las excepciones y encontré general todo lo planean muy profesionalmente.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Uno de los mejores restaurantes en los que he comido. Agradezco el detalle del pastel por nuestro aniversario. Esperamos la próxima vez hospedarnos en este hermoso lugar.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: Espectacular y delicioso

review_text: Excelente

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro

Por nuestra celebración tuvieron la cortesía de obsequiarnos un postre.Si lo que buscan es un lugar tranquilo y agradable para una celebración o simplemente pasar un rato en un lugar deferente definitivamente éste es el lugar indicado. Recomendado 100%, sin duda volveremos.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text:
Esta esquinita mágica de San Bosco, en San José, me transporta en el tiempo y me encanta 🫀💌
El hotel siempre ha sido precioso y lo mantienen impecable ⭐️
Ese día estaba trabajando y necesitaba un rinconcito con buen internet y café caliente para esa tarde lluviosa ☕🦦
Pedí un capuchino con sirope de vainilla, rico y pero un poco más dulce de lo que me gusta, pero el verdadero highlight fue la tarta de manzana 🍎💫
Literalmente solo manzana!! dulcita, tibia, con ese toque de canela y el helado derritiéndose encima🍨

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text: The restaurant at Hotel Grano de Oro is super beautiful, one of the nicest dinning places in San Jose, but it has a problem and is its chef. I mean... food is not bad but it's average... The quality of the ingredients can improve, for example instead of commercial butter they can use artisan and things like that. I ordered as a starter a beef tartare and it lacked of flavor and the beef wasn't very good for the price they charged. The beef raviolis were homemade which is something good but the stuffing tasted old, like fridge flavor and the use of canned tomatoes was notable in the sauce. The concept is classic French cuisine with some local touches but honestly the chef needs to step his game in French cuisine. I think people does not complain about the place because it is beautiful and it's customer service is great, but culinary wise is nothing memorable or to die for.

name: Restaurante Grano De Oro
review_text:
This restaurant is part of the charming Grano de Oro hotel but is open to non-guests as well and it came highly recommended, for good reason. Breakfast was outstanding and the outdoor seating very pleasant. Indoors looks very chic as well, as a 19th century hacienda.
Service was pleasant and quick. Breakfast choices were plenty but of course you have to try a traditional tico breakfast.
We returned a few days later for dinner and while not quite as good as the breakfast, it was well worth the return visit.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: We stayed one night in advance of flying out of Costa Rica to be close to the airport. The property is pretty but, the walls are paper thin and even the outside windows don’t keep the noise out. We called and complained multiple times about the noise and the hotel didn’t seem to care about quiet hours. There was a wedding going on and the noise from the outside was unbearable. We were on the 4th floor and you could hear people at 5am talking outside. This is not the place to stay if you are looking for a good night sleep before a long day of traveling.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: I had wonderful stay in Hacienda Belen. I really enjoyed walking around the premises, gardens are beautiful. The highlight of the day was the breakfast - the cook Antonio has magical hands and prepares amazing food. He was also so kind that to find for me corn tortillas since I can't eat too much wheat. Jeffrey was also great, always remembering about my almond milk and teaching me about Costa Rican food. I highly recommend trying Budin cake when you get a chance.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: Absolutely stunning Marriott property. From time you step onto the property it is spectacular. The attentiveness of the staff, the details they pay attention to and the overall ambience makes this a world class property. The grounds are stunning with lush foliage, sustainable fruits and veggies growing the grounds that they used in house. There are 2 pools and a jacuzzi. A playground for the kids. A massive driving range for golf lovers. There is also a mountain view from the property. The hotel offers 5 restaurants including a steak and sushi restaurant, a sports bar with many TVs and a 24/7 cafe with luxurious desserts.

I canceled within two hours of booking in the hope that they would be flexible with providing a credit towards a future stay. They absolutely would not. I lost $600 for a two-night stay. I'm a Bonvoy member. Marriott used to be our go-to hotel; we're very loyal to them, but it doesn't seem they care about their customers. Customer service is the worst!

review_text: The Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belén is the place to be if you need to stay in San José for one or two days.||First of all, the building itself is stunning, and the surrounding gardens are beautifully maintained. It feels like a peaceful oasis and offers a striking contrast to the rest of San José, while still being conveniently close to the airport.||The rooms are slightly noisy, but the staff more than make up for it—they are exceptionally friendly, attentive, and service-oriented.||The gym is excellent. We were hoping to play pickleball, but unfortunately there was no net available.||The swimming pools are a very welcome addition and add to the relaxed, resort-like atmosphere.||For lunch and dinner, the restaurants La Castilla, La Isabela, and Bar 10 are all highly recommended. For breakfast, I would skip the relatively expensive buffet and instead go to Casa del Café, where you can enjoy a delicious à la carte breakfast along with excellent coffee and pastries.||In terms of price, it was comparable to an airport hotel, but the experience felt much more like a mini vacation.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
You’re absolutely right — let me rewrite it with the language a Marriott General Manager actually needs to justify a promotion or raise. GMs respond to business impact, guest retention, and brand differentiation. Here’s the upgraded version:

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ “The People Who Make a Hotel Unforgettable — And Why They Deserve to Be Rewarded”

As a Marriott Bonvoy Ambassador who has stayed at properties nearly 2000 nights across the world, I can say with absolute conviction: it is never the marble lobby or the thread count that earns loyalty. It is the people.

Nearly a year and a half ago, my family and I stayed at the Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belén for close to three months during one of the most challenging seasons of my life. And yet — because of the extraordinary human beings at this hotel — my children still ask to go back. That is the most powerful endorsement I could ever give.

The Executive Lounge team became our anchor. Joanna and Lorena brought warmth and consistency every single day. But there is one person I must single out: Verónica.

What makes Verónica exceptional is not just kindness — it is elite-level hospitality instinct. She anticipates needs before they’re expressed. She remembers preferences without being reminded. She treats every guest — whether staying one night or three months — with the same genuine care. She handles high-pressure moments with grace and professionalism. She builds emotional connections that turn guests into lifelong brand ambassadors. These are not soft skills. These are the hardest skills in hospitality to find, and the most valuable.

The fact that we have stayed in touch across borders, languages, and cultures over the past year and a half tells you everything. A guest-staff relationship that becomes a genuine friendship is the rarest thing in this industry — and it is exactly the kind of human connection that no app, no renovation, and no points program can replicate.

A direct message to Marriott Bonvoy leadership: Professionals like Verónica are the reason Ambassador-level families like mine stay loyal. Not the installations. Not the points. Her. She doesn’t just represent your brand — she elevates it. She is the reason we forgive any imperfection, the reason we recommend this property to others, and the reason we will return.

People like Verónica deserve more than a thank-you email. They deserve career advancement, leadership development, and compensation that reflects the revenue and loyalty they generate. She has every quality of a future hospitality leader — invest in her accordingly.

Thank you, Verónica, Joanna, and Lorena. You hold a permanent place in our family’s heart. 💛

— Angelika- TheJWgirl, Marriott Bonvoy Ambassador

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: Tiene muy buena infraestructura, el punto a mejorar es que la búsqueda de eficiencias en costos afecta mucho la calidad, los pequeños detalles a los que estamos acostumbrados los miembros de la cadena no están presentes en este hote, no ofrecen agua de cortesía, no cabían las toallas diariamente, a pesar de dejar la TDC como garantía te obligan a pagar el desayuno el último de la estancia ya que alegan tener problemas con el sistema lo cual ha sido frecuente o casualidad el día de mi salida, no entregan una factura válida de ese cobro, no te ofrecen un sobre para la factura del alojamiento. En fin la austeridad se nota y lo que me dicen es que la tarifa que está pactada con mi empresa regular las condiciones, en resumen no vale de nada ser miembro de la cadena y por tener un contrato tenemos menos detalles de cortesía.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
This is a beautiful hotel with spectacular pools and grounds. It seems to be family oriented which wasn't exciting for me, disliking children, but on the whole they seemed well behaved. Two of the pictures I attach are from my room showing the morning view and the others were from the prior evening of the facility. The morning I posted these pictures, today in fact, it is very pleasant outside with about 72°. However the ambience was ruined listening to a groundsman using a terribly loud leaf blower on the tennis court. I will be telling the front desk that they have to do something about the noise created by these infernal machines. There are quieter ones available.

I did not rate the location, but I can tell you this hotel is near nothing, so choose this place if you want to have a "stay at the resort" type of vacation but if you want to see sites, stay in San Jose.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: Look, this is a Marriot and I suppose, true to form, the rooms are generic and quite basic. The bed was comfortable though. The aircon had two settings: freezer or off. I like the property though, and the breakfast was quite good too. Take a walk around the gardens. Be careful of rooms facing palm trees that have swarms of parrots crowing evening and early morning. The staff try really hard and service is generally good. Lots of folk from the US, so noisy, and very slow as everyone tries to use their points to pay for their daily included beer etc. The surrounding area is very Central America outskirts of the airport.

review_text: Wonderful hotel in nearly every way - but beware the "complimentary" airport shuttle only runs every TWO HOURS. They use this inconvenience to try to upsell you to a private shuttle.

review_text: Desde el momento en que llegué, las instalaciones del hotel superaron todas mis expectativas: espacios hermosos, impecablemente cuidados, con un nivel de aseo y detalle que realmente marca la diferencia. Todo transmite orden, armonía y excelencia.|Sin embargo, lo que hace verdaderamente especial a este lugar es su gente.|Quiero resaltar profundamente la calidad humana de todo el equipo. Tengo una discapacidad visual, y en cada momento encontré no solo apoyo, sino una disposición genuina, cálida y respetuosa. Nunca me sentí incómodo ni limitado; por el contrario, cada persona estuvo atenta, disponible y con una sensibilidad admirable que va más allá del servicio convencional.|El restaurante merece una mención aparte: desde la bienvenida hasta cada interacción, la amabilidad fue extraordinaria. La atención no solo es eficiente, es humana, cercana y auténtica. La calidad del servicio y el trato recibido son, sinceramente, difíciles de calificar… están muy por encima de lo esperado.|Este no es solo un hotel con excelentes instalaciones; es un lugar donde las personas hacen la diferencia. Gracias por una experiencia que deja huella.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen

review_text: Atención excelente

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
Das Marriott Belén bei San José ist eines dieser Hotels, die sich eher wie ein kleines Resort anfühlen als wie ein klassisches Stadthotel. Schon beim Ankommen fällt die besondere Atmosphäre auf: eine ehemalige Kaffeeplantage, gestaltet im kolonialen Hacienda-Stil, umgeben von tropischen Gärten und mit Blick auf die Berge.

Die Anlage ist weitläufig und ruhig – perfekt, um nach einem Flug anzukommen oder vor der Weiterreise nochmal durchzuatmen. Besonders angenehm sind die gepflegten Außenbereiche mit zwei Pools, viel Platz zum Entspannen und schönen Spazierwegen durch das Grün.

Auch kulinarisch bietet das Hotel einiges: mehrere Restaurants mit abwechslungsreicher Küche, ein sehr gutes Frühstücksbuffet und insgesamt eine solide bis gehobene Qualität, auch wenn manche Gäste die Preise als etwas hoch empfinden.

Was wirklich heraussticht, ist der Service – freundlich, aufmerksam und sehr hilfsbereit, sodass man sich schnell willkommen fühlt. Insgesamt ist das Hotel eine stilvolle und komfortable Basis in der Nähe des Flughafens, die Erholung und gute Erreichbarkeit ideal verbindet.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: Muy cómodos los precios de este hotel y muy cómodas las habitaciones

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: I’ve previously stayed at the Intercontinental Multiplaza but decided to try something different in terms of lodging this trip and glad I did. This hotel delivers a superior product in terms of cleanliness, executive lounge (breakfast is awesome!} and friendliness of staff. No matter which staff member you encounter here, you can tell that they have a genuine interest in making sure you have a flawless stay by greeting you and asking if they can assist with anything. Plus, it’s very convenient to/from the airport with complimentary shuttle service, which you don’t find in many hotels in San Jose. Needless to say, it’s now our ‘go to’ hotel when we travel here.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
Unfortunately, I need to correct and downgrade my experience:
When I checked out, the invoice showed items which I for sure did not consume in the hotel restaurant (I did not even use the restaurant during my entire stay). I was promised to receive a corrected invoice within a few days. They came back to me with a restaurant invoice showing a room number and a name which could or could not be mine (hardly readable) and a signature which is clearly not mine.
Bottom line: despite multiple calls, I am still waiting for the corrected invoice. This behaviour is not acceptable for a hotel of an international brand (I am a loyal Marriott Gold member).

Weitläufigen Hotelareal mit parkähnlichem Gelände. Alles sehr gepflegt und sauber.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: This may be one of the nicest Marriotts I have stayed in! The property was enormous and looked beautiful. The highlight by far was the staff - from the moment we arrived everyone was incredibly friendly, and went above and beyond. We came on Easter, and the staff gave our children chocolate eggs. My youngest had her very special stuffed animals on top of her luggage, and asked the bellman to be careful with them. He proceeded to instruct all the other bellhops that these were extremely important and must be handled with great care! This absolutely melted my heart. They also saw we had some beer with us, and brought us a bucket of ice, glasses, and fresh limes for us! The rooms were clean and lovely. The Hacienda Kitchen also had delightful, helpful and welcoming staff and went above had a lovely dinner. It was a one night stay before flying home, but we enjoyed it so much we discussed planning a trip around visiting here again!

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
Stayed at the Marriott overnight to be close to the airport to get home the following day.

The hotel looks grand and the grounds were immaculate front and back of the hotel.

The room was dated but clean, the pool was noisy with loud dance music that clearly didn’t suit the atmosphere. Both pools were freezing cold with leaves and bugs floating in the lower pool.

Service for drinks around the pool was terrible, a lady with short dark hair first forgot to get us a menu. Then when we asked for another drink said we had to speak to the lady who got us an hour earlier and then forgot to tell her until I’d actually found her 30 minutes later and had already spoken to her.

She actually spent more time chatting with some friends at the side of the pool than serve anyone.

The drinks and food are overpriced and resemble London prices. Our dinner and service at the hacinada was nice, but we ate better food in Sodas around Costa Rica. The breakfast buffet was overpriced and very average. Service was good however.

I’d say this is comfortably a 3* hotel and not 4*.

I’ve never stayed at a Marriott before, but thought they had a good reputation. I doubt very much that I’ll be staying at one again.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: Very friendly staff, super helpful. Shuttles to airport only during specific times. Clean. Air Conditioning++

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
We had a brief but enjoyable stay at this lovely hotel which is pretty close to the airport.
Our flight arrived early and we were very grateful that the super helpful staff gave us an early checkin (like 9:30)
The pool view room was great, clean and modern.
The grounds are nice to walk around with many statues of local animals and signs.  There are also some coffee history bits around.
We also found the food uniformly good at the cafe and restaurant.
I’d recommend getting the executive lounge access, including breakfast.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
I stayed at the Marriott Belén in Costa Rica in January 2026 and unfortunately the experience did not meet expectations.

The most concerning issue was the food quality. At one point, the chicken served in a Caesar salad was visibly grey and clearly not fresh, raising serious concerns about food safety and potential health risks. Other dishes were also disappointing: fish tacos with what appeared to be industrial guacamole and breaded fish with no flavor. Across two different restaurants, the overall food quality was consistently poor.

This is particularly surprising in Costa Rica, where food quality is generally excellent. It is disappointing to see that a brand like Marriott is not able to reflect the local standards.

To their credit, the F&B supervisor handled the situation professionally, apologized, and removed the dish from the bill. However, this kind of issue should not happen in the first place. Proper quality control and kitchen processes must be in place to avoid such situations.

The room, located on the ground floor, was very noisy and poorly soundproofed, making it difficult to rest.

Finally, the reception setup is inadequate for a hotel of this size. With only two counters, there was a constant queue and long waiting times, which significantly impacted the overall experience.

Overall, while some staff reacted appropriately, the hotel falls short on fundamental standards expected from a property of this level.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: I didn’t love this hotel but it served its purpose, which is all I can really ask for. ||We had a long flight in to Costa Rica, and then a long drive ahead of us for our accommodations, so we chose to break up the travel with a night at Hacienda Belen. The property was beautiful and the staff were friendly. We got the Presidential Suite plus two roll away beds for our family of 4 - plus access to the executive lounge, which I think we didn’t fully take advantage of. ||The things that were not impressive to me were the lack of any comforts in our suite. There was nothing in our suite kitchen to make it a functioning kitchen. The bedding was only sheets on any of the beds. I understand we’re in a tropical climate but heavier bedding should at least be available in the closets. There were no robes and zero towel hooks in the entire suite. We had pool towels draped over everything. I guess we should have thrown them on the floor or have left them at the pool but we have kids who transport towels and we like to reuse them at least once. ||We appreciated the on-site restaurants but spent almost $300 US on an only ok dinner for two (another $120 US on room service for the kids) and $60 US for a glass of wine and a margarita by the pool. ||Overall, housekeeping took care of us, we were fed, and slept well in our room. The staff were friendly and the grounds were clean. I was glad we weren’t spending our entire stay at this hotel though. I was grateful for what it was but not comfortable enough for an extended stay.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: We were staying in that hotel for one night . Amazing hotel , maybe even one of the best in this city . Rooms were authentic and clean . It’s good location next to airport, cause we just arrived in Costa Rica and we needed some good place to sleep . Breakfast were delicious 🙃 Fresh food and good taste . Service is also good . I lost my phone there , but the stuff found it and gave it to me back . Thank u , guys ! Hotel is not new , but it’s clean and has a nice atmosphere. But I think price for the room which we’ve got was a bit expensive. We took a room for 400 $ and on pictures it were bigger .

review_text:
Our group arrived at 2:00 on a Saturday and there was music blaring from the pool area that was so loud it made the floor vibrate on the 4th floor. Being at the pool was unbearable. The hotel is hosting two parties this afternoon to the detriment of the peace, quiet, and comfort for all the guests in the entire hotel. We had three rooms total and the music was loud in each one. I’ve never heard music played at this volume unless I voluntarily entered a club or attended a concert.

If you want peace and quiet know that parties are prioritized over patrons. Check for events before booking.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text:
100% Marriott pricing. 0% Marriott experience.
Staying in Marriott often due to work this was the worst i ever encountered. Asking for a standard water bottle to the rooms i was told to drink out of the sinkt as Marriott is not supplying water to guests.
Old, run down and with zero investments having been made over the last decades. Cleaning the property besidenthe rooms seems to be completely optional.
service staff in the Restaurants is very friendly though.
Absolutely not worth paying the premium here for staying in a "top" Hotel.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: Everyone was so nice! The building is beautiful and there are fantastic food options on site.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: We only stayed one night prior to catching a return flight, the grounds were beautiful but elements of the service were poor.  We were allocated a lower standard room than we’d paid for (but weren't told) and when we realised and complained, were offered a complimentary breakfast however, the system hadn’t been updated and the restaurant staff asked for payment. After another complaint this was removed but on the final bill, breakfast had been charged and had to be removed again. More annoyingly, 3 days after leaving the hotel two charges (£445 and £453) were added to my credit card with no communication from the hotel. I complained immediately by email but have received no response or apology. Three days later only one of the charges has been removed.

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: Simply a fantastic experience and stay at this hotel. What really makes a hotel is the staff and all the staff here are truly amazing with shoutouts to Mario and Bryan (they provide complimentary hotel and coffee tours in the hotels for guests). The executive lounge was amazing with just the right amount of quality food for breakfast and dinner (and snacks throughout the day). The grounds are extremely well kept and the pool and jacuzzi was fantastic.  A super unique feature of the hotel is that they grow their own vegetables in the back to serve guests!  Really looking forward to coming back in the future!

name: Costa Rica Marriott Hotel Hacienda Belen
review_text: The Hotel staff does not care about anyone who stays at this hotel. I had 2 clients stay just 1 night while waiting for their early flight the next day. Once they checked in there was a disturbance in the corridor. My client called the front desk to see if they could resolve the issue. 30 mins went by and nothing was done. The client then called a 2nd time to complain about the screaming taking place in the corridor. Another 30 mins went by and still nothing happened. the client called a 3rd time, this time being a little more insistent that they do something. Another 30 mins went by, 60 mins, 90 mins, and the scream still continued. The client didn't get any sleep. They complained to the front desk when they checked out. The front desk agent didn't say anything. I reached out to the management and requested some form of rebate or refund for their experience. Their words exactly... "We regret what occurred once again. We have taken note of your comments and will proceed internally as appropriate. Thank you for bringing this to our attention." Marriott has proven over and over that all they care about is money and the guest experience no longer maters. Book at your own risk... I would highly recommend a limited service option over this full-service option. You will pay a lot more for no service.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Grano de Oro is an amazing hotel in San Jose. Fabulously located within easy walking distance to museums and markets, and with a great restaurant. I would have loved to learn more about its history, the hotel once was a private residence and the rooms are part of a labyrinth! Super comfortable beds, amazing service, and quiet.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: As a long-time  domestic and international traveler, , staying for three nights at Hotel Grano de Oro was a simply fabulous experience. The hotel has luxurious old world charm. Our  room had a private garden with incredibly lush plants and the dining room and courtyard were absolutely beautiful combining  elegance and European style with contemporary appointments . The entire staff including Carlos the dining room manager, were extremely attentive and engaging. If you’re in San Jose, this would be my top choice to places to stay. We definitely will be returning. |David Hagerty and Louise Cadigan|April 18 2026

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: És un hotel on només vaig per fer-hi una nit, i repeteixo cada any perquè m'agrada molt. Servei atent i amable.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: On our tour to Costa Rica we spent our first and last nights in San Jose at the Hotel Grano de Oro.  It was an excellent choice.  Instead of the sterile could-be-anywhere typical downtown hotel we were treated to a small boutique hotel built in and around a historic 19th century mansion.  Super friendly, helpful staff, a delightful in-house restaurant and lovely rooms rounded out a great experience.  If you are traveling on your own stay here.  If you are booking a private tour ask if they can lodge you at the Hotel Grano de Oro.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
We stayed for one night at the end of our two week holiday to Costa Rica.  It was a lovely comfortable hotel.  The staff who welcomed us were very kind, informative and didn't try to bombard us with too much information.

The bar and restaurant were good but the service felt a bit aloof.

Our room was very nice, the hotel had upgraded us as it was our anniversary. But the only issue was the slight step up to the bathroom which I missed as we were going to bed and unfortunately fell and ended up breaking a rib.

The location was good making an easy walk to downtown San Jose and the museums.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: This hotel exceeded my expectations! I decided to stay in San Jose one night before heading out on a little bit of a road trip. The hotel is absolutely beautiful and it was built in the early 1900s and I believe it was a house.    The room was kind of small, but it looked like it was redone and very suitable for what I need it. The hotel has so many different sections that take you inside and then outside. The restaurant was spectacular and probably one of the most favorite restaurants I’ve ever eaten at. The service is kind of old-fashioned in a good way and the food was absolutely delicious. The restaurant is mostly outdoors, but there are inside sections. The area around the hotel seemed fairly safe as I walked a lot. It seems like it’s may be a working class perhaps middle class area in parts??  I highly recommend this hotel to anyone

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: The Hotel Grano de Oro is an historic home converted into a hotel. We found it a charming, clean place to stay while we were in San Jose.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Excelente servicio por parte de todo el personal! Un hotel con una arquitectura muy peculiar de casona antigua! El restaurant con excelente servicio! Todo el personal muy amable y atentos.

The Decor is Quality, the Ambience is Amazing, the Food in the Restaurant is Fantastic and the Staff as a credit to the Hotel & Costa Rica. Loved every minute of our time here to relax, after two weeks driving around Costa Rica, in luxury

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: The décor is tasteful. We stayed in their Standard Room as nothing else was available at the time of booking. The room was neat, but really small. Hardly enough room to open our travel bags.  It overlooked a portion of the roof of the neighbouring dwelling, and was easily visible from neighbouring windows unless the curtains were drawn. No bedroom slippers, no iron or ironing board. It did have a coffee-maker and a choice of coffees. The bathroom was roomy enough, clean and with necessary amenities. The shower cubicle, however, was extremely slippery, although they do provide an anti-slip shower mat. My husband had a bad fall while in the shower. Thankfully, he was only slightly bruised, and we were able to carry on with our travel plans. The restaurant had good ambience, and also offered good vegetarian food on the menu. The staff at the front desk was especially courteous and helpful. We stayed in Room 2. Perhaps, a different room may have offered a better experience.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Beautiful old style and very elegant hotel in San Jose.  The price is reasonable, service is great and the restaurant is superb.  We were only there a couple of nights and wished we had stayed longer.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Das Hotel Grano de Oro liegt in einer ruhigen Seitenstraße nahe dem Paseo Colón in San José. Wer mit dem Mietauto anreist, kann kostenlos auf einem eingezäunten Parkplatz nebenan parken. Bis ins Zentrum von San José läuft man ca. 15 Minuten. Seit 1991 ist das sorgfältig restaurierte viktorianische Herrenhaus aus dem frühen 20. Jahrhundert ein Hotel. "Grano de Oro" bedeutet "Goldkorn", eine Reminiszenz an die Kaffeebohne, die früher das wirtschaftliche Rückgrat Costa Ricas bildete. Heute verbindet sich hier historischer Charme mit dem Luxus eines modernen Boutique-Hotels.
Die 40 Zimmer und Suiten sind mit dunklen Hölzern im tropisch-viktorianischen Stil eingerichtet und sehr unterschiedlich, kein Zimmer gleicht dem anderen. Unser Zimmer hat ein bequemes Bett, ein sehr geräumiges Bad und einen romantischen Patio mit viel Grün und einem plätschernden kleinen Brunnen. Auch in den Gemeinschaftsbereichen des Hotels stoßen wir immer wieder auf lauschige Innenhöfe mit tropischen Pflanzen. Es gibt eine begrünte Dachterrasse mit einem schönen Ausblick auf San José und die umliegenden Berge. Dazu noch Whirlpools und einem Fitnessbereich.
Das Hotelrestaurant gilt als eine der besten Adressen der Hauptstadt. Serviert wird französische mediterrane Küche mit tropischem Einfluss. Man sitzt entweder im Freien im eleganten Innenhof, oder innen im stilvollen Ambiente. Sowohl unser Abendessen als auch das À-la-carte-Frühstück waren ausgezeichnet.

Das hat uns gut gefallen:
- Ausgezeichnete und ruhige Lage, man kann bequem ins Zentrum von San José laufen, und zum internationalen Flughafen sind es nur ca. 20 km zu fahren.
- Schönes Ambiente.
- Das Essen war ausgezeichnet.

Das hat uns weniger gut gefallen:
- Unser Patio war zwar sehr romantisch, aber da das das einzige Fenster war, wirkte das Zimmer etwas dunkel.

Unser Fazit:
Ein komfortabler Start- oder Endpunkt für eine Costa-Rica-Reise.

Ein ausführliches Video zu dem Hotel findet ihr in unserem Kanal auf Youtube @travellingpigs

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: We stayed here for one night. The service and the rooms were excellent. The hotel was very clean and well-maintained. Dinner was excellent, and the atmosphere was authentic and elegant. The only downside was that although breakfast was included, we were charged extra when we ordered additional bread or croissants. If breakfast is included, everything should be included.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: A delightful old house in a residential area that is ideal for a night stopover after the flight and before heading to explore! Breakfast in the courtyard was outstandingly good.  Highly recommend

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Interesting colonial building, well situated for walking into the city centre. Delicious breakfast. Comfortable room and bed but our room did not have an outside window. We did not discover the sun terrace and jacuzzi tub until it was too late to use them.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Sehr stilvolles Hotel mit gutem Restaurant

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Nous avons séjourné 2 nuits au Grano de Oro, en janvier 2026
L;hôtel est vraiment magnifique et bien situé pour aller, à pied, visiter San Jose.
Cependant, nous avons trouvé que le service n'était pas à la hauteur de la qualité de l'établissement (Beaucoup d'attente au petit déjeuner, service au dîner, professionnel, mais peu chaleureux.)
Un point à revoir
Dommage.
Il manquait peu de choses pour que tout soit parfait.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Abseits vom Trubel der Stadt, durften wir nach unserer Rundreise, 1 Nacht im Hotel verbringen und wow, wir haben uns wohlgefühlt. Hotel ist von Aussen gut anzuschauen, alles gepflegt, vor der Tür gibt es 4-5 Parkplätze sogar mit E-Ladesäule. Alles ist gepflegt, Bäume, Sträucher, Blumen. Es gibt einen Lift am Eingang für Personen mit Einschränkungen. Rezeption offen gestaltet mit schönen Bildern, viel Licht und vorallem freundlichen Mitarbeitern ♡ auch wenn man kein spanisch oder englisch kann, wird einem geholfen. Innen im Hotel schöne Holz Möbel im Kolonialstil gepaart mit modernen Farben. Unser Zimmer im EG im Innenhof Garten, mit Blick ins Grüne. Was will man Mehr. Betten bequem und nicht zu weich. Bad modern und sauber. Frühstück per Karte, sehr lecker. 😋 Wir haben uns rundum wohl gefühlt. Kaffee und Saft zum Frühstück hat schmeckt. Es gibt eine Dachterrasse mit Whirlpool und ein kleines Fitness Center.
Unsere Koffer konnten wir bis zur Abholung zum Flughafen, im Hotel lassen.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Hotel Grano de Oro is a very nice hotel, where we had lovely room. The service is excellent, friendly and efficient. The restaurant offers very nice breakfast and à la carte choices. Even though the hotel isn’t very far from the center of the city it is very quiet.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Incredible hotel with well maintained classic Victorian style interiors, beautifully decorated. The small courtyard is charming with beautiful plants to complement the gorgeous architecture. The room was lovely with all of the necessary comforts and most importantly clean. The service from every single employee was great and they made us feel cared for. The restaurant meals were delectable, I also enjoyed one of their signature martinis. Walking around the Hotel in itself is a wonderful experience, beautifully decorated and is located in the center of San Jose. There is a local park about 6 blocks away that hosts local free events with live music that brings the community together. If you are a bird watcher this park was great for watching the wild conures and various dove species in the trees. Will definitely come back here!

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Mala relación calidad precio|El edificio principal es bonito, el servicio en general es bueno y la comida muy rica|Dicho esto, la visibilidad del número de las habitaciones en el edificio nuevo es malísima por lo que tres personas diferentes en 9 horas se confundieron e intentaron acceder a nuestra habitación |Lo que peor me pareció fue la reacción del director al comentárselo ya que no puedo entender que sabiendo que tiene ese problema no lo haya solucionado (me parecería incluso peor que desconozca ese problema ya que espero de un director de un hotel de 5 estrellas el que conozca bien su propiedad)

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Hotel Grano de Oro is a very charming place and an excellent starting point for a Costa Rica round trip. It’s not an anonymous chain hotel, but a place with real character. The courtyard in particular (with its restaurant) conveys a true Central American ambi-ance. The dinner menu is diverse and locally inspired. Breakfast is outstanding, gener-ous, and easily keeps you satisfied well into the afternoon.|Our room was a bit dark (both sides facing the courtyard, with large plants in front of the windows). But in the end we weren’t staying at Grano de Oro to spend time in the room.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Edificio principal es bonito y tanto la comida como el servicio son buenos
Dicho eso, os recominendo evitar a toda costa las habitaciones del edificio nuevo ya que no tienen bien resuelta la visibilidad del número de las habitaciones y tuve al menos tres personas en 12 horas que se confundían e intentaban entrar en nuestra habitación
Asimismo me sorprendió la reacción del Director (Marco) que no era consciente de ese problema. Acaso no tiene un director de hotel que conocer los puntos de mejora de su propiedad (no puedo creer que esa noche y habitación concentraran tan mala suerte)?

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Stunning historic hotel and service perfect. Great espresso drinks and very nice menu with lots of choices. Wine selection very good. One of the best.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Hotel Grano de Oro feels like a refined colonial house where the inner courtyard and rooftop terrace provide a peaceful setting that contrasts nicely with the lively city outside.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Very nice hotel in San Jose.  Just stayed overnight but really enjoyed it.  Restaurant had great food.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Me encanta la comida

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Très bel hôtel bien situé à 10 minutes à pied du centre ville. Toutefois, le service ne nous a pas paru à la hauteur de la qualité de l'établissement. Service du petit déjeuner très long, personnel au dîner, courtois, mais pas chaleureux. Des progrès restent à faire.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: El mesero Alberto tiene pésimo servicio al cliente. Llegué a las 5 p.m. y sólo quería un postre y un café. Alberto nos recibió de mala manera, apenas nos dirigió la palabra. Le dije que quería un brownie y se rió de forma burlona diciendo que ahí no venden brownies. Después se fué sin decir nada y cuando volvió nos dijo que no estaban atendiendo a, aún cuando solamente había una mesa ocupada. Yo trabajo en turismo de lujo y nunca me habían recibido tan mal.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Stayed here for 3 nights after landing in San Jose from London and before heading off around Costa Rica. It is an oasis in an otherwise busy city. The restaurant is lovely and both breakfast and dinners were great. The location is very good. |I had a massage which was fab after a 13 hour flight. |Hotel decor is wonderful

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: El desayuno horrible, los panqueques son de mezcla comercial, el café aguado. El bowl de frutas diminuto. Desayunamos 2 personas. La otra pidió huevo con bacon (nada especial) venía con unas tostadas de pan cuadrado de mala calidad. Pagué 60 dólares. Mejor vayan a desayunar a otro lugar.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: What a great place to stay. Rooms are large and have all the usual amenities plus free snacks in the minibar. The restaurant is excellent and the entire staff can't do enough to help. From the front desk personal to housekeeping they couldn't do enough to help. They have 2 hot tubs and a exercise room. I would gladly stay here again.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Beautiful old colonial building with stunning furnishings and grounds (would have been nice to have a couple of benches to sit on in the courtyard gardens). Very good breakfast, though we did not eat dinner here. Pleasant roof terrace and jacuzzi (if you like that sort of thing) alongside a small gym. The location is quiet - close to Sandana Park, with a restaurant just opposite. We ate in the city - downtown is approx ten mins by Uber or 45 minutes to walk. |Efficient check in and desk service, they very kindly looked after our suitcase for a couple of days. These are the things you remember: when a hotel goes out of its way to help you. Also, with an early check out, they gave us breakfast in doggy bags to eat at the airport. Many thanks.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Ambiance colonialiste nous trouvons cependant le lieux très charmant.
Parfait pour une nuit à San Jose. Équipe serviable et j’ai adoré. Le petit déjeuner dans la cour était un accueil spécial au Costa Rica

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
If I could give this place 1000 stars, I would. Our stay here has been nothing but incredible. The staff, the room, the food, everything has been amazing. We have felt very welcomed here, if you are wondering where to stay in San José, this is the place! The bed is so comfortable, the pillows are next level cozy, and my goodness, the shower pressure is top notch.

I really want to thank Kenneth for being the absolute best, your cocktails were delicious, your hospitality is amazing. Everyone has been so kind to us, I really can’t thank them enough.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: I’ve only been in the restaurant in two different situations and the food it’s amazing. The service is super nice and they are very much into details.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: I've not stayed at the hotel however I have been to the restaurant on numerous occasions. Food is always stellar and the servers are always nice. It's a beautiful place and if I were visiting, I'd definitely want to stay here. Great location close to downtown San Jose.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Sehr stilvolles, gepflegtes und freundliches Hotel im Tico Stil. Sehr erfreulicher Kontrast zu den amerikanischen Einheitshotels ...

Sehr schöne Zimmer - aber leider nicht alle mit einem Fenster nach draußen.

Hervorragendes Restaurant.

Eher auf der teuren Seite - für meine Begriffe tatsächlich zu teuer.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: El ambiente, aunque está en pleno San José es muy tranquilo, la comida definitivamente deliciosa, los postres... los postres hubo que repetir. Excelente servicio

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Very nice, small hotel with everything you need. Kind service. The breakfast was included and served in the hotels restaurant which offers french kitchen. It was the day of our arrival and we wanted to try some local traditional food. The recommendation 'Nuestra Tierra' by the attentive receptionist was sadly a tourist trap. Next time we'd   have dinner in the restaurant because breakfast on the next morning was delicious. They have almond milk as milk alternative. We could choose a fresh juice among a huge variety which was great. They offer a huge variety of brealfast options too. Unfortunatelly they said that the crep for breakfast is very small and we should better take something else for breakfast. I wonder why not all offered breakfast options are around the same size?

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Splendid private Victorian home converted into a hotel. Impeccable attention to detail, from the common areas decor, with botanical garden level of plant design, to the furniture and wall decor. We are here on a Christmas vacation, and on every door hangs a beautiful stocking, and the Christmas decorations throughout the hotel are beautiful and in style with building. Impeccably clean rooms and bathrooms, and the level of maintenance is very high. Fridge is stocked with free refreshments, Nespresso-type coffee maker (the Britt coffee is the BOMB!), laundry service, 24-hour reception, free parking. Upstairs you will find a lovely spa area with hot tubs, chaise longues and fitness equipment. A special mention deserve the beds: I normally find fault with most hotel beds due to an achy back, but have been waking up rested and pain-free... Overall, a level of warmth, elegance, comfort and amenities hard to find in Costa Rica.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Lovely hotel with great dining space both indoor & outdoor.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Service beim Frühstück miserabel, warteten eine Viertelstunde auf 2 Stück Toast. Preise zu teuer für was man erhält, nämlich kleine Speisen. Für junge, hungrige Leute enttäuschend.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: This review is for the restaurant. Fantastic food in a very cozy atmosphere. Great wine selection and the deserts are amazing! Highly recommended.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Exelente hotel para descansar, por un viaje de negocios o de paseo, total mente recomendados por su servicio personal instalaciones. Recomendados.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: This hotel went above and beyond during our stay. The grounds are beautiful and the restaurant was delicious. Marco and the staff at the front desk treated us like family in a time we needed it most. We will be forever thankful! If we are back in the area the choice of hotel will be an easy decision. Thank you!!

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Restaurante 🤌

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Hotel in stile classico coloniale con grande charme ( per chi sa apprezzare queste cose). |Ogni dettaglio è stato curato nel minimo dettaglio. |Abbiamo molto apprezzato il rooftop con 2 jacuzzi. |Il ristorante è molto elegante e il cibo è ottimo. |Veramente un grand hotel!

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: This hotel is such a treat! It is beyond what you would expect from a boutique hotel. From the moment you check in, you're greeted with special care and treated like royalty. The grounds are heavenly kept. The restaurant is next level and beautiful! The front desk will assist you with whatever you need and then some...umbrellas, towels, taxi, restaurant reservations, etc. It's located in the city so about 3-6 hours from the experiences we traveled to Costa Rica for (Beaches, volcanos, waterfalls, wild life). Roads are easy for travel so we're fine.  Don't attempt any short cuts...take highways 27 or 34...no less traveled roads!!! Consider yourselves warned.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Es mi visita por primera vez, me encuentro en lugar acogedor, un personal de recepción muy educada y servicial. Las habitaciones, los pasillos, área de restaurante muy limpio. Y acogedor.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Schlimmes Personal und minderwertige Speisen in tollem Ambiente.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Wird in keinem Punkt dem aufgerufenen Preis gerecht!
Wir sind viel, vorallem in eher luxuriösen Häusern unterwegs. Leider rangiert dieses Hotel ganz weit hinten, was eine Weiterempfehlung bedeuten könnte.
Los ging es mit dem 300$- Zimmer, das eher einer Kammer ähnelte. Dunkel, mit Fenster zum Gang, auf dem sich dann nachts lautstark unterhalten wurde.
Steckdosen, zum aufladen elektrischer Geräte Mangelware. Im Bad keine Lüftung, stattdessen ein Klappfenster zum bereits genannten Gang.
Das Frühstück konnte die hohen Erwartungen ebenfalls nicht erfüllen. Gleich zu Beginn, eine lange Erklärung des Kellners, was alles nicht inkludiert sei. Beim Kontinentalen Frühstück wurden dann noch Butter und Honig vergessen und erst auf Nachfrage gebracht. Auch Salz und Pfeffer scheinen Mangelware zu sein, finden sich auf keinem Tisch und müssen erbeten werden. Im servierten Obst, waren die Melonenstücke bereits in Auflösung begriffen und nicht mehr essbar.
Der Kaffee könnte ein paar Bohnen mehr vertragen. Man ist in Costa Rica!  - wo, wenn nicht hier, sollte man einen guten Kaffee bekommen?!
Alles abgerundet von Personal, welches einen recht arroganten Eindruck hinterlässt.
Die Lage des Hotels ist im Übrigen auch nicht gerade vorteilhaft.
Insgesamt ein Hotel, dass wir auf keinen Fall empfehlen können.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Das Grano de Oro ist ein sehr schönes Hotel im Kolonialstil, mit modernem Eingangsbereich. Die grosszügigen Zimmer sind sauber, mit Safe, TV, AC ausgestattet. Das wunderbare Frühstück im Patio mit aufmerksamen Service ist aussergewöhnlich.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Had high hopes here on a one night stopover before flying.
It’s historic, but beyond reception that also translates to dated.
The reception / front desk staff were very young and not all that experienced.
Restaurant was a lovely setting, but the food wasn’t great. Dinner was totally underwhelming and over priced. Breakfast was a fiasco. Despite being first in and only one other person it took nearly half an hour to get anything, and they was what they offered us as what they could get us quickly as we had a flight to catch. We ended up rushing rating in about 5 minutes so we didn’t miss our flight. And we’re talking juice, fruit, yoghurt - that sort of thing. There was never a smile in the restaurant.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Nous avons passé deux nuits à une semaine d’intervalle dans ce très bel hôtel à l’occasion d’un voyage pour notre anniversaire de mariage au Costa Rica.|La première nuit, nous avons été très bien accueillis et surclassés dans une très belle chambre pour notre anniversaire. La seconde nuit, plus d’attention. Dommage…|L’hôtel est situé dans une ancienne batisse au charme certain avec plein de recoins de nature.|Beau restaurant avec une très bonne cuisine mais service très guindé et peu sympathique.|Jacuzzis sur le toit mais ils sont d’un autre âge et ne fonctionnent plus très bien.|Hôtel proche du centre ville de San José.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Nice

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Had a great one night stay. All front desk staff were thorough and very accommodating. Beautiful grounds with open concept and beautiful gardens. Close to restaurants and other places you can easily walk to. Forgot a medication at home and one of the front desk staff called the pharmacy for me to translate. We left early in the morning and they made us a to-go breakfast box. Highly recommended!

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: What an incredible welcome to Costa Rica! We adored this hotel and couldn’t have chosen a better spot for our one night in San Jose. The food was out of this world and the service was impeccable. Such a charming and timeless spot, thank you for having us ❤️

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Come here for the eggs benedict. I removed a star as they decided to serve what i think was jamon from localy packaged cinta azul instead of the typical offer of jamón serrano. Were you out??? Check photo. Removed another star as attention to patrons was lacking. They have done better in the past and could improve. I hope they improves on my next visit.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Hotel Grano de Oro is truly a special place! It's an updated first class Victorian hotel in the Downtown San Jose that is an amazing hotel unlike any other chain or boutique hotel in the city.

It's an incredible throwback that is updated and decorated where every single detail was taken into account to create something beyond memorable. From the flowers decorating the hotel, better than an arboretum, to the old school photos of the city, it's easy to get lost in the hotel and its restored glory from an old mansion.

We stayed for a one night staycation in the city for an anniversary and it went so far beyond expectations from even before setting foot in the hotel. The staff was incredible working to arrange everything from a flower petal laden table for a world class dinner, to an amazing room with a private flower sitting area, to a Spa appointment, and even everything to enjoy on site like roof top temperature controlled jacuzzis- the Grano de Oro surpassed out wildest expectations and its impossible to say enough good things.

To anyone out there considering a hotel in San Jose, please spend a night here and make sure to eat in the restaurant for dinner and breakfast plus give the Spa a go as well. It's a throwback to when incredible detail and service sets hotels apart with the Grano de Oro first in its class.

PS. While in the restaurant, make sure to try the pistachio crusted steak and for dessert the namesake Grano de Oro pie as they are worth the trip in themselves.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Gran hotel, el servicio de recepcionistas puede mejorar. Buenas habitaciones y baños
Estupendos desayunos en un patio muy bonito. Mi suegro pidió un omelette con verduras a parte, 20dolares... Excesivo.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Nice luxurious place. Stayed with family of four in one room. Was prefect size. Breakfast in the morning is a al carte and somewhat slow. But food is good and service great. Slightly outside heart of the center, so either take an uber or walk for 20mins

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text:
Unser Aufenthalt im Hotel Grano de Oro in Costa Rica war rundum perfekt. Das im charmanten Kolonialstil gehaltene Haus ist mit viel Liebe zum Detail hergerichtet und strahlt eine warme, einladende Atmosphäre aus. Besonders schön sind die zahlreichen tropischen Pflanzen, die dem Hotel einen kleinen, grünen Paradiescharakter verleihen.

Die Zimmer sind komfortabel ausgestattet, die Betten unglaublich bequem – wir haben herrlich geschlafen. Besonders aufmerksam fanden wir die Bereitstellung von kostenlosem Wasser im Zimmer. Auch die eigenen Parkplätze sind ein großer Pluspunkt, gerade wenn man mit dem Mietwagen unterwegs ist.

Das hoteleigene Restaurant ist absolut empfehlenswert: exzellente Küche, die sowohl das Auge als auch den Gaumen verwöhnt.

Ein großes Dankeschön gilt dem überaus freundlichen und hilfsbereiten Personal, das unseren Aufenthalt noch angenehmer gemacht hat. Hier spürt man echte Gastfreundschaft.

Alles in allem bietet das Hotel Grano de Oro wirklich alles, was man sich für einen erholsamen Aufenthalt wünscht. Wir bedanken uns für die wunderbare Zeit und empfehlen dieses Hotel uneingeschränkt weiter!

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Am Ende der Rundreise haben wir für 1 Nacht hier eingecheckt. Unbeschreibliches Ambiente und tolles Frühstück. Ein toller Ort zum verweilen.

name: Hotel Grano De Oro
review_text: Heel mooi hotel met goede kamers en goed eten in San Jose waar verder niet veel te beleven is. Voor 1 nacht zeker prima

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
La experiencia en general estuvo bien y el hotel tiene varios puntos positivos que disfrutamos. Las piscinas son muy bonitas, amplias y agradables para pasar el día. Uno de los aspectos que más nos gustó fue la comida. El buffet tiene bastante variedad y constantemente están reponiendo la comida, así que casi siempre se encuentra todo fresco. Había opciones diferentes tanto para desayuno como para almuerzo y cena, incluyendo frutas, ensaladas, carnes, pastas, postres y comida típica. Además, nos pareció positivo que hubiera varias estaciones y opciones para distintos gustos.

El restaurante japonés Furama fue una de las mejores partes de la experiencia. La comida estaba muy rica, bien presentada y el ambiente era agradable.

Sin embargo, hubo varios detalles que nos dejaron un sinsabor. El servicio al cliente es bastante inconsistente: algunos meseros fueron muy amables, atentos y serviciales, pero otros prácticamente nos ignoraban. En el American Country, por ejemplo, tuvimos que esperar bastante para que alguien nos atendiera porque los meseros evitaban hacer contacto visual. Después de un buen rato fue que enviaron a otra persona a atendernos. Algo similar pasó en algunos bares.

También tuvimos problemas con la habitación. El aire acondicionado no funcionaba y, después de tres llamadas a mantenimiento, nos ofrecieron cambiarnos de habitación cuando ya faltaba solo una noche para irnos. El jacuzzi tampoco tenía agua caliente y el sauna de hombres estuvo fuera de servicio toda la estadía, por lo que mi cuñado no pudo usarlo.

Otro punto incómodo es el sistema de electricidad de la habitación, donde hay que dejar la llave conectada para que funcione la corriente. Eso impide dejar cargando celulares, tablets, audífonos o mantener el aire acondicionado encendido mientras uno sale. Cada vez que regresábamos, la habitación estaba extremadamente caliente y había que esperar bastante a que se enfriara. Además, no hay enchufes en la terraza, así que si alguien quiere trabajar ahí con la computadora, debe asegurarse de cargarla antes. La pantalla tampoco se puede conectar a Netflix ni emparejar desde el celular. El Wifi es de lo peor, hay que estar iniciando sesión una y otra vez, y a cada rato se cae.

En las áreas comunes también notamos problemas de limpieza. Varias sillas alrededor de la piscina estaban sucias, incluso con restos de comida, y los baños comunes olían mal y no se veían limpios.

Por último, al salir hacia la playa todas las sillas estaban apiladas y cada huésped debía cargar y acomodar la suya por cuenta propia. El servicio de toallas también fue bastante inconsistente. Según el personal, solo permiten cambiarlas una vez al día porque “se les acaban”, así que si uno usa la toalla en la playa durante la mañana y queda completamente mojada, en la tarde ponen mala cara si se solicita una nueva.

En resumen, el hotel tiene cosas muy buenas, especialmente las instalaciones y la comida, pero definitivamente hay aspectos de servicio, limpieza y mantenimiento que deberían mejorar para que la experiencia realmente esté al nivel esperado.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Tenía 4 años de no ir y debo decir que en infraestructura, esta muy bonito. Sin embargo,antes los meseros te tomaban la orden en la piscina y llevaban las bebidas ahí, eso ya no sucede. El mini bar lo llenan cada 2 días y no ponen bebidas para niños (y bien que cobran la estancia de ellos). El parque acuático cierra un día a la semana y tiene horario de apertura a las 10 am. Me parece que se paga mucho dinero para esas restricciones.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Best trip, everything was clean and people are sooo nice!

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: I had a wonderful time. The staff was great. The food was great. My room was amazing. I loved every minute of it.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: I ENJOYED myself there. The resort is Beautiful! We do plan on returning. Thank you so much for the service and accommodations. Your staff is polite/patience/friendly and professional.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Rooms were spacious, but they need more hanging areas/desk or table. They felt moist often, but the 6xxx building was just too far from all amenities and restaurants. About 4 elevators per building. Only offered drinks every other day. Food is more commercial, but will say the specialty restaurants were great! Standard buffet with same daily. Didn't offer a ton to do at the resort. Pool options were plentiful. Beach was nice but you walk thru a pocket of vendors. Not potable water every where but plenty of bottle and bar options to drink. They offer a taxi service to Coco Beach, as the Riu Guanacaste & Palace are secluded in along the dry coastline. Wake up early and walk left along the beach to see wild monkeys! No sloths in this area but there is a local 'zoo' with shuttle about 4 mins away. A local restaurant: Monkey House and bar: Monkey Bar are within a 5-10min walk off property/beach line area. Service, food, and drinks were good, location was eehh, but glad we ventured into the local city to roam and went to a supermarket. 630a weed eaters and trimmers were not great!

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Clean comfy rooms.  A good selection of restaurants with good food.  Loved the crafts, exercise classes and the shows each nights.  Nice beach.  Very attentive staff.  Several pools with splash park.  Poolside bars had good drinks. Great pool dancing, water volleyball and beach volleyball.  Great family vacation!

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Loved our stay. We were there May 28-June 2nd. Wonderful experience. No complaints. Great food, good alcohol, and friendly staff. Only downside was the towels. They smelt moldy We requested a new set and they still had that smell. Overall, I would stay here again (I've been twice :)

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Muy mal servicio en la telefonia. He llamado 5 veces nadie contesta

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Riu Guanacaste was a BLAST! They had tasty food, a diverse amount of activities, and night shows.I enjoyed the splash waterworld and beach a lot to. The lobby was set beautifully, and all the employees were kind. There are also many night shows where dancers do skits, and cultural dances. Overall it was a very fun experience.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: La remodelación quedó muy bien, las instalaciones muy limpias. La comida en su mayoria estaba bien. A la hora de la salida solo dan la opción de un restaurante y muy poca comida, ni siquiera tenian pizza, papas o helados. Se siente como si ya por el hecho de salir ya no merece uno algo bueno. Deberian mejorar esa parte.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Love Costa Rica and RIU, hands down a must see and weekend getaway fully equipped resort, buffet crusie like meals are fantastic, great stay, can't wait to return

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Great location: only 45 minutes from Liberia airport. Just know this a mega resort and the customer service quality goes down. Great size room. I enjoyed the room. Just keep in mind, all the restaurants except the steakhouse are buffet style. I have traveled to a lot of buffet only resorts and all of them at least offer an extra ala carte room service  option or can deliver the buffet items to your room for extra fee if they are any dietary or medical needs. They don’t even have an optional paid  room service and the staff makes a very big deal if you carry a few snacks for medical needs. I don’t mind paying extra for food and room service if my kids get hungry in between meal times or if someone in the room is sick and can’t walk over to the restaurants. This was a really huge downside and the lack of training and education from the staff really disappointed me! People have medical needs and the staff is poorly trained on how to handle this. We also had someone that had gastric bypass on the trip years ago and they can’t eat large amounts of food at once, and had to constantly take a small plate of food from the buffet restaurants to have for a few hours later so their blood sugar doesn’t fall too low; if she doesn’t do this her blood sugar runs too low and she will faint, especially with the heat. The staff treated her like a criminal and like she was stealing food and several levels of management had to get involved and she had to explain each time her medical condition and they were complications everytime at each meal . When we asked to speak to the hotel manager about this, we were told he was too busy and wouldn’t bother to come and speak to us for such a small problem.
Also they don’t seem to be prepared for emergencies, my daughter broke her nail and needed a larger band aid. Most hotels have a trained medic and several first aid kits, when i asked the front desk they said they said they didn’t have any dressings or bandage in their first aid kit and to visit the quick store. We walked to the quick store in the heat and it was closed along with the urgent care next door; it was too early. I returned and to literally begged them to call the medic , or phone the urgent care of the emergency for me because i couldn’t call internationally it was a local costa rican; they refused to bother .They finally found a small bandage in the first aid kit
after giving me the run around for an hour!The customer service is soo horrible if you have any medical emergencies or food dietary needs. I have never had this issue at any resort, and i have traveled to many countries in latin america, africa, and europe and hotels and resorts are prepared and trained to handle these small situations except this one !
Everything else was great, I am not a picky and over the top person. Food was great and decent. Activities were awesome and plenty. Pool was nice and relaxing. And at least half, of the other staff went out of their way and were extremely nice making up for the bad customer service. I liked everything but customer service is poor and lacking. Employees need better training to handle special situations. In their defense some employees seemed like they were overworked and burned out, which strongly correlates to bad management. These are common problems and every resort i stayed at is equipped and knows how to handle these situations. How is a large resort like Riu not able to have an adequate first kit? And not be able to address dietary and medical  needs. The resort was cheap and inexpensive in pricing, so if cost is a problem, offer these services at an extra cost to guests. I wouldn’t mind paying extra fee  for any additional food and  room service delivery fee to accommodate any extra dietary and medical for my friends and family . This was not even offered to us.  The medical needs with my daughter was frustrating and not being able to access a medic or first aid. I will not be booking at Riu

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Nos encantó el show nocturno del viernes White party otro nivel, ha mejorado mucho todo, nada más que el restaurante asiático debería ser con reservacion, no estar haciendo una fila enorme desde temprano..

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Es un linda experiencia, la pasa uno muy bien, agunos de los que atienden al público en los bares, principalmente, tal vez, por ser uno, nacional, no son amables, son toscos y el servicio lo hacen de poca gana, pero, a los extranjeros los atienden muy bien.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Si tienen chance de pagar un poquito más, vaya al RIU Palace, es un hotel también familiar... menos gente, mejor servicio, cerveza en lata nacional, room service y se puede pasar al RIU Guanacaste. Algo que debe mejorar Riu Guanacaste es en aumentar la cantidad de personal en los bares, se nota que hay uno sirviendo, el otro acomodando y la multitud esperando; por esto las malas caras y el maltrato al personal. Tanto en Riu Guanacaste como el Riu Palace se puede usar el parque acuático.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Me gustaria un hotel riu en puntarenas o jaco.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: El hotel, el servicio, la comida...
