Tienen un 10 de 10 pero tuvimos un par de inconvenientes primero el agua del lavatorio de la habitación  cada vez que se usaba olía muy mal como que reciclan el agua de alguna manera y obviamente le advierten a uno que no la puede consumir pero el olor es lo más molesto, adicional a eso para ser un hotel tan grande no tener cargadores eléctricos para vehículos deja mucho que desear ya que cada vez más se están usando este tipo de vehículos en el país, y para los que viajamos desde muy lejos es muy incómodo andar buscando un cargador lejos del hotel.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: i loved it sooo much!! everything was so nice!

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Great experience at this resort in Costa Rica. Great choice for an all inclusive resort. Rooms are clean, staff is friendly, and had plenty of activities for guests.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Hotel hermoso, los baristas tienen un excelente Servicio al cliente, habitaciones lindas, aseadas.
El personal del restaurante de carnes, se veían muy cansados, exageradamente serios y se sentía que atendían por hacerlo.
Podría mejorar en los shows, el grupo musical tenía muy ma sonido, se oía como "guardada la música, como en un hueco ".

Me hizo falta la famosa fiesta de espuma que hacen en algunos hoteles, es divertido y entretiene bastante.

Tiene más variedad en comida que otros hoteles, sin embargo podrían variarla aún más.

Una desventaja es que el Internet se va constantemente, por más que pedimosa clave ahí, no teníamos señal en cualquier lado. Aún teniendo nuestro Internet, no entraba tan fácil.

El personal de limpieza y los qe entregaban bebidas son personas agradables y muy educadas. Los felicito

Quedé satisfecha 🙂

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Very Noisy hotel. Upon check-in, I wish front desk would explain amenities and light switch ( Have to use key to turn on lights). Huge hotel.
Can hear guest stumbling back to their rooms drunk in the wee hours of the morning. AC  on a timer-very noisy when chipping in. Spotty Wi- Fi.
A lot of  unsupervised kids running around.
Love the in- room wet bar and balcony. Transportation is easy to reserve in lobby for day trips.
Lot's  of food and alcohol. Swim up bar is nice.
However,  I've been to better beaches. All in all. I'd  rate this a 6.5 out of 10

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Me gusta mucho siempre lo visito de 1 o 2 veces al año y para mi siempre he recibido buen servicio. A mis hijos les encanta y es bastante familiar

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Excelente ahora con heladería

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: We are currently staying at the resort and so far our experience has not been the best we were given the wrong room to start with. We paid extra for a good view and king sized bed and were given two queens and bad view. We talked to front desk and was later resolved but the hardest thing about the resort is getting a decent meal. Most of the themed restaurants are over crowded and labeled as restaurants but are really just buffets. It’s really hard to sit down and have good service at any of these restaurants. Everything closes very early too sun doesn’t set until 7 yet slot of the different activities like the water world close at 5. I wish this stuff was mentioned when booking the resort and making the trip all the way out here. I would rate this a 1 star but The drinks at the bar are all really good

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
We had a fantastic stay at the Riu Guanacaste Resort in Costa Rica. The resort exceeded our expectations in every way. The pools were beautiful, clean, and well-maintained, with plenty of space to relax and enjoy the sunshine. The beach was absolutely stunning, with soft sand, gorgeous views, and warm water that made every day feel like paradise.

The food was excellent, with a wide variety of options available at every meal. Whether dining at the buffet or the specialty restaurants, everything was fresh, flavorful, and plentiful. The drinks were also great, and the all-inclusive experience made the vacation completely stress-free.

What really made our stay special was the friendly and hardworking staff. Everyone was welcoming, attentive, and always willing to help with a smile. The resort was clean, the entertainment was fun, and there were plenty of activities to enjoy throughout the day and evening.

Overall, Riu Guanacaste is a wonderful destination for anyone looking for a relaxing and enjoyable vacation in Costa Rica. We would definitely return and highly recommend it to family and friends.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Excelente atención y muy agradecidos con todos ustedes
Esperando ansioso volver al hotel

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Overall,  I would stay again. This resort is beautiful and newly renovated. I'll break this review down into sections. We were a group of 5 with 3 rooms. 2 rooms celebrating anniversaries and 1 a birthday.

Check in: Quick, not a long line. Was early and had to wait for room, but was expected. Told us about the app that you can request housekeeping,  restocking of minibar, maintenance, and reserve Steak House dinner. (This will come into play later)

Rooms: Beautiful! Newly renovated spacious. Only "complaint" water pressure was horrible. I have long thick hair and trying to wash out shampoo or conditioner was a lengthy process. Balcony is spacious and we got to watch monkeys from Diomante next door in the mornings. Really cool! We all had the basic rooms. Housekeeping cleaned them throughout the week and did a great job.

Pools: Beautiful pools! Basically it has 2 sections within the resort. The adult/kid pool located by the lobby is where we spent most of our time. Spacious and not as popular as the main area. Adult pool had the Guaro swim up bar and staff was fun and kept the drinks flowing... Main pool, was HUGE with smaller sections breaking off. Swim up bar connected to main pool was also crowded. Which is expected. The side single adult pool closer to the beach is more laid back. All the swim up bars had the same issue.... BEES! Here is the downside of these options, all the pop and juices for mixes and open cups had a lot of bees around. Many bees also floating in the pool. My husband got stung day 1 and thankfully is not allergic. We also, throughout the week, witness at least 3 other people get stung. Either by bees at the bar or in the water. I inquired about them using a net/skimmer to clean all the bees out of the pool or that I would do it, and they explained they did it at night. Not helpful at all.

Food: Steak House, you have to make reservations through the app. One thing that wasn't explained during check in was that to log into the app you have to enter last name, room #, and check out day. 2 of our rooms were registered with men who last name is followed by III and IV. We tried multiple times to find out on day 3 that they needed to put the III or IV as the last name not their actual last name. This delay caused them to not be able to get reservations as the place was booked up the entire week by the time they got in. 2 of our rooms got sick after eating in the Italian restaurant for approximately 24hrs. We mainly stuck to Liberia buffet for all meals or PePes grill. The buffet is very bland and none of the signage matched up with the food. So unless you knew by looks what it was,  it was a mystery dish. Overall,  food needs some work. Oh, and BACON and HASHBROWNS! How do you not have these for breakfast????

Entertainment: The theater had adult items but not usually until 10pm. This was late for us so we didnt get to to see much. This isn't a + or - against the place.

Scuba Caribe: Main reason we choose this resort was to scuba dive! Scuba Caribe did a great job. Each person did 6 dives which included 2 at Catalina Island. Our guides were Nicholas, Ricardo, Orlando, and Flavi. All great! We felt safe and they pointed out some great underwater sea life! We felt safe with the guides which is a huge + in my book as this is really important when putting your life in someone else's hands.

Overall, it was a nice stay. Had fun. Got stung. Food was bland. Rooms were clean.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Hotel was ok at best. Bathroom smelled like sewage.  The water in the shower would not go down. I spoke with the front desk and was told they switched my room when I went down the guy told me it wasn't in the system. Half hour lady it was there.  Not the best experience but not terrible

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: The hotel was great.  The staff was super friendly and kind.  The place was freshly remodeled.  My only complaint is that the food was not that great.  The buffet was the same thing every single day.  They had no bacon or hashbrowns.  The food was also very bland.  The steak house was really bad.  None of the food had flavor.  The ribs were really over cooked.  The shrimp was tasteless and over cooked.  The chicken was the best thing at the steak house.  We stayed last time at the RIU palace next door and the food was so much better there compared to this hotel.  Even though the food was not that good we would still go back as the beach is great.  The monkey bar on the beach was nice.  The scuba shop was full of very nice people was well.  The overall experience was great there.  My family loved the shows as well.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: We are checking out in two days and overall this RIU does not compare to other RIUs internationally. Firstly, it was a struggle to find a solid lunch and dinner option at this resort. The buffet for breakfast had staples that were done generally well and the poolside food is the only thing consistent and good for lunch. We relied on the app and the app mentioned that there were themed buffet dinners, but when we spoke to the reception, this was not the case. The front desk and managers were also unfazed when I was telling them how disappointed I was about the service. They didn’t seem to care that it took a long time to even get a table for dinner and then the food not being good after waiting over an hour for a table. The bathrooms also smelt very bad. It smelt like sewage. There was also an incident of mosquitos/spiders at my friends room, and there was pushback for them to get another room. The drinks and service at the pool and at the bars were good. I wish there were more spots to get towels on the resort and if there was closer drink options to the beach. I do not think I got my moneys worth.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: First time on a plane and I don't think I could've picked a better location. Enjoyed every single moment. RIU definitely TOP Notch Establishment

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: We had a great stay at RIU Guanacaste. The pools were fantastic, the food was delicious with plenty of options, and the beach was beautiful and relaxing. The staff were friendly, and the resort had a fun atmosphere. We would definitely stay here again and recommend it to anyone visiting Guanacaste.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Great experience and would highly recommend!

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: The hotel was pretty good they had plenty of activity’s and you don’t even need to have an excursion booked since the hotel helps you out the staff was sooo very nice specially the ones at the pool bar but they had a very big problem with the food the it was almost impossible to eat in certain restaurants as you had to be there since 6 and the lines were massive and the only one that was really worth eating in was the furama ( the photo shows how big lines get to eat

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Everything is great put the toilet smell like sewer water.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: La comida repetitiva,el día de salida no puede uno escoger el restaurante y la comida muy sencilla,hay que solicitar varias veces entre ir y llamar para que le den un paño,armen el sofá cama ,le ayuden con la caja fuerte ,no rellenan botellas de champú o acondicionador ( estaban menos de un octavo cuando ingresamos.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Para mi ha sido la mejor experiencia de mi vida. Todo fue increíble. Claro que volveré a ir

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Took my son for his 52 birthday.  We have a very nice time catching up with life-  Zip line, sunset cruise - Diamante Eco Adventure Park was beautiful -  Animals were taken such great care of.  Hotel was very clean -  Food was GREAT.   Can't wait to go back

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Instalaciones muy bonitas y limpias. Buen servicio. Variedad de comidas bebidas y postres. Muchas actividades de entretenimiento, playa cerca y personal con buena mística de trabajo y servicio. Lo que si note que el personal que uno se topa en el pasillo no dice Buenos días si uno no saluda y en su semblante da una percepción de no estar muy cómodo

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: We were really disappointed. We paid for an ocean view and our view was obstructed by a solid wall, most the other rooms had a wrought iron gate. Our coffee pot was busted, there were pieces of toilet paper on the bathroom floor, from the previous guests, our sheets were stained, when we arrived. The water from the toilet is brown and smells horrible. My husband and I both got sick, either from the food, water or both. We had to stay in our stinky room for a majority of our stay. Drinks are extremely watered down, everything besides the steakhouse is buffet style. There were flies on some of the food in the buffet. Kids were putting their hands on the quesadillas to pick through it. We reserved the hot tub and steam room, and when we arrived, they informed us the hot tub isn’t working. The steam room in the men’s area was also not working. We do not recommend.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Excelente Hotel.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: En el check in dure 1 hora y en la salida el restaurante muy poca selecion y no habia helados ya que lo mandan aotro restaurante que no es liberia.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
After we booked this and were already in Costa Rica, we read a review about this property summarized as “Spirit Airlines meets Carnival Cruise” and it was 10000000000% accurate.
Upon check in, our room still had some women’s long black hair everywhere all over the floor and shower. Thankfully we were able to communicate with the cleaning crew that they “missed a spot” and got it taken care of. The food is….food. Don’t get your hopes up.  You might even lose weight by the end of your stay. Wear your shoes so you don’t step on surprise trash at the beach. The landscape hillsides around the beach reminded me of the scene in Indiana Jones Temple of Doom Indian village before the children returned.
Overall, the property isn’t awful and the staff was nice. If I were in my 20’s and on a tight budget looking to drink tons of alcohol, this would be a fantastic option. I’ll spend the money next time.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Hace unos años nos hospedamos en el hotel y nos robaron en la habitación todo el dinero, el hotel no se hizo responsable de nada y dejaron de respondernos los emails. La habitación estaba cerrada con llave como la dejamos y sucedió mientras fuimos a desayunar. La ventana hacia el patio también estaba trabada por lo que no pudieron entrar por ahí. Un desastre.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: This was such a great location. The food, rooms, service, entertainment. Amazing

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: 1000 % Recomendado el mejor Hotel Todo Incluido

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Excelente opción para vivir unas vacaciones en familia. Solo tuvimos problemas al ingreso porque un fin de semana larga todos los visitantes tenemos que ingresar a las 3pm y se bloqueó de forma adversa para el trato fluido qué uno espera. Punto a mejorar.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Clean and well maintained resort.  There seemed to be someone cleaning something 24/7. Beach not so much. I usually go for the smaller resorts and a more personalized dining experience. This resort has a ton of rooms so its all about feeding the masses, get them in and out. Not a place to consider if you are a foody.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: El hotel está completamente renovado. Las instalaciones son hermosas, nuevas, limpias. El personal es muy atento y amable. La comida es muy típica y deliciosa. El gimnasio esta bien equipado, aunque solo le faltan barras y discos. Lo único es que si te tocan las habitaciones que empiezan con 6, prepárate para caminar muchísimo para ir a la playa, al foro o al restaurante Liberia (el más grande).

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Excelente lugar bastante variedad y muy amplio nunca se ve abarrotada, el. Personal debería enfocarse en atender a todos bien no solo a los que traen propinas...

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
The check in at the resort start well with a receptionist named Sussy. But on our third day at the resort, the lights and AC went out and seek help from the front desk people and that experience was a nightmare! I have to go twice to the front desk to have the lights/AC fixed. the lights went out again in the middle of the night and I was told by the front desk people earlier that no one can help us if the lights went out in the middle of the night so we waited the next morning to have it fix. The front desk people named Dacey /Daisy and Naye and all the rest of the front people at the desk were all defensive, rude, and sarcastic about the situation. I send emails regarding this situation and have gotten no reply. I have to ask a WestJet representative at the resort to help me sort out the issues. Fortunately, NAOMI was the one who handled the issue with professionalism and with an excellent problem solving skills and shown so much empathy with the nightmare we’ve experienced.  We cannot thank her enough for making the rest of our stay smooth and enjoyable. She is the reason why I am giving Riu Guanacaste a 5 star.  The people at the dining buffet and Steakhouse were also wonderful.
I hope and I am wishing Naomi well that she will succeed in her career to an even higher heights for being such a kind beautiful soul and a wonder worker.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Great resort with wonderful service and the friendliest staff Ive ever met

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Overall, there were little hiccups but it was a fantastic vacation.  I would recommend this resort to my friends with a few disclaimers.  The buffet staff were quite nice but sometimes we didn't see them so weren't able to get the drinks we wanted (i.e. sometimes it was just coffee or water).  My niece loved the pool and it was such a nice and clean atmosphere.  The beach was awesome but make sure and wear your watershoes or flipflops as the beautiful dark sand gets hot!

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: A must visit to this location. Had a ball! Service was amazing.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Estuvo todo muy bonito solo siento que debería haber mas variedad en carnes
Pero por el resto todo excelente

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Le côté positif :
Le personnel est très attentif aux besoins des clients.
La nourriture/buffet est variée et fraîche.
Les chambres très propres et grandes.

Côté à améliorer :
Le restaurant italien : j'ai été très déçue de la nourriture. C'est un buffet pas si bon.
Le restaurant Steakhouse : nous avions 1hre pour manger, et après dehors/laisser la place aux autres.
Je n'ai pas eu le temps de m'installer alors que le bol de salade était déjà sur la table.
Insatisfaction garantie.

La plage est publique. Donc, pas nettoyée. Déçue de ce côté-là.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: C'est vraiment un bel hôtel, flambant neuf, la nourriture vraiment bonne, belles piscines, jeux d'eaux pour enfants et adultes, service impeccable ! Juste du WOW!

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Y nous manquait des serviettes un soir sur 2 pour se laver.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: I enjoyed

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Stayed a week with my wife and kids for our 10 year anniversary. Rooms were nice enough. Very nice views. Plenty of pools and the beach isn't too far a walk.

Food could be better, some things needed longer cooking mostly the vegetables (sometimes the veggies felt raw). Things could've been seasoned better, pizza could use more sauce. Drinks are good, they don't feel watered down and the unlimited mini bar in the room is great.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Puntos a mejorar:

A nuestra llegada, el personal de recepción nos atendió de mala forma.

El internet fue inestable durante la estadía.

Tuvimos problemas con el check-in online y debimos volver a recepción.

La calidad de los cócteles y la atención variaban según el bar y el bartender.

En los restaurantes temáticos nos dijeron que no requerían reserva, pero no encontramos espacio.

Aspectos positivos:

La habitación y las áreas comunes siempre estuvieron limpias.

El personal de limpieza y del restaurante buffet Liberia fue muy amable y atento.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Muy sastisfecho mi familia les encantó muy muy espacioso la comida muy buena no hay quejas me encantaría poder volver ..

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text: Segunda vez lindo les quedo el hotel renovado de guanacaste.

name: Hotel Riu Guanacaste
review_text:
Everything from food to experience was just average.
Breakfast at the buffet could have more variety. The drinks at the bar are not done very well and it can take very long to get service. You'll be ignored entirely even.
There's a sewage smell as soon as I enter the room. The towels have a dirty smell like they weren't washed with detergent.
The beach is not white sand but it is still good.
For entertainment, the shows are done really well. I enjoyed it.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Really nice restaurant, great Peruvian food,Denzel our server was very nice guy,and very professional. Highly recommend.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Exelente comida, ubicación y Servicio.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Fuimos a almorzar y nos trajeron platos diferentes a lo que pedimos y mucho más costosos y un restaurante peruano que no tenía pizco.
Pensé que por lo menos nos cobrarían lo que pedimos pero no les importo
Pésimo servicio y el restaurante lleno de zancudos y caliente

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: La Divina Comida es definitivamente uno de nuestros lugares favoritos. Siempre disfrutamos muchísimo ir porque la comida es deliciosa y las porciones son súper generosas. Nuestros platos favoritos son la causa de mariscos, el ceviche clásico con leche de tigre y el risotto paisano, todos con muchísimo sabor y muy bien preparados. La calidad de la comida y la consistencia hacen que siempre queramos volver. Muy recomendado para quienes disfrutan de buena comida

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: We kwamen met een grote groep, meerdere keren bevestigd maar ze hadden duidelijk niet op ons gerekend. Bepaalde gerechten waren er niet of werden verkeerd gebraht. En we hebben ruim een uur op het voorgerecht gewacht. Het water werd niet echt goed aangevuld. En toen we bij het dessert aankwamen, kreeg iedereen gewoon een random klein potje voor zijn neus zonder uitleg. Het was echt slecht geregeld.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: The food and service were on point. One thing to be aware of is the menu is gigantic and only in spanish.  Its also very tiny type. If you dont know spanish bring your translate app and plan on it taking a while to review the huge selection.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: La comida muy mala para el precio, cero calidad y nada extravagante. El ambiente malo tambien, no habia nadie, se nota que esta mas en decadencia, pero el servicio excelente, la gente nos atendio muy bien.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Excelente la comida y para que decir los postres. Creo que es el mejor suspiro limeño que he probado!!. Buena sentencian del personal.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Lugar y ambiente muy bonito en la zona de Escazu !!! Comida fusion buena y sabrosa el sitio

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Buenísima comida y el servicio excelente! Fue nuestro primer día en Costa Rica y no pudo haber empezado mejor en este restaurante.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Desde la comida hasta la atención super el menú es poco complicado pero con la ayuda del mesonero pides.

Gastamos 95.000 3 plato 1 entrada mas bebidas

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Primera y última vez. La comida estuvo bien, pero el precio no se justifica. El servicio al cliente fue deficiente: pedimos una entrada, quedaron de confirmar si tenían los ingredientes y nunca nos respondieron, no ofrecieron bebidas y en general el trato fue pobre.

Mientras esperaba la comida vi en su Facebook una promoción del 25% de descuento en todo el menú, válida hasta el 31 de enero de 2026. Al pedir la cuenta se lo mencioné al mesero y me indicaron que ya no aplicaba. Intentamos hablar con alguien más y nos dijeron que nadie podía ayudarnos, con una actitud despectiva, poco profesional y de mala gana, lo que terminó de arruinar la experiencia.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Extremely slow and inattentive service. Asked for and got still water, but then they topped up with sparkling. Ordered a beer which took 15 minutes minutes to arrive, and then they came with two…
The food var hugely oversized, maybe to compensate for the poor taste.
The sound level is incredible noisy, as it bounces off the cement walls.
Don’t go to this restaurant!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Food is excellent and the staff is superb.  They are also very honest.  We left our backpack with wallet and passports and everything was there when we came to look for it. We will be forever grateful to this establishment.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
We left disappointed.

The biggest issue: I ordered pasta for my kids and was charged extra for Parmesan cheese. Charging separately for something that is a basic part of a children’s pasta dish feels unreasonable and frankly absurd. It left a very bad impression.

To be fair, not everything was bad.
The tiradito starter was good, and my wife enjoyed the corvina a lo macho, both were recommended by the waiter. The service itself was fine; the waiter did his job well.

Unfortunately, the rest didn’t deliver. The tuna Nikkei ceviche was very average, and the seafood risotto lacked flavour and was not enjoyable.

Overall, the place let us down. The owner should take a hard look at the concept and pricing decisions, charging for basic items like Parmesan undermines the experience and doesn’t match expectations at this level.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Excelente comida

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Todo fue excelente, el lugar, la atención y la comida fue exquisita, los platillos ordenados fueron un asado peruano y risotto con lomo salteado, las porciones eran muy generosas y todo en su punto

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: El lugar es muy lindo. Fuimos a celebrar un cumpleaños (13 personas). Sin embargo, esperamos 1:40min para que nos trajeran la comida. Pedimos entradas y platos fuertes y todo llegó al mismo tiempo. Algunos platos estaban muy ricos pero a otros les faltó sabor. Para la cantidad de tiempo que esperamos no volveríamos a ir. Llegamos a las 8:30 y a pesar de que éramos la única mesa que quedaba, la comida nos llegó hasta las 10:20pm. (cierran a las 11pm). Durante todo ese tiempo no se preocuparon para ver si queríamos algo más de tomar. Para cuando terminamos de comer, obviamente el restaurante ya había cerrado. Mientras comíamos comenzaron a recoger y acomodar mesas lo cuál hace que uno se sienta apresurado para comer. No nos dio tiempo de tomarnos un cafe tranquilos (primero nos dijeron que ya habían apagado la máquina)  y aunque si nos ofrecieron postre ya era muy tarde.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Great service, terrible food, tasteless fish, boring ceviches.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Pésima relación calidad precio , la comida sabe súper básica y nada “peruana”, porciones pequeñas y sin sabor.
Rescató la atención de los meseros que es realmente buena.
De las peores formas de gastar el dinero , nosotros nos sentimos estafados :/

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: La comida no era de buena calidad el ceviche umami estaba amargo , nos lo comimos y nos cayó mal , decía que tenía leche de tigre pero nunca la sentimos , no se puede comparar con la comida peruana real , comida mala calidad, mal sabor y mala relación calidad precio , en cuanto servicio de personal es lo único que rescatamos de nuestra mala experiencia , meseros muy amables y serviciales ! Íbamos con altas expectativas y salimos desepcionados :( con hambre y enfermos !!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Ubicado en una de esas zonas modernas y vidriadas que podrían ser Puerto Madero en Buenos Aires, o cualquier barrio cosmopolita de una gran ciudad. Si uno se despertara allí sin contexto, podría creer que está en cualquier centro urbano de primer nivel. Y la propuesta del lugar acompaña esa sensación: un restaurante de cocina fusión peruana que busca ser moderno, elegante y con un toque de exclusividad, pero con los pies firmes en las raíces de la gastronomía del Perú.

Desde la mesa se entiende rápido que acá hay alguien pensando en la experiencia completa: el bajo plato de cerámica en tonos fríos, el individuales de fibra trenzada que aportan textura y calidez, el vaso verde tallado, la servilleta celeste con la marca bordada y, como guiño marino, el aro con forma de pez que la abraza. Nada grita “lujo”, pero todo susurra criterio y coherencia. No es decoración al azar; es narrativa visual.

La cocina está a cargo del chef Marco Antonio Ganoza, y su presencia en sala es parte central de la experiencia. Se lo ve de buen humor, cercano, atento, moviéndose entre mesas con la seguridad de quien domina su producto, pero sin el ego sobreactuado que a veces intoxica la alta cocina. Se nota que el show existe, pero también se nota algo más importante: autenticidad.

Su carta es de esas que se leen y se entiende que hubo horas de prueba, error y corrección detrás de cada plato. Fusión bien entendida: respeto por la base peruana y licencias inteligentes para jugar con sabores y técnicas. Hasta ahí, todo dentro del manual de un buen restaurante contemporáneo.

Lo verdaderamente interesante apareció cuando uno de los comensales decidió salirse del guion y pidió algo tan simple como peligroso para cualquier cocina seria:
un atún en punto blue, apenas marcado, sin más artificios.

Ese pedido, en muchos lugares, se resuelve con una negativa amable o con una versión “adaptada” que encaje en el concepto de la carta. Marco Antonio, en cambio, aceptó el desafío sin dramatismo. Y ahí es donde se ve el oficio.

Un atún servido casi desnudo no tiene dónde esconderse:
•	si la materia prima no es impecable, se nota al primer bocado;
•	si el punto de cocción se pasa medio segundo, el resultado se vuelve corriente;
•	si la temperatura o el sellado están mal resueltos, el plato se cae.

El chef no solo cumplió con lo pedido, lo superó. El atún llegó a la mesa en su justa expresión: color intenso, exterior apenas sellado, corazón crudo y sedoso, sabor limpio, sin rastros metálicos ni excesos de frío. Un plato minimalista que, por simple, se vuelve examen técnico. Y la cocina lo aprobó con nota alta, al nivel de cualquier casa especializada en plazas como Lima, Buenos Aires o Ciudad de México.

La sala acompaña ese estándar. El equipo de servicio funciona como una extensión del criterio del dueño y del chef: profesionales, atentos, siempre con una sonrisa sincera, sin caer en la sobreactuación ni en la confianza excesiva. Cada movimiento parece ensayado, pero sin rigidez; como si todos supieran que el objetivo es uno solo: que el comensal se vaya mejor de lo que llegó.

En definitiva, La Divina Comida es de esos lugares donde todo encaja: el contexto, el espacio, la propuesta gastronómica, la materia prima y el factor humano. Se siente la mano de alguien que eligió a su equipo con el mismo cuidado con el que selecciona un buen trozo de atún: sin concesiones.

Una experiencia altamente recomendable para quien busque en Costa Rica algo más que “comer bien”:
buscar coherencia, identidad y oficio en cada detalle, desde un simple plato de atún vuelta y vuelta hasta la sonrisa con la que te despiden en la puerta.

Gracias, Marco Antonio, por la propuesta y por recordarnos que la verdadera fusión no está solo en el plato, sino en la manera en que se combinan técnica, producto y humanidad.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
We use to love this place! It use to be one our favorite restaurants in Ave. Escazu.

Unfortunately it is a different experience than what we use to look forward to.

Now our favorite dishes are inconsistent, service is also not impeccable like it used to be to be (I think the servers are unhappy, for whatever reason - I’ll let that be)

I think the biggest mistake they made was changing what they have their menu on. It use to be on you basic laminated menu with pictures to a newspaper format that is so confusing that you spend most of your time at dinner trying to figure it out, and when you ask the waiter questions, their frustration is noticeable.

We will not be looking forward to this experience any longer.

Sad! 😔

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Lo que pedí de comer no tenía nada de sabor, la mesera fue muy amable pero la calidad de la comida no vale la pena y menos a esos precios, cambiaron el chef y se nota por qué el sabor es pésimo.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: En la vida comeria yo aqui

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Clasica comida Peruana, muy buen sabor en un ambiente agradable

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Pedimos un ceviche, carpaccio de remolacha y chaufa de pollo. Con el ceviche me intoxiqué y pasé toda la noche más que fatal. El carpaccio eran rodajas gruesas, no Delgado como debe ser un carpaccio. El chaufa 1kg de arroz sin ningún sabor, ni sal y mínima cantidad de pollo. Los 3 anfitriones al lado de la mesa quitando toda privacidad en su vaciladera y el salonero al pedirle agua para tomar y no un trago, cambió por completo su manera de atendernos. A veces uno solo quiere agua. No volveremos.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
La comida no cumplió con las expectativas. Pedí un tacu tacu  y un ceviche; ambos sin sabor y con mala textura. El ceviche se sentía viscoso y la leche de tigre casi no se percibía. Lo mejor fueron las cortesías de yuca con una salsa roja muy buena. En general, mala relación calidad-precio y poca autenticidad peruana.
A pesar de eso, el ambiente y la atención fueron excelentes. El lugar tiene una temática muy original, el host y los meseros fueron súper atentos y amables. Una gran experiencia en servicio, pero la comida necesita mejorar.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: La comida en otras ocasiones fue un éxito, después de unos años sin ir al restaurante me encontré algunas cosas que no me gustaron, el menú es un periódico enorme que es difícil de leer, no se ve atractivo aparte de tener anuncios que no son interés a la hora de elegir la comida, los asientos que están pegados a la pared son despachadores tienen una espuma suave a la que se le sienten las tablas, los colaboradores estaban hablando y riéndose fuertísimo con un vocabulario no apto para el lugar y uno de ellos no tenía conocimiento del menú, para terminar algunas sillas rotas y sucias como la de la foto, creo que hay cosas que deben ir acorde al precio de los platos, para pagar 20 mil o 35 mil colones por un plato es porque va más allá de la comida, es el servicio, la comodidad y que se brinde toda una experiencia

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: We had heard really great things about this restaurant, so we had to try it. We made a reservation for a Saturday night and when we got there, there were only 2 other tables with customers. I had a ceviche as the entrée and the  portion was really big. If I had known, I wouldn't have ordered a main (salmon cakes). I wasn't even able to eat had of my main course. The ceviche was really good, and the salmon cakes were tasty. However, the service was disappointing. They seemed confused, barely spoke to us and were slow. Even though the food was good, I didn't feel like coming back here because of this.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
El lugar es privado, perfecto para un evento. Sin embargo, el servicio y la comida no estuvieron a la altura del precio.

El pan y las bebidas tardaron o nunca llegaron; el agua solo la sirvieron una vez.

El menú era limitado y varios platos no estaban disponibles. Cuando llegó la comida, la calidad fue decepcionante: carnes secas, pescado amargo y postres servidos sin opción real de elegir.

En general, la experiencia no justificó el costo.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Un servicio muy bueno, pero la comida no está al nivel de los precios. Nada fue extraordinario, y al contrario, el ossobuco estaba muy duro y chicludo.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: The food and service were good overall. However, it took nearly 1 hour and 40 minutes for our meal to arrive, even though there were only three of us in my table and the restaurant was less than 30% full. Because of the long wait, I couldn’t fully enjoy the food as much as I would have liked.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: El servicio fue en extremo LENTO, más de una hora para el plato fuerte. Y era un menú dirigido

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Had a great time. La Divina Comida has amazing food, and the waiter was very kind. Would definitely come back once again.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Food was delicious. I enjoyed everything i had. It was a bit on the expensive side but I knew that going in.
Service was great.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Llegamos 930 no les gustó mucho un jueves
El ceviche lo sirvieron sin el maíz y simplemente me
Dijo no hay maíz
Sin antes avisarme para yo cambiar de plato
Pedí un rissoto paisano
Rico sabor muy bueno pero TENÍA 100 gramos de carne ósea es un plato de niño de 21 mil colones
Le pregunté a ella cuánto ponen de carne me dijo 200 gramos
Se les dañó la pesa porque eso tenía 100gramos

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Demasiado ruidoso

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: We are here twice in one week. I fell in love with the leche de tigre.  The salmon with golden berry sauce was unique and delicious. We had two different cerviche dishes that were great but I forgot to take photos that evening. The passion fruit dessert was a hit. Again, if you want your bill from the server you need to ask, they will let you sit there for a long, long time when you are finished eating and never offer the check. I think most customers also have drinks while they are there, but we were there for dental work so we were drinking only water. Overall good service other than waiting for the final bill for a long time until we realized we needed to ask specifically for it. I believe it is just a cultural difference. Fourth visit - lunch menu with amazing appetizer of ceviche with leche de tigra (I should just order large version of this every time!) for my main entree I tried the 4 hour stewed beef which was very tender and tomato sauce with rice. I wish there always a bit more flavor and more sauce in the meat. My friend has the seafood entree with fish and clams in tomato sauce. Traditional creme carmel type of dessert was good. Another lunch visit for traditional cerviche 🤤 so good!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Primera vez que vengo, quitando las entradas el plato fuerte decepcionante, casi 20,000 por un Pulpo a la parrilla con ensalada, por la porción de pulpo (2 tentáculos), más bien debería promocionarse como "ensalada con pulpo", quitando la parte de las porciones, el pulpo bastante duro, no se cuantas veces lo tuve que masticar, si lo comparó con otros restaurantes que he ido, muy caro y no se compara con esos otros lugares y son muy buenos.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Tardaron media hora en venir a tomarnos la orden y alrededor de hora y media en servirnos el plato.
Plato que, dicho y sea de paso, es excesivamente caro para lo que es.
El pollo a la huancaína traía dos gotitas de "salsa huancaína", la cual no sabía a nada.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Hands down the best food I’ve had in Costa Rica . The octopus is absolutely amazing. Greta is a fantastic server , very professional . I would eat here every day.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Ate there recently based on the reviews.
Food was wonderful, nice intimate setting.
Service was real good, would recommend more people try this restaurant 👍👍👍👍

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Un lugar y atención que hace que uno desee volver!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Absolutely amazing restaurant, the food was incredible. We had carpaccio for starters, I had the burger and my wife the octopus. The fries with the burger were the best I have ever had. The sauce with the carpaccio was delicious. Great drinks selection. Wonderful atmosphere and will accommodate groups and children.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Absolutely outstanding food, especially the seafood soup. Great service as well, thanks Jose!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Sehr professioneller und wertschätzender Service; obwohl ich allein kam, hat man mir mit den besten Platz gegeben (kommt auf dem Foto nicht ganz rüber) hat eine gemütliche Stimmung und es wurden tolle Empfehlung in der Karte ausgesprochen.

Vor allem im vergleich zu anderen Restaurants auf der Avenida, kam man sich hier nicht wie ein ,,nerviger Tourist“ vor.

Hatte das Ceviche Clasico (schon große Portion für ein entrada) und ein octopus mit Chia und Gemüse & sehr leckerer Sauce. Preis eher hoch, aber auf jeden Fall wert. Danke für das tolle Essen.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: The food is outstanding and fresh. Delightful wait staff and super friendly towards this gringo! What an amazing Lunch!!!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Delicioso todo!   Y excelente servicio

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Vengo saliendo de la Divina Comida con mi esposa. Vinimos por casualidad y por la hora, pero el servicio brindado por don Tulio, aseguró que la casualidad se hará rutina.

Muy impresionados de la amabilidad y el extraordinario servicio, algo que ahora cuesta mucho ver en el país. La comida le hace honor al nombre, muy buena.

Solo por el servicio, vale la pena venir al restaurante.  Muy bueno.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
La comida es excelente, pero el servicio deja mucho que desear.
Los platillos son muy buenos, con sabores bien logrados y buena presentación. Sin embargo, el servicio fue extremadamente lento y desorganizado. Tardaron demasiado en tomarnos la orden, en retirar los platos, y en general, los meseros se mostraron poco atentos. En varias ocasiones fue la anfitriona quien tuvo que venir a atendernos, lo que refleja una clara falta de coordinación en el equipo. Una experiencia que podría haber sido memorable por la cocina, terminó opacada por un servicio deficiente.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Brought my 84 yo mother for her birthday.nit was truly an elevated fine dining experience.  The food was remarkable which each appetizer and entree offering nuanced, surprising and delicious flavors.  Our staff, Jose, was warm and made sure he knew all of our names.  The manager and owner also came over to make certain our experience was good.  Truly a birthday we will never forget.  Thank you to everyone at La Divina Comida for a wonderful evening.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: La comida es deliciosa y sirven porciones bastante grandes , no había exactamente lo que quería en el menú y pero  me hicieron la opción,  pasta en salsa bechamel con camarones , tienen  buenas opciones de vino . El ambiente es muy acogedor y tienen una linda terraza . La atención de Antonio es excelente , muy amable , cortés y carismático.  Recomiendo este lugar !

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Great food and ambiance!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Fui a cenar con mi esposo y salimos bastante decepcionados los dos. Pedimos dos ají de gallina (que es nuestro platillo favorito) y nos pareció demasiado pesado, al final nos cayó mal la cena a los dos y no pudimos terminar de cenar porque teníamos dolor de estómago.
En media cena pasó una cucaracha grande cerca al lado de nuestra mesa y tuvimos que pedirle al mesero que hiciera algo.
La información de los precios no es clara en el menú, ya que pedimos 2 ají de gallina que al final nos dijeron que eso no está en el menú y lo que habíamos visto era la opción de sándwich, entonces lo cobraron más caro.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
I can no longer recommend this place as highly as I did.
Old post: Excellent. Great food. Waiter is a little pushy but otherwise very very good.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: So, i have been visiting La divina comida for special occasions since 2017, If I'm not mistaken, used to be very delicious and excellent service, with a very calm and fancy atmosphere, however, last time I went. It was a chaos, the waiters were super accelerate if you will, they drop things, broke glasses, the atmosphere was veeeeryyy loud. They used to give you bread with a tomato sauce,  now it's gone, also the risotto paisano (favorite dish in there) was not cooked perfectly like used to be, and the spiciness and flavor was not the same it used to be. Unfortunately, no longer my favorite restaurant anymore.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Un excelente lugar para comer.
Los sabores y presentación de los platillos es excelente.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: This is my favorite restaurant for seafood in San Jose. Everything I tried was very good. Highly recommended!

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Good, delicious and expensive, but u paid for something really good

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text:
Excelente servicio💯 Santiago y Sebastián fueron muy atentos durante nuestra estadía

La comida exquisita.

Muchas gracias

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Cool cool cool place! I stumbled upon this place as I was soaked from getting caught in the rain…they warned my stomach and my heart! Service was perfect, friendly, cheery, and helpful. Food was impeccable! Delicious.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: The food was meh. The meat was over cooked and tough. The portions were fine with my meal but other’s items seemed small.  The actual restaurant looked fine but the service was extremely slow. Our table was charged for 8 bottles of water when that was literally impossible when they poured us all a half glass of water and never refilled a glass. Four half filled water bottled were left on the table and removed while we waiting an hour and 15 minutes for our meal. I laughed when the little white rolls were brought to the table and had no flavor or salt. Very disappointing as I love Peruvian food and would have come back multiple times during my stay.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Santiago hace la diferencia.  Excelente servicio, se ve que lo hace de buen gusto.  Espero que pueda graduarse de medicina.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Very good but expensive

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Le di 4 estrellas porque un lugar donde el pulpo invade la carta, no tenía pulpo disponible en la cena, y tampoco te avisan al traerte el menú. También faltaron opciones de vinos blancos.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Excelente servicio , especialmente la atención de Karol, divina ella. Deliciosos pisco sour, especialmente de Pitayaha, no lo había tomado. El pulpo a la parrilla crujiente, todo muy sabroso. Vale la pena ir a comer la rica gastronomía peruana.  Felicitaciones al chef y al equipo.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: No le doy 5 estrellas porque la mitad de la carta tiene pulpo pero no tenían pulpo en la cena. Además pedí vino blabco en botella y sólo tenían una opción (la más cara) de las 5 de la carta. Una ebtrada compartida, dos platos fuertes, un piatre compartido, dos copas de vino de la casa y dos aguas, 140 dólares.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: El ambiente y la atención de los meseros son muy buenos, pero lamentablemente la calidad de la comida deja mucho que desear. Las porciones son grandes, pero el precio elevado no se justifica por la comida mediocre, insípida y alejada de la verdadera esencia de la gastronomía peruana. Quienes elogian este restaurante claramente no han probado auténtica comida peruana. Pedimos dos ceviches y dos risottos de gallina al ají, y ambos platos carecían de sabor y originalidad. La cuenta final, que ascendió a casi 60 mil colones por dos entradas, dos platos principales, dos bebidas y dos postres, no reflejó en absoluto la calidad de lo que recibimos. Además, nos sorprendió que el postre, que inicialmente pensamos que era un gesto de cortesía por la celebración de un cumpleaños, nos fue cobrado. En comparación, restaurantes como Silvestre o Sikwa ofrecen una experiencia gourmet auténtica a un precio mucho más razonable. Lamentablemente, esta experiencia fue una gran decepción y no volveré a este restaurante.

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Take care with the Menú price

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Excelente

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Fui a almorzar con unos amigos . Ya había pedido varias veces pero para pasar a retirar y comer en casa. El lugar es precioso y la comida realmente deliciosa . Tuve un pequeño inconveniente con la muchacha que nos atendió pero el dueño Marco lo resolvió de una forma extraordinaria . Volvería con mucho gusto . Recomendado si quieres probar comida peruana fusión en un lugar diferente

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Buena experiencia excelente ambiente para repetir

name: La Divina Comida Fusión Peruana
review_text: Una de las mejores opciones para ti y los tuyos🤗

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Best resort I’ve ever visited. The private swimming pool gave us a lot of fun. The resort restaurants are decent. It’s the copycat version of a Michelin three key hotel with only half of the price. The room was luxurious for two people.  Go for it even one or two days you won’t regret. Cheers!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
Excellent resort. Room is sparkling clean and has excellent view of the Volcano. Pools are surrounded by rain forest and the privecy is great.
The only words I can say about their service are"Five Stars".
The only negative things I can say is the road from St. Jose to the hotel is a little bit challenge and the price is a little bit high. As a 5 star and 3 keys Michelin resort, these are worth it.
Highly recommend this resort, especially to those couples with special events, such as annuversary or honeymoon.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: This is an incredible hotel, incredible service and just amazing location and surroundings. The room was probably one of the very best I have EVER stayed in - esp the private plunge pool. I cannot recommend this hotel highly enough. I am already booking to go back with my whole family (I was solo this time around). Whether you're a couple, a family, old, young - everyone was at the resort and it seemed perfect for all. I honestly can't fault one thing. The people and the service were the real stand outs. But its location, stunning, amazing rainforest location and high quality maintenance and facilities are just some of the best I ever been to. Their restaurants and food were incredible. Finally its the little things I loved too - like the free mini-bar, always replenished, free breakfast (amazing quality - full omelet bar etc, daily specials, local flavors and produce from the markets) and free laundry. Other hotels offer a good base product & then charge you on and on - not here. Book it now - you will have the holiday of a lifetime I promise.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: My stay at Nayara Gardens was beautiful, and I had a wonderful trip overall! Traveling solo can sometimes be intimidating, but the effort from all the wonderful people working here made me feel completely safe and deeply valued throughout my entire stay.|The Highlights: Incredible Staff|A huge thank you to the amazing team. Greivin completely elevated my experience right from the start and was there for me until my very last day. From showing me to my room to pointing out resort highlights and spotting wildlife, he really inspired me to explore. I will absolutely ask if he is available to assist us again when I return to stay at the Nayara Tents with my partner and family!|Jack and Michael were also fantastic. They went out of their way to show me a three-claw sloth close up, which was an absolutely unforgettable experience. Huge appreciation to Andy at Sukha Spa and Julio at Nostalgia Wine Bar as well. Their wonderful service is a big reason why this resort is worth returning to.||There are just a quick area that could use a little streamlining to make the guest experience perfect:|| Late Checkout Process: I had a midnight flight and tried to arrange a late checkout in advance. I was told to check back on the morning of my departure. This made it impossible to plan my last day or comfortably book any final tours, and my checkout still ended up being at noon anyway.|Streamlining these scheduling details would make an already great experience even better. Thank you to the team for a lovely visit—I look forward to coming back!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Fun, excellent friendly staff

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: This our third year in a row at Nayara Gardens!  We LOVE it there! The staff is over the top professional, nice and accommodating.  The resort is simply one of a kind!  The rainforest villas are incredible. Each one is unique and nestled in the rain forest. The food is fresh and delicious.  The spa is incredible.  I have travelled extensively and this is by far my favorite resort to visit. I can't say enough about Nayara Gardens!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Incredible customer service, immaculate, spacious rooms, outstanding food– a luxurious getaway perfect for relaxation. My partner and I loved our stay here! We got engaged the same day as we arrived to the property and when we mentioned it upon check-in they had so many thoughtful touches to make our special day even more special, even though it was last- minute notice.||Highly recommend this place if you’re looking for somewhere to fully recharge and relax.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
We spent three nights at Nayara Gardens for our honeymoon. Magical is genuinely the only word for it.
From the moment we arrived, it was clear this place operates on a different level. Our room — the Arenal Pool Casita — had a rose petal arrangement on the bed, a bottle of prosecco, and a chocolate cake waiting for us. The kind of welcome that sets the tone for everything that follows.
The casita feels more like a home than a hotel room. The furnishings are tasteful, the bed is incredibly comfortable, and the bathroom is honestly something else. Every evening, the turndown service transformed the bungalow into a quiet, peaceful cocoon — coming back after dinner always felt like a little moment in itself. The daily laundry service was also a genuinely appreciated touch. One less thing to think about.
And then there’s the deck. A private heated pool overlooking the rainforest, with the Arenal Volcano right in front of you. The water temperature is perfect, and we ended up spending more time in our own pool than anywhere else on the property — which says a lot, given how many beautiful pools there are.
The wider property is vast, but getting around is easy — either on foot or via the golf carts that are always on hand. Taking a golf cart across the grounds for a dinner reservation genuinely felt like a little date night each time. You’ll find pools, bars, restaurants and shops dotted throughout, and our clear favorite was Ayla at Tented Camp — outstanding food in a really special setting.
Don’t sleep on the binoculars available at reception. Take them everywhere — we came across sloths, monkeys and toucans just walking between venues. On the same note, the complimentary nature walks are not to be missed. The guides are friendly, knowledgeable, and you’re guaranteed to spot something remarkable and learn a thing or two about this beautiful country.
The staff across the whole property were wonderful, but a special mention to the team at Ayla — and particularly to William, the restaurant manager, who made sure every dinner we had there was a great one. He would always take a moment to chat with us at the end of the meal, which made the whole experience feel very warm and personal.
We left already talking about coming back. If you’re looking for somewhere truly special, this is it.
Thank you Nayara for making our honeymoon so unforgettable.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Truly one of the best stays ever. Nayara  Gardens is a very  special place. Everything was perfect - the setting, the service, the food - all beyond anything we could have imagined.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
Amazing magical hotel
Is soooooo beautiful

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
We stayed at the Tented Camps 2 years ago and loved it so much we decided to go back to Nayara.

This time with our 1 year old and her grandparents and at Nayara Gardens since it was noted as family friendly. Unfortunately we didn’t have as great of an experience this time around and it didn’t feel family friendly. I’m not sure we’ll be going back to stay at Nayara again.

First, there was blackouts and wifi outages when we arrived. They mentioned the blackouts were due to the power company. They also said the same for the wifi but oddly enough, reception somehow still had wifi.

Second, half of the outlets in our room didn’t work, and only the ones in the bathroom area were functional. Reception seemed skeptical and asked if we had the card in the slot. We did as the air conditioning was obviously working. When they sent someone to check, it seems like something may have been switched off and he had to flip it back on from a panel located outside.

Third, communication of the onsite activities available at the resort was contradicting which was frustrating. The Tented Camps offer a night walk but the male receptionist we spoke to made it sound like it’s strictly for guests of the Tented Camps only. It seemed unfortunate and a bit family unfriendly considering all the hotels are under the same resort. It would be more understandable if it was a first come, first serve basis especially as the night walk seems more targeted for children. Later, when we were speaking with the receptionist at the Gardens and mentioned it, she said that there is priority for the Tented Camps guests but guests of the other hotels could also participate. But, by that point we had already made other plans.

Fourth, I understand there’s Nayara Springs and some places would be adults only. But, the espresso bar being adults only seems strange as it is for coffee, tea and some pastries.

Fifth, at random times throughout our stay there was a sewage smell. This made it difficult to enjoy time in the pool in our room. Also, not something we want to smell on the way to breakfast.

Shoutout to Jafeth at La Terraza. We had dinner there one evening and he was very thoughtful and had our daughter’s order prepared first, unprompted, just incase we needed to get her ready for bed.

The complimentary laundry service was also a plus especially since it’s so hot.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Staying at Nayara was a truly unique experience. Everything within the hotel grounds is incredible. The views, the food, the facilities, the animals, the flora, and the staff’s hospitality and service, everything is excellent. It’s an experience that’s hard to describe or put into words; you have to experience it for yourself to understand just how incredible it is.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: We visited for three nights as part of our honeymoon trip to Costa Rica and Nayara is one of the best hotels I have stayed in across the world as a pretty well travelled lover of luxury resorts. Attentive, friendly staff, which absolutely are the reason this hotel is outstanding, good restaurants and beautiful setting. Particular shout out to José at Henry’s Bar - great drinks and company! ||I could not recommend enough, we will certainly be back to see the Nayara team in future!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Our stay at Nayara Gardens was simply spectacular. From the moment we arrived, the kindness and professionalism of the entire staff truly stood out. Everyone was attentive, welcoming, and always ready to help with a smile, making us feel comfortable and cared for throughout our stay.}|The property itself is stunning, surrounded by beautiful nature and designed perfectly to create a peaceful and luxurious atmosphere. The rooms were gorgeous, spacious, and extremely comfortable — the perfect place to relax and enjoy the experience.||The food was another highlight of our trip. Every meal we had was outstanding, with amazing flavors, excellent presentation, and top-quality ingredients. In addition, the gelateria on the property is an absolute must-visit for a sweet treat; the ice cream is delicious and truly complements the whole experience.||Without a doubt, this is a hotel we highly recommend, and we would love to return in the future.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nayara is a luxurious resort and spa that has everything you might want on vacation - on-site volcanic-fed thermal hot springs, villas and glamping with heated plunge pools and volcano views, and a host of on-site 4- and 5-star restaurants that catering to anyone's taste.  It's actually made up of three distinct areas, all walkable but spread out, including an adult-only section and restaurants.  The hotel includes a host of included guided nature activities, and we saw more sloths on the property at Nayara than anywhere else in Costa Rica, including both 2 and 3-toed, one with a baby!  While it's a huge complex, a system of golf carts and paved paths can take you wherever you may want to go, and all communications are through "What's App," with dedicated operators taking your messages for immediate action.  No one uses this form of communication than Nayara - ice comes in minutes, we never waited more than 5 minutes for a ride - and it was usually only a couple.  From the property you can access some truly wonderful experiences:  we were easily able to do the dawn bird watching at Mistico (highly recommended before the crowds), and canyoneering thru PureTrek, located only a kilometer or so from the Gardens.  Rappelling the waterfalls in the Arenal area was one highlight of our trip.  Our room was spacious, well-appointed, with a truly fantastic bathroom and storage, complete with a large private deck with a partial volcano view and heated infinity pool with jacuzzi jets, but the villas - all separate buildings - are close together, and there will be some noise from AC units and pool pumps.  There are complimentary refreshments stocked daily in the room, and turndown service while you are on tour or at dinner.  While we did experience an issue with our room, the staff immediately responded and corrected the issue (more or less), and the general manager of the property became personally involved offered us significant compensation for the trouble - a mark of a truly world-class destination.  The property has pools with swim-up bars; 5 thermal pools fed by natural springs, each a different temperature, and the wine-pairing dinner at the Nostalgia Wine Bar is an experience that is simply not-to-miss, one of the most intimate experiences of the type we've ever had.  Make sure you give yourself some time here, though; Nayara is a massive luxury resort with so many on-property amenities and activities that it demands the necessary time to fully enjoy.  And please please please take the birding and sloth tours - you'll see a massive amount of wildlife on the property's lush tropical rain forest grounds.  The grounds are spotless and the rooms clean and fresh; stop by the lotus pond at night and get a real treat from all the frogs out looking for dates!  I can't speak highly enough about the staff, from our greeting to check-in to housekeeping to the golf-cart drivers, especially Kevin who took extra time to show us some animals, and Jefferson - call him "Crazy Ivan" if you get a ride from him.  While I can't speak directly about value since our stay was part of a much larger vacation package, I would consider Nayara one of the best resorts in Latin America, and perhaps around the world.  Seamless operation, exquisitely beautiful property, centrally located in the Arenal area.  Can't recommend a stay here enough.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: The Nayara Gardens was wonderful, quiet, breath taking and exceptional staff. We would highly recommend if you are looking for a eco friendly experience in the Costa Rican rainforest - they will not disappoint.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: The best all I can dream. Full immersion in the nature Eco sistema. All is amore

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Beautiful location and wonderful rooms.  Our room was very spacious ans rhr balconies overlooked the pools, but were occluded leaving privacy intact.  The layout helps to maintain a quiet peaceful vibe and you almost feel like your the only one there.  Great stay.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Excellent hôtel. Le service est à la hauteur du prix. Le buffet petit déjeuner et les restaurants étaient excellents. Seul bémol le personnel du bar à la piscine infinity qui était froid et n’avait manifestement pas envie de travailler.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: tolle Villa mit Pool, super Service, wunderschöne Anlage, super restaurants

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nos hospedamos dos noches en este hotel en una habitación Suite con piscina privada, viajamos por nuestro aniversario de bodas, nos esperan con decoración especial, chocolate y champagne. La habitación es preciosa, muy amplia y cómoda. Cuenta con un gran jacuzzi, y una ducha exterior doble. El hotel es super grande y ofrece múltiples actividades. Es precioso caminar por los senderos, mucho verde y muchos animalitos para ver. Cuenta con varias piscinas y restaurantes, así como con piscinas termales. Ofrecen actividades para niños. La comida estuvo correcta. El personal es simpático y servicial. Es un hotel para no fumadores. No dejan fumar dentro del miso, hay que salir del predio para hacerlo.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
I have never stayed in a hotel quite like Nayara. If you are in Costa Rica this is worth every penny. This hotel is super close to the Volcano where the activities are endless. Hot springs on campus and private ones in the rooms (which I highly suggest). The service was also impeccable, they were so nice and accommodating. A wonderful Breakfast ordered to our room every morning and great restaurants all around.

Best part was one of the bar tenders found a Sloth, 2 Toucans, and monkeys in the forest near one of the pools. We really wanted to site some good animals and this made our stay well above 5 stars. Would come back here 200%. Do not cheap out if you stay here, just go big or go home, it is so worth it. Very expensive but we didn't blink an eye because the place was truly magical.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Un rêve cet hôtel est incroyable ! Il y a tout, vous pouvez profitez de toutes les piscines, sources d’eau chaude et Restaurants de l’hôtel. Nous avons aussi fait un cours de yoga. C’est le meilleur hôtel que nous avons fait. Nous avons aussi vu un paresseux et son bébé, des singes, un tayra, un énorme lézard et de beaux oiseaux.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Really a very beautiful hotel. What makes this place standout, however, is the staff. The staff goes above and beyond to assure that your experience is perfect. Admittedly, we had a slight hiccup upon check-in. We made our unhappiness known, and the manager fixed everything exceeding our expectations. Really top notch customer service in our opinion. And, frankly, it’s the little touches that make this place stand out - the free laundry done for you at your request, the complimentary mini-bar. I don’t think people mind spending for luxury when it feels luxurious. Nayara does that beautifully. Kudos to them.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
We had an incredible stay and would recommend this to anyone. This was one of our favorite hotels we’ve stayed at.

The room and property were both amazing; you feel like you’re in the middle of the rainforest. We saw sloths, toucans, and many other types of wildlife just around the property and from our room. The room was beautiful and felt very private.

The food was all very delicious; we tried many of the restaurants, and everything we ate was great.

The staff were all incredibly kind and attentive in every way.

We booked a few excursions through the hotel, and they all were great and easy to arrange.

We can’t wait to come back!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: We have been to Nayara three times now. Each time we fall more in love with it. We stay at Nayara Springs, but we love to have breakfast at the Gardens each morning. While all of the employees we encounter are very friendly, I feel there is one that especially stands out. This year we met the most incredible manager, Raul. He was always so attentive to the restaurant and the guests. We never saw him standing still, he is so hardworking. He always had the brightest smile on his face! He is the type of employee that any company would be blessed to have. On our last day he even surprised us with a lovely gift bag filled with his favorite local treats. What a wonderful way to end our trip. I truly hope that Raul gets the recognition he deserves for bringing so much joy to the guests he encounters! Thank you for making our mornings so special!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Hotel impecable en la calidad de la atención, la decoración del lugar, sus espacios y rincones. Mágico, lleno de vida. Habitaciones muy lindas y cómodas. Recomiendo Rainforest y Casitas, ambas con su piscina. Los senderos del hotel son muy lindos y todo muy bien cuidado, las piscinas termales un 10. Fuimos por 4 días y fácilmente me hubiera quedado 1 mes, no alcancé a recorrer completo el lugar. Recomiendo caminar mucho por el interior. El desayuno exquisito. El restoran de muy alto nivel. Lo repetiremos !

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
All the other reviews are true. This is one of the best properties you can stay at. If you’re coming to La fortuna no need to keep searching, book this place.
Grounds are gorgeous beyond belief. Service is truly top notch. Food was incredible. Huge property to explore.
We were here when property was fully booked but it never felt crowded. There are so many pools- one at each room and then several large ones throughout- so you could always find a place to sit even midday at the pool.
Did zip line with ecoglide (booked directly) and did canyoning and hanging bridges (booked thru hotel). All were great, canyoning was our highlight of the bunch.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Thank you Alexandra and Team for a lovely stay! Stayed with our 8 month old, extremely friendly and accommodating staff. Amazing breakfast. Beautiful rooms. Stunning grounds to walk around. Saw great wildlife around the property including sloths, capuchin, anteater and iguanas.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nayara was the most perfect vacation I've ever had! All of the staff was incredible! My girlfriend and I did a tour of the hanging bridges with Ana that was so amazing. I wish I could name every single staff member in this message since everyone was so genuinely nice.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
My wife, 11 month old and me enjoyed Nayara Gardens for 2 nights in February 2026.

Absolutely immaculate property and wonderful service. They treat you extremely well here (we are from Canada and some of the higher-end hotels locally are Fairmont - by far, in comparison, Nayara Gardens is superior in every way).

The property actually encompasses 3 different Nayara resorts (Gardens, Springs and Tented Camp). They are all unique with Gardens being a great family spot, Tented Camps being a more luxurious family/larger group area, and Springs being an adults-only zone. It also includes natural hot springs, a fitness center, 2 yoga pavilions, meditation areas, spas, and a handful of restaurants on property.

It is so beautifully curated that you can find birds, sloths, frogs, snakes, iguanas, tapir all on site wandering around.

Ultimately, once you've arrived, there is no reason to leave! You can find a piece of all of Costa Rica within is resort - all packaged in a thoughtful, wellness-focused nature resort.

Pura vida!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: This hotel was amazing. The service, the functional layouts of the rooms, the amenities, all top teir. 10/10

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
INCROYABLE !
C’était juste magnifique. Rien à dire.
L’hôtel est absolument bien pensé. Le petit déjeuné délicieux.
Tout était parfait.
Si j’avais juste une suggestion à faire demander aux techniciens de trouver des solutions contre le bruit des pompes à chaleur qui dans ce cadre enchanteur sont parfois dérangeants.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: So just simply one of the best hotels in the world. remarkable attention to detail, remarkable gardens, impeccable training and service in serine understated glamour… perfect

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: We stayed at Nayara Gardens for a week in mid January.  The place is beautiful and the staff was extremely accommodating and helpful.  Every meal was high quality as well as nutritious in a wonderful setting.  We particularly enjoyed the wildlife as we spotted toucan and sloths.  We think they certainly earned their 5 stars!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Hands down one of the finest resorts I have ever stayed at. The setting is incredible and the rooms are without compromise. But even more impressive than that is the level of service provided by the always friendly staff. 10/10

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nous avons été accueillis comme si nous étions des personnes importantes par un personnel aimable et attentif à nos besoins. La végétation luxuriante est époustouflante même sous la pluie à 6:00 avec un naturaliste qui nous fait voir et découvrir animaux et oiseaux tropicaux… . La chambre et le jacuzzi extérieur sont soignés et d’un goût exquis, la nourriture excellente… Deux journées sans quitter ce petit coin de paradis tropical… une expérience inoubliable… Michel

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: O hotel é muito lindo, com bom café da manhã e quarto maravilhoso. Apesar de ter tido alguns contratempos em relação a reservas de atividades, dá para perceber que existe uma preocupação genuína da gestão do hotel para resolver os pequenos problemas que podem surgir. Mais do que um hotel, todo o complexo do Nayara faz parte de um grande projeto social de (re)qualificação da comunidade local. Algo muito bonito e que vale a pena viver!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: This is the best hotel I’ve stayed at. There is nothing to improve on. Thank you so much for such a truly special stay. We will be back one day!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Great! That is the only word that describes my stay here. The staff was attentive and friendly, and the "casitas," very comfortable and inviting. The property is immaculately maintained and beautiful. Just....GREAT!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
I’ve stayed in the Four seasons in Costa Rica and this blew that out of the water. I’ve stayed at some amazing hotels all over and I think this takes the cake as the best resort I have ever stayed at. They just nail everything, the food, the intrinsic rainforest connection, the service, it has so much to do you wouldn’t need to leave the compound but La Fortuna is to beautiful not to. The many pools
And swim up bars. The spa. There’s just not one thing that underwhelmed me. The coffee shop in the morning is 10/10 too.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: One of the best hotels we have ever stayed at. The rooms were beautiful. The service was incredible. The food was delicious. The property and amenities were amazing. We did not want to leave. I would highly recommend staying here if you ever get the chance.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Stayed here recently with my family. Absolutely a pleasure - staff were amazing/attentive, food was delicious, room was clean and well laid out with many Instagram-worthy moments. Babysitter who we used was also fantastic as my daughter asked to see her again the very next day. Would definitely visit again.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Amazing stay with family at Nayara gardens. The service was amazing and the rooms were perfectly curated. Special shout out to Mayank Singh for being attentive and making out stay super comfortable. We will definitely be staying here on our next trip to La Fortuna.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Das Nayara Gardens war das Highlight auf unserer 25 tägigen Costa Rica Rundreise.|Die Anlage ist ein Traum und die Restaurants absolut top. Das Frühstück exzellent. |Die erste Nacht war leider etwas zu laut durch die Poolpumpe welche die ganze Nacht lief, AC vom Nachbar Bungalow und 6 Uhr Morgens die Lastwagen von der Baustelle vom Nachbarsgrundstück. |Wir haben dann ein anderes Bungalow bekommen mit direktem Blick in den Regenwald, weg von der Strasse und keine störende AC vom Nachbargebäude.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
