review_text: From the time we arrived and were given our welcome drink, to the time we left, the hotel team always went above and beyond what was needed. Can’t say enough good things about this hotel, everything was awesome and we can’t wait to come back. Easily one of the best hotels we’ve stayed at.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Es todo espectacular, el hotel, la atencion del personal, la habitación es exactamente como en las fotos.||Volvería una y mil veces más.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Muy buena super atentos buena comida en desayuno lo único comida de almuerzo o cena un poco alto en precios 3 personas no menos de 100 dolares pero muy buen personal y buena atención

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Definitivamente el mejor hotel en donde me he hospedado, no cambiaría en ningún aspecto mi experiencia en el hotel. La atención realmente no la hemos tenido en ningún otro hotel, la comida de todos sus restaurantes deliciosa, los cocteles, todo.|Queremos volver a Costa Rica exclusivamente para volver a hospedarnos allá

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
I’m a wedding photographer in Costa Rica, and Nayara Gardens is one of those places that instantly captures your heart. It’s a beautiful option in La Fortuna for engagement sessions, family photos, and intimate elopements.

The property features two special areas with views of the Arenal Volcano, surrounded by lush forest and gardens that create a very private and peaceful atmosphere. The architecture is stunning, and the hanging bridges are incredibly photogenic, adding a magical touch to every session.

If you’re dreaming of a photo session in an exclusive setting, surrounded by nature and breathtaking scenery, this luxury hotel should definitely be at the top of your list.

Pura vida,
Joice Dahianna

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
Hemos reservado cuatro noches de las cuales ya llevamos dos en Nayara Gardens y solo puedo decir que es espectacular, desde las habitaciones que son exactamente como en las fotos, la comida y el personal que es increíblemente atento y servicial, te hacen sentir como si estuvieras en casa.

El lugar es mágico rodeado de tanta naturaleza, sin duda sería un alojamiento al que volvería una y otra vez.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: El hotel es espectacular! Tiene de todo mas de 5 piscinas, aguas termales y mucha variedad en comida. La atencion al cliente es asombrosa y aue cuidan cada minimo detalle para que uno se sienta a gusto. Todos los colaboradores atienden con la misma calidez y amabilidad.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Mi experiencia en Nayara, en La Fortuna, fue excelente. El hotel está rodeado de naturaleza y se siente una tranquilidad increíble. Las habitaciones son muy cómodas y todo está pensado para que uno realmente descanse. Lo mejor fue la atención del personal, siempre amables y cercanos. Quiero destacar especialmente a Johel Sánchez, que tuvo un trato excepcional durante mi estadía. Sin duda, un lugar al que volvería.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Absolutely incredible resort. Everyone on staff was so kind and knew our names. I am an ambulatory wheelchair user and had no problems throughout our stay. There is one restaurant with stairs but the staff assisted in a respectful way at all times. The food here was amazing, especially the breakfast (SO many options to chose from). Everyone knew of our allergies and preferences and always offered suggestions. It is VERY hilly but it is very easy to call a golf cart to take you anywhere on site. In order to use the golf cart, you will have to be able to transfer in. My wheelchair (folding) fit right on the back of the golf cart. At one point, my wheelchair tires started to go flat and staff filled the up for me without a question. The spa was so relaxing and our massages were just right. We cannot recommend Nayara Gardens enough.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Absolutely amazing experience, with beautiful private plunge pool villa, outstanding service, lush forest environment, geothermal hot springs, wildlife, and great food options.  Would be hard to dream up a more terrific place to stay.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Eine der schönsten Unterkünfte meines Lebens! Einfach nur WOW!!!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: I will like to express my sincere gratitude to Nayara Gardens, since the beginning doing our reservation with Mr. Sebastián Quesada he was amazing and so helpful responding all my questions, to arriving and meeting with Mr. Jafet Vargas at checking he was so respectful, and very welcoming, always there to greet us and answer all our questions, giving the best suggestions, Jafet thanks so much for making our vacation so pleasant our respect and gratitude for you always, I will like to mention Mr. JOSHUA one of the driver's at the golf card,he was so amazing and so honest we give him a tip and we got confused with the currency of colones we were giving him like $40 dollars and he was so honest to let us know that the amount was too much,my respect to you for your honesty thanks so much.  To all the staff in restaurants,housekeeping, pool services everyone of you our gratitude for ever I had never been in my live to a hotel like this that everything is 100% we will definitely come back soon! Thanks so much Nayara gardens for making my 25th Wedding Aniversary so memorable and loving PURA VIDA!!! Costa Rica you stole our hearts 💕

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
Wonderful place and rooms
Staff is amazing and very helpful and cheerful
The rooms have everything included.
Kid friendly

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Top nur zu empfehlen

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
Updated 2025:
We stayed in a villa at Nayara Gardens, just two years after our first trip to the Springs. This time we made sure we stayed longer! Nothing beats Nayara properties. While the Springs, Gardens, and Tented Camp are advertised separately, you can access all three seamlessly. We enjoyed the pools in both the Tented Camp and Springs, and spent a lot of evenings in the numerous hot springs, as they are open until 10pm. The service is perfection- from house keeping, breakfast room service, laundry services, bartenders, servers- everyone strives to provide the best stay for guests, and it shows. It is very WORTH the money and will ruin you for other hotel stays!

Original Review 2023: Nayara Springs exceeded our expectations. The website does not do the property justice. We stayed two nights in a Springs villa and the service was out of this world. I recommend ordering breakfast on your porch. The pools are great (sunscreen is available) and while we didn't know they had numerous hot springs on property, we sure enjoyed using them one evening.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
One of the most beautiful hotels we ever stayed in . Its lush green surroundings is so fascinating and relaxing , with the most courteous and friendly staff .
The rooms , their restaurants, and other amenities everything was too classy and done with a fine taste . It’s just in the middle of a rainforest. So many trees and plants and still so well kept . It was actually impressive how fabulously they are maintaining such a huge property.
We really had the best time , enjoyed their personal jacuzzi pools in the comfort of our own room , with serene surroundings.
It rained while we were there but we still enjoyed their warm hospitality, they took us around their lavish property in the golf carts at just a call .
Also enjoyed their Mediterranean restaurant where we had our little celebration. Also their complimentary breakfast buffet was amazing, with some birds watching while we ate .
It’s also just 15 mins away from the volcano and the main town . Loved our overall stay . And it was so reasonable too . We have stayed at much expensive hotels with far less amenities, so this was really amazing .
I just can’t wait to visit back with the whole family .

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Awesome place and experience like no other. Room, service, food, drinks, facilities, mud massage, people, animals, ambience. Truly magical, even with the nonstop downpours we enjoyed our stay.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Luxury Hotel ,Great Food

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: No es nuestra primera vez,  hemos ido 3 veces a celebrar nuestro aniversario de bodas.  El servicio excelente,   realmente es extraordinario.  Las instalaciones perfectas,  la habitación es perfecta y romántica.  Los restaurantes y bares todos de excelente calidad.||Es una experiencia que hay que tener.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
I would recommend Nayara Gardens a thousand times over. Absolutely amazing stay. My entire trip was incredible, and it was all made possible by the outstanding staff and the resort itself. I don’t have a single complaint — not one. I’ll definitely be back within the next six months.

Special shoutout to John, Johan, and Francisco — they truly went above and beyond to make my stay exceptional. I celebrated my birthday and a few personal milestones while there, and the team made every moment feel special with thoughtful complimentary touches.

Breakfast was included and hands down one of the best I’ve ever had. So many delicious and healthy options — I left the country craving their breakfast.

Even though I didn’t get to meet the property manager, Luis, his leadership clearly shows through the entire team. Every staff member was kind, attentive, and dedicated to making sure guests had a perfect experience.

If you’re planning a trip to Costa Rica, do yourself a favor — go to La Fortuna and stay at Nayara Gardens. Tell them I sent you.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Great stay!!!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: This place is 10000/10. The service is unmatched. I have a Gluten and dairy allergy and they were very accommodating. Everything was so CLEAN. Johnn the front desk staff at Nayara Gardens was the sweetest and most helpful person ever. Keren at the Nayara Tented Camps Lapas bar was one of the highlights of our trip. Along with Christopher and Junior. They were so fun and we were excited to talk to them every day.  The drinks are amazing and well crafted. Even the coffee. nahomi makes the best coffee I have ever had in my entire life. I will have to come back just for the coffee alone. I really just can’t say enough about this place. Clean. Beautiful. Great location. Amazing rooms. FANTASTIC STAFF, they really make you feel like a million bucks. We would gladly stay here any time and will recommend to everyone!!!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Staff were incredibly attentive, friendly and accomodating. They made you feel like family and attention to detail was second to none. From the housekeeping (thank you Darling), reception, bar/restaurant staff and the assistants who drove us around, everyone was so knowledgeable and welcoming. A stunning resort that was peaceful, luxurious and amongst the wildlife of Costa Rica. The daily sloth hunts were a highlight, along with the incredible breakfasts.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Quedamos encantados, lo que ofrecen en las redes sociales lo puedes sentir real, me encantó la limpieza , los alimentos , la atención del personal. Siempre complacen tus peticiones les doy un 10/10

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Best place I’ve ever stayed and my favorite Relais and Chateau so far. Amazing resort. Can’t wait to return. We had a private infinity pool/jacuzzi on our balcony deck (this was so nice after a long day! The room is a huge casita with an indoor and outdoor shower. Room has a couch, coffee station, desk and reading nook. Balcony also has a large futon couch, hammock and table and chairs and looks into the rainforest. The resort has many hot springs, beautiful pools and wonderful restaurants. Food was delicious. Breakfast was included and the fresh coconut was fun. The Italian restaurant was perfect so we did both lunch and dinner there (live music at dinner). Mini bar in the room has drinks and snacks and is complimentary as is the clothing wash service which was welcome at the end of our trip. Resort has lots of beautiful native plants and animals (sloths, giant green tree iguanas, birds and more). You are in the rainforest. We also did a couples volcanic mud massage and highly recommend. Service is perfect. We did a cooking class and the hanging bridge walk off property. Planning my trip back next year.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nayara Gardens is absolutely exceptional! From the moment we arrived, everything was perfect. The rooms were luxurious and spotless, the food was top quality with so much flavor, and the service was beyond amazing—every staff member went above and beyond to make us feel special. The beautiful natural surroundings and incredible amenities made the experience even more magical. Truly one of the best stays we’ve ever had. Highly recommend to anyone looking for a perfect getaway!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
This is possibly one of the best hotels we have stayed at!

The attention to detail was incredible and the rooms were extremely clean and comfortable. This was arguably the highlight of our trip.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
Nayara Gardens is a lush rainforest retreat where luxury and nature meet seamlessly. Villas and casitas are surrounded by tropical gardens, many with private outdoor showers or plunge pools, creating a serene, romantic atmosphere. Guests can explore winding jungle trails, hanging bridges, and gardens alive with birds, monkeys, and sloths.

The resort shares its grounds with Nayara Springs and Nayara Tented Camp, so you have access to multiple hot springs, pools, world-class spas, bars, and a variety of excellent restaurants. It’s the ideal blend of vibrant wildlife, beautiful landscapes, spectacular views of the volcano and five-star comfort.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: This is a five star resort for a reason. The service to the food was all amazing

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nuestra experiencia en Nayara Gardens fue absolutamente increíble. Las habitaciones eran preciosas, impecables y estaban rodeadas de impresionantes vistas al volcán Arenal. El personal superó todas las expectativas: siempre amable, servicial y profesional. Todas las comidas en los restaurantes eran deliciosas y preparadas con esmero.||Este lugar realmente combina lujo con cariño. Una de las mejores experiencias hoteleras que hemos tenido. ¡Muy recomendable!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Magnifique séjour. Tout d'abord il faut souligner la qualité du personnel présent. Tout le monde est aux petits soins dés l'accueil. Nous avons pu séjourner dans une casita magnifique avec un soin du détail impressionnant couplé à des finitions de très haut standing. Un lieu magique pour passer un moment inoubliable dans les différentes piscines, les thermes ou dans les différentes installations presentes. Un grand merci à Raquel qui fut attentionnée tant lors du repas du soir dans le restaurant fusion asiatique que lors du petit déjeuner. C'est un lieu de rêve dont nous nous souviendrons pour toujours.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Mi experiencia fue increíble, la gente de Costa Rica son maravillosas está en mi top 1 de países a viajar. Amé la experiencia, cuidaron de cada detalle.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nayara Gardens is quite possibly the best resort I’ve ever stayed in. The staff were attentive, kind, and genuinely caring, with excellent guest recognition—always proactive and anticipating my needs. The spa was outstanding, offering a truly relaxing experience, and the on-site dining was exceptional. I loved the morning yoga classes and the nature tour, both of which were beautifully run. The facilities throughout the resort were first-class, and my room was wonderful—comfortable beds and a lovely heated pool. I cannot recommend this place highly enough.

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: Nayara Gardens is such a wonderful place to stay when visiting Costa Rica.  Everything about it is five star!  I look forward to staying at Nayara Gardens again on my next trip.  William,  our driver, who took us around to the different areas we wanted to go on the resort, was amazing!  He spotted so many different animals for us to see - including (highlight of our trip) a mama and baby sloth coming down a tree.  And this only happens once a week for them to go to the bathroom.   Priceless!  Thank you so much William for creating such memorable moments for us!

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: El hotel es impresionante , el servicio increíble y la pileta en la habitación con agua del volcán hace de este hotel algo único

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text: This was by far one of the best resorts we have ever stayed. The staff were beyond friendly and helpful, the grounds were meticulously maintained, and the rooms were unbelievable. Highly recommend this resort for couples. The breakfast was phenomenal and the restaurants on site were so so good! We will be back

name: Nayara Gardens Hotel and Spa
review_text:
Absolutely stunning property! The lush, tropical landscaping and winding paths make it feel peaceful and secluded. We stayed in a casita with a private heated pool, beautiful room and a dreamy outdoor shower.

Service was excellent, with thoughtful touches like nightly turndown and desserts. They even surprised us with balloons and champagne for my husband’s birthday!

As adults, we could enjoy the other two resorts’ pools, which was a great perk. The breakfast was included, and they had a wide range of dishes to enjoy, plus fresh coconut! Food was delicious, though a bit pricey, but worth it for the relaxed vibe. We loved every minute!

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Strong waves , keep your little ones near .

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Der beste Nationalpark in Costa Rica! Wir konnten auch ohne Guide viele Tiere sehen und für den Eintritt wird um eine kleine Spende gebeten.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Netter Park mit schönen Stränden. Affen sieht man immer. Wir waren ohne Guide nur zu einem Spaziergang, daher haben wir nicht so viel gesehen.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Super abwechslungsreiche Naturlandschaft! Mega!

Mindestens 3 Stunden zu rechnen!

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
הכניסה עם תרומה שאי אפשר לסרב לה
הג'ונגל מדהים כרגיל
יש שביל לאורך החוף כך שאפשר לטייל לאורך כל חוף בלי לקרוא לזה פארק

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Beautiful

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: De lo mejor de costa rica, fue donde mas animales vi.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Sehr gut begehbar mit kleinen Kindern (Holzsteg).

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Viele Tiere, schöner Park!

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Prachtig park met mooie hike.
Wij namen de hoofdingang en hele hike gelopen.
En terug met taxi naar de huurauto.
Ondanks dat het bijna de hele hike heeft geregend, hebben we genoten.
Geen bijzondere dieren gezien helaas.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: The nature in this park is just incredible, and the park is very well managed with specific rules to protect the environment. Some animals, like monkeys or some birds, can easily be spotted by tourists, but if you wanna see more I recommend a guide which can help you spot them, see them close with binoculars and also take pictures.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Lugar precioso para visitar, comer ir a la playa y ver animales

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Parc magnifique, avec énormément d’animaux si vous venez tôt le matin ! Pas besoin de guide pour en voir beaucoup

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Limpio. Organizado.

Detallazo no reciben tarjeta

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Somewhat disappointed. Didn't see much but at least it's donation based. Nice little walk.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Très bien aménagé.
Seul bémol, les sacs de sable qui partent à l’océan.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
The park is clean, calm, and beautiful.

The donation based entry fee helps maintain the park. You won't find trash on the beach or the trails.

We started at the Cahuita entrance and ended at the Puerto Vargas trail.

The entire loop took three hours with lots of stops to watch wildlife. I was wearing flip-flops and my friend was wearing sneakers. It was a very easy trail for both of us.

The raised boardwalk through the jungle on the last stretch was peaceful.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Jo, paráda. Přesně takhle jsem si představoval národní park na pobřeží Kostariky. Dlouhá, ale dobře upravená stezka vás provede všemi typickými oblastmi pobřeží. Na obrátce na peaku vás pobaví drzé opice a nosáli ve větvích stromu. Dost vody sebou a dostatek času. I na koupání by mohlo dojít.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Kind of touristy but it's free (you need to pay a donation) and there's plenty of wildlife to see. Also good for snorkling (with licensed tour)

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Die Parkwächterin war extrem unfreundlich. Sie hat uns uns andere ohne Grund mit ihrem (privaten?) Handy gefilmt und uns um 14.00h wie Tiere heraus getrieben.  Als wir eine frage stellen wollten, hat sie nur geantwortet, dass wir ihr als Frau gegenüber respektlos sind und sie uns melden wird, wenn wir nicht sofort den Park verlassen. Nichtmal mit einem Hund würde ich so reden, wie sie mit uns. Anderen Touristen erging es genauso.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Umgangston sehr unhöflich. Die Wache hat uns um 14 uhr pünktlich rausgeschmissen, sie filmte uns schon beim herantreten, warf uns vor respektlos zu sein und reagierte keineswegs auf Fragen. Würde nicht noch mal dahingehen

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Lindo recorrido, no muy distinto a la vegetacion de la zona

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: A great park for sloths, but after a kilometer or so you'll have to cross a small river so be prepared.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Simplemente precioso.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Es magia absoluta

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: De lo mejor que ví en Costa Rica

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Un parc national exceptionnel, accessible avec une participation libre, chose assez rare au Costa Rica. La faune y est particulièrement riche : paresseux, singes, ratons laveurs, coatis.
Les plages, quant à elles, sont splendides, eau limpide.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Un parque impecable. Hay mucho que hacer y ver. El horario es limitado, así que llega temprano. La gente es maravillosa y cuida muy bien del parque. Asegúrate de mostrarles tu agradecimiento a ellos y a la Madre Naturaleza, que nos regala semejante obsequio.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Tenemos la suerte de vivir en puerto viejo y poder disfrutar cuando podemos del parque, muy tranquilo y con mucho animales

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Lo mejor del caribe  es imperdible, volvereeeeee

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Belle balade dans la forêt, parmi les insectes et serpents venimeux, les singes capucins, paresseux et un tas d’autres d’animaux et d’insectes. Arrivés sur la plage, on profite d’un très bon bain de mer aux Caraïbes. Petite frustration, la garde du parc vous sort à 15:30. Dommage.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Aucun animaux visibles même avec un guide !
J’ai vu plus d’animaux à la plage de Uva …

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Super mooi park, veel dieren in het wild gezien.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Meilleur parc du Costa Rica. RAS. Entrée sur donation. Et il n’y a pas la foule de touristes de Manuel Antonio

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Lugar precioso, lo recomiendo

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Pretty park! We saw a ton of monkeys and some racoons. Unfortunately a lot of people were trying to touch and mess with the monkeys.

We went mid week so it was easy to find a secluded spot to enjoy the beach!

The park runs on donations. Also, dont let them stop you in the street and pay for parking. There is a free parking lot and free street parking.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Hermosa experiencia en Limón

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Park doet zich voor alsof er veel dieren zijn en je een "vrijwillige donatie" kan geven. Niks van waar, donatie wordt afgedwongen en werknemers geven onjuiste informatie door waardoor meerdere bezoekers niet meer bij hun auto's terug konden komen. Overal liggen ook plastic zakken zand op de stranden terwijl ze doen alsof ze zo tegen natuurvervuiling zijn. Absolute afrader!!

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Doing the whole loop can take 3 hours plus, depending how much time you spend to look for the animals. My time was 3.5 hours. Bring enough water with you as temperatures and humidity can get high. Animals seen here include different monkeys and raccoons. Swimming is allowed and it's better in the national park than at the beaches in Cahuita town. Avoid bringing food as the monkeys WILL steal them.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Très belle ballade avec une faune omniprésente. Dommage que le tarif des guides soit aussi exorbitant

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Amazing nature réserve

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: No esperes ver practicamente nada, si tienes suerte algo podrás ver.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Loved hiking here. Beautiful and serene.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: El parque es increíble y se encuentra muy bien cuidado. Recomiendo ir temprano y aprovechar a recorrer lo más que puedas sin la presencia de extranjeros y locales que no saben apreciar la naturaleza en silencio por lo que es más difícil lograr ver animales con ellos cerca.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: For just $5 you get an amazing jungle and wildlife experience with a great wooden trail!

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Only a small part of the park was visitable.

Not many animals were there.

The trail itself with 2,5 km was okay.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Increíble parque. Sendero espectacular, playas soñadas

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Parque con gran variedad de animales para avistar!
Cuidado con las pertenencias en las playas 🐒

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: We had a fabulous day at the Cahuita National Park! The guides for the snorkelling were really good, and ‘Tony the Tiger’ who led our nature walk was fabulous, finding Mammals, Reptiles, Birds, Arachnids and insects we hadn’t previously seen during our four-week tour of Costa Rica. I particularly loved the Long-Nosed Bats, that we would never have spotted alone. The tour ended with a Two-fingered Sloth and her baby hanging out in the low branches of a tree. What a way to end a day! Absolutely amazing and I’d wholeheartedly recommend a visit if you are on the Caribbean side of the country 👌

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Un Parque Nacional increíble... apto para hacer una buena caminata a lo largo de la playa, desconectarse del día a día y vivir un espacio fuera de la ciudad. *Llevar cámara porque fijo van a ver diferentes animales como los monos cariblancos.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: le daría 10 estrellas si pudiera

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Ett trevlig besök och en bra aktivitet att göra en dag om man tar bussen från Puerto Viejo. Inträde till parken är $5-6 och sedan kan man spendera hur lång tid man vill där. Det finns mycket fina stränder och rikt djurliv, vi såg massor med apor, ödlor, näsbjörn och till och med en krokodil!

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Amazing corner of the world hosting an explosion of wildlife nature!

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
We visited here and was blown away by the incredible wildlife we saw here!

The entrance from Playa Blanca (2 more stops on the bus) doesn’t charge an entry fee but ask you to donate to the park. I recommend donating because they’ve really got something special here and doing well to preserve it!

We didn’t use a guide and saw ALOT of up close wildlife. The guides are very good at spotting smaller wildlife and tell you a lot about the trees/ plants in the park too which is cool if that’s your thing.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Si cammina immersi nella natura, con scimmie, scoiattoli, bradipi e svariati uccelli che appaiono all' improvviso. Un viaggio nella foresta, senza pericoli, accessibile anche ai bambini

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Visitamos el Parque Nacional Cahuita y nos pareció un lugar precioso. Nosotros entramos por Puerto Vargas, donde el aparcamiento no tiene coste y solo se paga la entrada al parque, algo que se agradece.

El recorrido comienza con una pasarela de madera que te lleva hasta la primera playa, haciendo el paseo muy agradable. Durante la visita se pueden ver distintas especies y también hay varios puntos donde es posible bañarse.

Además, existe la opción de hacer snorkel (solo con guía), lo que añade un extra interesante a la experiencia. El parque está muy bien cuidado y el personal en general es amable, lo que hace que la visita sea aún más agradable.

Muy recomendable.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Hermoso parque nacional, la entrada por Puerto Vargas puedes dejar el automovil en parqueo e iniciar la caminata por un hermoso camino en el bosque, la playa es genial
Se observan monos, pizotes y mapaches en el camino

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Es un parque nacional con un sendero espectacular sobre todo si ingresas por Punta Vargas. Los 2.5 km de sendero sobre manglar y "selva" son muy lindos al lado de grandes árboles y animales como perezosos, mapaches, pizotes, monos, mariposas, etc. Caminar más allá de punta Vargas podría no ser tan neceserio sobre todo si estás cerca del mediodía. Las instalaciones están en un estado adecuado y los guías son muy amables. De verdad que se esmeran por hacerte sentir bien y muy seguro.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Wir waren mit einem Guide hier, der uns ganz viel zur Natur und den Tieren erklären konnte. Ohne diesen hätten wir sehr viel nicht gesehen. In dem Nationalpark gibt es eine beeindruckende Artenvielfalt. Bei uns war es leider sehr Überlaufen (Sonntag), weshalb es teilweise sehr stressig war.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: We saw sloths racoons and monkeys

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Wir konnten (vor allem dank Guide) viele Tiere sehen. Insbesondere Faultiere haben wir sehr viele gesehen. Die Strände sind schön.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Der Nationalpark ist schön, aber nichts besonderes. Als erstes ist er kostenpflichtig und Schnorcheln ist nur mit einem Guide erlaubt und kostet 45 $. Sehr enttäuschend. Am Eingang wird der Rucksack kontrolliert und sämtliche Plastiktüte (für Trinken oder Sonnencreme usw.) eingezogen. Weiterhin führt nur ein Weg durch das Innere des Parks, der an den Stränden endet. Die Strände sahen nach relativ starkem Regen in den Tagen zuvor bei weitem nicht karibisch aus und sind auch vergleichsweise schmal.
Gesehen haben wir Affen, Waschbären, Schlangen, Vögel und Schmetterlinge. Dies sieht man aber auch außerhalb des Parks, wenn man die Hauptstraße verlässt.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Lugar maravillos la cuota es voluntaria, son 8 Km de caminata por bosque y playas, vale la pena ir una experiencia inolvidable..

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Gratuito, la voluntad, bien cuidado, la gente justa...perezosos...aves...playa preciosa...

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Mágico, increíble , sanador, maravilloso… no te lo pierdas

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Muy limpio
No sé puede meter comida
Ni alimentar a los animales

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Beautiful and serene.  I iguanas,  monkeys one racoons

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: El parque es muy bonito, y sus playas también.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
Sehr sehr schöner und weitläufiger Nationalpark.
Die Tiere haben sich trotz bestem Wetter leider nicht so eifrig gezeigt.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: Hermoso lugar

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: This is a beautiful park where we really saw a lot. It is not crowded here, making you feel like you are truly in nature. Highly recommended if you are in the area.

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text:
MY FAVORITE PARK in Costa Rica.
Seriously, we donated 5 dólares per person.
We've seen so so so many animals (more than in Manuel Antônio). It's super beautiful place and the beaches are breath-taking

name: Parque Nacional Cahuita
review_text: We really enjoyed our visit to Cahuita National Park. The entrance is based on a voluntary donation, which makes the experience feel welcoming and accessible. Our guide showed us an impressive variety of wildlife, from tiny creatures to larger animals we would have easily missed on our own. The park was pleasantly uncrowded, allowing us to walk the trails in a relaxed way. We also loved the beautiful beach inside the park, which made the whole visit even more memorable.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: The park was okay, but needs maintenance. We didn’t eat not see any macaws, but we did see a ton of poison dart frogs and also some basilisks. For some reason the park hours aren’t posted on Google, but FYI it closes at 4pm. We arrived at 3 and the park staff were on us to hurry up through the park right away.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Leider ist nur eine online Buchung möglich.
Haben es dann fast 20 Min. Versucht uns korrekt zu registrieren.
Leider vergeblich. Hilfe wurde auch nicht wirklich angeboten.
Sehr schade, denn wir wären gerne in den Park gegangen.
So sollte es dann nicht sein.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Eigene wäre der NP richtig schön,vor konten auch viele Tiere beobachten. Leider liegt er so nah an der Straße, dass man zu 90% des Weges Auto bzw Straßen Geräusche gibt. Das stört die Dschungel Atmosphäre sehr

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Great trek looking for birds.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Ame el lugar, Como recomendación ir temprano para aprovechar el espectáculo de ranitas 🐸❤️

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Tres joli parc avec plusieurs circuit plus ou moins longs (de 1km à 4). Les oiseaux sont difficiles à voir si on ne vient pas le matin sachant que le parc ferme à 16h. Mais il est possible de voir pleins d'autres sortes d'animaux (singes, chevreuil, grenouilles, iguanes, etc)

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: So many animals including colorful parrots. Also there’s some huge trees

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Maravilloso lugar para caminar y ver aves

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Es un parque muy cerca del Valle Central, bien planeado se hace la caminata de 5 kms y luego a la playita, el mirador Nuevo de Britt, etc. Es un parque subestimado, pero quien tenga la oportunidad de visitarlo podrá disfrutar de muchas ranas Dendrobates auratus, diversas aves, anfíbios en general, monos, entre otros. El personal muy amable, hay guías y el costo para nacionales es muy accesible, vale la pena visitarlo

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Está bonito para dar una caminata larga y escuchar los ruidos de la naturaleza

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: This park is okay, we saw frogs, deers, a giant ant eater and thats it, the infrastructures is of poor quality, the main bridge over the river is sketchy and one of the railing is heavily corroded and can be a hazard for kids. Its a walk in the forest simply. We saw more birds and Macaws at the beaches. We were expecting more from all the reviews.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Uno de los mejores bosques para observar árboles enormes en un corto recorrido. El sendero universal es una bendición para personas con limitaciones para caminar. Atención del personal y servicios sanitarios muy buenos. Algunas infraestructuras y rótulos en los senderos muy deteriorados, pero volveré. Gracias.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: É um parque pequeno, fácil de percorrer as trilhas em um tempo mais curto. Vimos muitos répteis, para quem gosta é um prato cheio. Alguns pontos da trilha com manutenção falha.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Sistema de ingreso catastrófico

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Nice, must buy tickets online

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Extremely disappointing national park. Infrastructure in poor condition, lot of métal parts anywhere, route along the road so a lot of noise and no animals. Forest not beautiful. 10 USD per person to visit in such conditions is not correct. This Park shouldn’t be anymore on the list.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: I recommend visiting this park if you are staying nearby. It was not busy and had nice trails that were very reasonable for my mom who just got foot surgery. The forest was beautiful and we saw lots of wildlife even without a guide - birds, lizards, a snake, and butterflies. Great for a morning walk only 20-30 minutes from Jaco.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: De los parques nacionales más hermosos, instalaciones muy bonitas, servicios sanitarios bien aseados.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Lugar para caminar y conocer fauna, si no tienes binoculares o contratas un guía difícil!!! Muy abandonado y mal mantenido. Puentes caídos y rotos, senderos clausurados.solo si te sobra tiempo vale la pena ir.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Bonita caminata muy suave, todo el recorrido pueden ser 2 horas a un ritmo suave para disfrutar la naturaleza, en mi visita no ví bastantes animales. El recorrido es un circuito.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: The tropical forest is stunning and worth considering as a quieter alternative to Manuel Antonio, which has the same ecosystem. It could certainly use some investment in infrastructure like bridge repairs, and a more user friendly portal for buying tickets online.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Leider keinen einzigen Ara oder ähnliches gesehen. 2 Leguane und 2 kleinere Vögel. Uhrzeit 12.00. Ich finde der Park ist viel zu nah an der Straße. Weiter drin ist es aber dann herrlich ruhig mit einigen Vogelgezwitscher.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Beautiful NP with well maintained infrastructure. Not very big though, so more of a walk than a hike. More or less highly frequented, so not a huge chance of seeing animals besides birds and howler monkeys bc of the noise.

Don’t bring a plastic bottle, they are not allowed in the NP.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Completely run down park. All ropes rotted away on walking paths. Bridges and lookout points crumbled to the ground. Guides are asking ludicrous prices for a park that should be closed. You will see a few frogs and lizards and the occasional bird. Monkeys are long gone.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Me encantó este lugar

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: One of the smaller and less maintained national parks but offers a,large selection of animals,if you walk quietly and slowly. No guide needed. Macaws are active in the morning.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: We were fairly disappointed with this park compared to all the others we visited in the country. Very limited wildlife sighting and car noises are always there. I would recommend to skip this park and spend one more day in Manuel Antonio, Arenal or Tenorio. No plastic bottles or food are allowed inside.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
We went at 8am and saw plenty of poison dart frogs (13 exactly), tinamou, coati, birds, butterflies and lizards.
The vegetation was also beautiful.
The park was not well maintained however, many observation platforms and bridges were unusable.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: We went in the afternoon, which is a mistake in the heat.  Plan on coming here in the morning.  It is a bit less dense than other parks as the river allows for some airflow.  Trail is divided into 3 sections of loops, so you can hit them all just by only taking lefts.  Watch for snakes in the foliage.  The path is well groomed but there are leaves on either side.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Venimos de España a Costa Rica a conocer el país, todo ha ido super bien hasta llegar a este parque,las entradas hay q comprarlas on line y no nos funcionaba a nosotros y a otras dos parejas de turistas y el señor q estaba ahí nos ha dicho q no podía hacer nada,así que nos hemos tenido q ir xq no nos ha dado opción ni de pagar en efectivo,no tarjeta ni nada de nada, seguro q el parque está muy bien, pero nosotros no hemos podido disfrutarlo.
Espero solucionen este problema para futuros turistas

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Nous y avons vu des toucans, des chauves souris blanches et des basilics.
Nous étions accompagnés d'un guide pour la visite et nous le recommandons fortement. Sans cela il est très compliqué de voir les animaux dans ce parc à la végétation très dense !

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Dense forest with nice walking paths, but unless you go early (7-8am) you won't see any animals except the occasional iguana. Barely any places to sit.   So you probably won't enjoy unless you simply like walking through a dense forest 😆

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
As national parks go in Costa Rica, this one would be pretty low on the list. It is described as a transitonal forest,- between cloud forest and rain forest. The first thing you notice when you get here is the deafening noise of what must be hundreds of thousands of cicadas. You can actually see their cicada pee like rain falling all around you.

There was almost no bird or animal life ..we saw one iguana and literally, two birds. While the plants are interesting, compared to everywhere else in Costa Rica, it was a bore. Plus the idea of having cicada pee falling on my head was yucky.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Überragend die Voelfalt der Tiere, Eintritt 10 Dollar ist im Vergleich zu anderen überteuerten Parks angemessen. Ein Guide bietet sich an, die hören und sehfn einfach mehr. Leider etwas laut durch die Straße und die Bauarbeiten.

Die Beschilderung ist wirklich unklar. Der größte Schwachsinn ist jedoch, dass über die fürchterliche Webseite gebucht werden muss. So etwas schlechtes und umständliches habe ich noch nie gesehen. Meine Kreditkarte wurde abgelehnt, der Mann an der Kasse hat dann für mich gebucht. Hat 20 Minuten gedauert mit Ausweiskontrolle-widerlich.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Be sure to bring water IN A REUSABLE BOTTLE. And a telephoto lens - the animals are often high in the trees.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Como todos los parques nacionales bien cuidados buena atención y limpieza por los baños

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: As with all national parks, you need to book online first! Good trails to follow, though they are, at the furthest point only 100m from the highway, but you still get all the chance to see birds and other wildlife. We were approached by a few unofficial guides. Although they are a bit pricey, I expect they would point out things you wouldn't see yourself. Noise of cicadas is unreal, so bring earplugs if you get affected.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Comme tous les parcs nationaux du Costa Rica, il faut réserver en ligne. La nourriture et les bouteilles d'eau en plastique ne sont pas admises dans les sentiers.

Il y a un vaste stationnement gratuit. Le trajet se fait bien à partir de Jacó en Uber ou taxi. Le retour aussi d'ailleurs.

Il y a des tables à l'ombre pour un pique-nique.

On retrouve des toilettes sur place au chalet d'accueil et à un endroit dans les sentiers.

Les arbres géants sont impressionnants!!! Il y a plusieurs affiches explicatives pour ceux qui aiment apprendre.

Une grande boucle du sentier est considérée universelle où les fauteuils roulants peuvent aisément circuler.

Comme toute activité au Costa Rica, il est recommandé d'y aller tôt pour profiter de la fraîcheur du matin.

On peut y voir beaucoup de faune aussi.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: This park is absolutely wondeful. We had a certified guide, Geohanna, and she was exceptional. She was able to provide to us the opportunity to see spiders, frogs, lizards, birds, monkeys, and plants that we would have never spotted without her expert skills. In addition, she had so much information and insight which really enhanced our experience. Had we not been guided by her, we would never have realized what a treasure Carara Park is. In addition, part of the park is handicap accessible. I would highly recommend this park and encourage you to hire a guide. It will make your experience a huge success!

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Pas top . Nous avons rien observé.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Loved the park. Spent 2.5 hours wandering. Saw a frog, a parrot, many lizards. The cicadas are deafening- if you are sensitive to noise you may want to bring ear plugs. Trails are well marked. Only challenge is booking tour tickets. Must be done online and the UX for the site requires you to sign up first. Cost approx $20USD. There is parking and bathrooms onsite. No food or plastic bottles allowed on the trails.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Il parco purtroppo non è tenuto molto bene. Ci sono due sentieri e uno è fuori dal parcheggio principale quindi devi parcheggiare la macchina bordo strada e probabilmente, a saperlo, riesci ad entrare senza biglietto in quel punto. I sentieri nella zona del parcheggio invece non sono tenuti bene. Consiglio la guida per vedere i pappagalli nel sentiero fuori dal parco mentre se volete avvistare solo le scimmie, potete farcela da soli

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Great park! Arrived at 12pm on a mid Feb day (35°...) and saw 2 3 other ppl in! Despite warnings that animals wont be present unless u go early or at 15:00, we did the full 4.8km loop and saw 2 different poison dart frogs, family of bats, an anteater from very close, a pair of curassows and a monkey. Nature is absolutely fantastic- despite it being just a forest walk they chose the path so that u see some impressive trees! Sometimes ure close to the road, but i respect that, more of the park is for the animals than humans! Combined with the croc bridge its a great experience!

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: La signalisation est manquante, les lieux auraient avantage à être rénovés.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: recomendable si te gusta caminar y la naturaleza

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: This was a super fun park. We enjoyed all the trails and saw lots of wildlife, including toucans, yellow flycatchers, white faced monkeys, iguanas, lots of butterflies and other insects. The trails are easy to hike, so take your time. Also, buy your tickets ahead of time online, the cell service at the park is poor. Thanks Costa Rica for preserving such a beautiful space.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: We got to see a Great Tinamou.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: 2h de balade libre mais pas d'aras. jolie jungle par contre

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Der Eintritt war teuer und hat sich für uns leider nicht gelohnt. In unserem Hotel und auf der Straße haben wir mehr Tiere gesehen

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Es un parque mas que todo para avistar aves o animales pequeños, sin guía difícil sacarle el jugo

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Si te interesa la naturaleza y la observación de pájaros, o la botánica o en si tienes un interés en los ecosistemas tropicales este lugar te va a parecer fascinante, si por el contrario buscas algo llamativo como una catarata o un mirador, puede que este parque te decepcione, tiene varios senderos y si tienes suerte podrás ver algunos animales y varias especies de árboles enormes y muy fotogenicos. El sendero universal le permite a personas con problemas de movilidad hacer parte del recorrido. Te recomiendo ir con un guía naturalista que te ayude a buscar animales y comparta información sobre los distintos ecosistemas. Es buena idea aplicarse repelente para mosquitos y usar pantalones y camisas de manga larga pero frescas, es un lugar muy húmedo y puede ser muy caluroso.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Al parecer es uno de los parques nacionales menos visitados y menos atractivos para los que le gusta la aventura extrema. Senderos cortos y sin pendientes son de nivel muy principiante. El lugar es ideal para ir en familia con niños pequeños o adultos mayores. Aun apto para personas con movilidad reducida ya que uno de los senderos es universal. hay varios senderos y creo que he demorado menos de dos horas con paradas ocasionales. EN lo personal si me gusta la aventura extremas, pero el lugar me ha encantado. Si eres amante del avistamiento de aves, este es tu lugar ideal. Tal y como su nombre lo dice "Carara: rio de cocodrilos", al lugar lo cruza un río que se ve super refrescante. Tuve el deseo de por lo menos meter los pies pero creo que no lo permiten. No se me dijo nada por parte de los guarparques pero si no recomiendo meterse. Podria haber un cocodrilo por alli :). Por ultimo, debes reservar en linea. No cobran el parqueo. Hay guas certificados por si quieres ver aves. No hay servicio de comida. Si o si llevar repelente. Hay un video del lugar en mi canal de YouTube @daroar 🙌

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Il est d'abord très long d'acheter des entrées via le site Sinac ! 10 euros l'entrée.
Le guide coûte 25 $ en plus par personnes.
Nous l'avons fait seul, avons marché plus de 6 km pour voir un agouti, des lézards et un papillon morpho, et une très belle nature.
Au final, nous avons vu plus d'animaux dans le jardin de notre lodge !

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Amazing wildlife, hard birding in such a high and dense canopy. We saw white-faced and spider monkeys. Mot-mots, macaws, antbirds, trogons. The main entrance isn’t as cool as the entrance north on the main road, but still worth checking out. Guides are very helpful! There were some chilling out front for a good price 🙏

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: On a absolument rien vu.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Great park, we saw so much wildlife even without a guide! Of course it's small and close to the road, but it is still a very wild forest -- and I really appreciated all the educational signs along the main trail, including one that explained the negative impacts of the road noise on wildlife. Macaws are present but hard to spot due to the density of the folliage. The trails are well-maintained but still feel nicely rugged. If you willing to be patient and take your time, you will see lots of birds, mammals, and other critters!

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
This little park was one of my favorite stops in Costa Rica. Other tourist-wise, it's very quiet; I was chatting with the park ranger and he mentioned they'd only sold a double-digit number of tickets that day, even in peak high season.

Ignore the people who say they didn't see any wildlife, as those people probably didn't deserve to. We encountered a couple parties who were stomping, racing, and shouting down the trail, scaring off animals not just for them but for everyone. Whenever you encounter such groups, just hang back and let them pass.

To see the animals, you need to go slowly and quietly, and keep listening and scanning for movement. All in all, we spent about 6 hours here--three on each trail. We saw so many birds (that's what we came for!), along with reptiles and monkeys. We encountered a group of capuchins with a couple small juveniles, and it was fun to watch the differing parenting styles among monkey moms. We were super excited to see a great tinamou, a pale-billed woodpecker, various species of parrots (including macaws), a couple trogons, and a lot of beautiful smaller birds--Morelet's seed eaters, barred antshrike, riverside wren, etc.

You can indeed hear road noise at parts, but there are many interpretive signs discussing it. The proximity to the highway is nice in ways; it's easier to access and makes it a convenient stop between destinations. There were paved and unpaved trails, which was nice, as there are still some accessible areas but I also wanted a more rugged feel compared with the highly developed trails at some sites (MA, for example). The entry fee is incredibly reasonable for what you get, just make sure you buy before you go as the internet connection is not great from the park itself.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Nous n'avons pas pris de guide mais je ne sais pas si on aurait vu plus de choses que nous en avons vu, iguanes et colibris à l'entrée du parc, un woodpecker, un capybara, une sorte de dinde et j'ai aperçu un toucan
Personnel agréable

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Seen more wildlife in the parking than in the park

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Lovely, quiet nature reserve.
We visited Fri 2nd Jan. Started our walk at 12:00. Only meat a few other people on the trails. They were all there to enjoy nature so they were all quiet.

It's dry season. It was hot and humid. The trails were dry (no muddy or slippery sections). Most of it was flat. We took 2.5 hrs to walk the full park, stopping lots to look at lizards, bugs, birds and monkeys (we could hear them well but needed the binoculars to see them). We could here macaws but we couldn't see them. We didn't hire a guide.

It took us nearly 4 hours from Monteverde to get there. The 9 km before the crocodile bridge took nearly 1.5 hrs.

There is no cafe at the park. And it's not allowed to take plastic bottles in so being your tin bottles.
There are toilet facilities and drinking water.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Une très belle promenade, facile, avec une végétation luxuriante très belle. On peut voir des animaux si on est très patient.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: I found a python at the start of the trail and also a frog and a moth camouflaged as a leaf.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Un des plus beaux parcs!!! Le bruit de la route par moment est dommage.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Er ist vielleicht nicht der bekannteste Nationalpark, aber sicherlich ein wichtiger Nationalpark, wenn es um Flora und Fauna geht. Er enthält ein noch größeres Gebiet an Regenwald als der nahe gelegene Manuel Antonio Nationalpark und ist bekannt dafür, einige der größten Tierarten Costa Ricas wie das costa-ricanische Krokodil, zu beherbergen.
Dieser Park ist ein weltweit bekanntes ornithologisches Ziel, da er eine sehr vielfältige Vogelwelt aufweist. Aufgrund seiner Lage an der Route 34 ist dieser Park besonders geeignet, im Rahmen einer geführten Tagestour, besucht zu werden.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: We came with an experienced tour guide who showed us all kinds of wildlife that we wouldn't find by ourselves!

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Dumb website won’t accept cash absolute joke not pura vida at all

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Increíble lugar para ver diferentes especies de aves, reptiles, insectos y árboles centenarios.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Kleiner Park mit sehr vielen Tieren. Unbedingt weiter laufen und nicht nur den kleine Rundtour machen!

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Very quiet and calm park, can encounter many animals if you pay attention.
This park is not that commercialized and hustle free.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
⚠️ EXTREMELY SERIOUS INCIDENT – THREAT WITH A FIREARM ⚠️

At 15:00 we entered the park parking area, and at 15:03 we reached the trail entrance. We had purchased our tickets online in advance, and the park was still officially open for at least another hour.

Because of a three-minute difference, a woman staff member denied us entry.

When we calmly began to walk a short distance toward the trail, she started running after us with a GUN in her hand.

We felt directly threatened. A threat involving a firearm in a national/state park, directed at tourists with valid tickets, is completely unacceptable and terrifying.

The staff member did not appear to be properly trained in handling a firearm, and while running, the gun could have fallen from her hand and discharged, potentially causing a tragic accident.

People should not fear for their lives over three minutes. This represents a serious abuse of authority and is something that the responsible authorities must investigate urgently.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Knapp 4 Sterne. Wichtig: Tickets vorher online kaufen! Zur Zeit sind nur die südlichen Wanderwege rechts des Eingangs geöffnet. Die Ausschilderung der Wege ist außer auf dem Lehrpfad eher schlecht. Am einer Stelle steht gar ein falsches neues Schild, zum Glück liegt das alte Holzschild noch darunter.
Die Wege sind lange nicht mehr gepflegt worden, eine Brücke ist eingestürzt und kann den nächsten Teil nur erreichen, wenn das Flussbett trocken ist.
Rote Aras seht ihr höchstens mal über den Bäumen fliegen. Ansonsten das übliche Programm an Vögelchen, Echsen, Leguan und am Wegesrand fanden wir eine Schildkröte.
Kann man mal machen, muss man aber nicht, wenn man schon in anderen Parks war.

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text: Lugar excelente y muy lindo para visitar

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Wij hebben een prachtige route gelopen met onze gids Antonio. Eigenlijk was de trip maar 1,5 uur maar Antonio nam zeer uitgebreid de tijd voor alles waardoor we uiteindelijk 3,5!! uur met hem gelopen hebben. Echt een hele fijne gids die veel over de natuur weet, en ook nog humor heeft. Aan het einde van de tour heeft hij ons nog naar een andere plaats gereden om de ara's van dichtbij te kunnen zien. Dus als je een gids zoekt, vraag naar Antonio.
Het park is overigens ook heel mooi :)

name: Parque Nacional Carara
review_text:
Bellissimo posto per trascorrere qualche ora.
La fauna non si avvista facilmente.
Un po' scomodo pagare i biglietti dovendosi iscrivere sul portale dove internet non prende neanche bene.
In compenso costa solo 10€

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Today we had another early start. The alarm went off at 4:30 a.m. and after a quick breakfast we took the truck to the harbor. From there we headed out to sea on our way to the park.

After a strict bag check — food and certain other items are not allowed — we were allowed to enter the park. Together with a guide, we spent several hours walking through the jungle. It was a great experience, finished off with lunch on the beach.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Great and amazing experience

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: El mejor parque nacional de costa rica , increible la biodiversidad que hay en el parque.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Wow!!! Beautiful and unspoilt!! Great guides, amazing amount of nature and vegetation!!

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Exceptionnel, à faire absolument :)

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Had a wonderful experience with my daughter and granddaughter ...

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: 125 $ ist viel zu teuer und es ist übertrieben dass man nur mit einen Guide rein kann.. Ist leider eine abzocke und schade

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: An amazing and charming nature reserve with a huge variety of animals that are amazing in their beauty and appearance, and a huge variety of birds.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: 10 de 10 el mejor parque nacional

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Muy bonito

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Hermoso con su gran variedad de flora y fauna y su biodiversidad. Super recomendado

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Corcovado es una de las experiencias más agradables y también un poco desafiante por el clima principalmente . Hicimos un hiking con una empresa y fuimos 2 días en la Selva con un guía que conocía la ruta como la palma de su mano . Durante el camino vimos abundantes animales principalmente especies de mono , comimos termitas , observamos al tapir , comimos un pequeño snack a la mitad del
Camino . definitivamente todo una aventura que volvería a repetir . Es un lugar increíble y probablemente lo que más me gustó de Costa Rica. No es una buena opción si solo quieres ir a relajarte y no batallar por que tendrás que hacerlo un poco .

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
My recent visit to Corcovado National Park in Costa Rica left me utterly mesmerized by its breathtaking beauty and astonishing wildlife. As a passionate wildlife enthusiast, I couldn't have asked for a more remarkable experience. From the moment I stepped foot in this tropical paradise, I was immersed in a world teeming with exotic creatures and lush biodiversity.

One of the highlights of my trip was witnessing a tapir gracefully swimming with its adorable baby. It was an awe-inspiring sight, and I felt incredibly fortunate to have encountered these magnificent animals in their natural habitat. The park's commitment to conservation and preservation has allowed these endangered species to thrive, making this encounter even more extraordinary.

The symphony of howling monkeys reverberated through the dense foliage, providing an enchanting soundtrack to my exploration. The sight of spider monkeys leaping effortlessly from branch to branch and playful squirrel monkeys frolicking in the treetops filled my heart with pure joy. The park's vast canopy serves as a sanctuary for these primates, offering a glimpse into their fascinating lives.

Corcovado Park is truly a haven for reptile enthusiasts as well. Spotting various snake species slithering through the undergrowth was an exhilarating experience that tested my knowledge and appreciation for these often misunderstood creatures. Additionally, the opportunity to observe ant eaters, coatis, and agoutis going about their daily routines added a touch of magic to my adventure.

The park's avian inhabitants are equally captivating. Colorful birds flitted among the trees, their vibrant plumage catching the sunlight and painting the landscape with vivid hues. The melodic tunes of countless species filled the air, creating an ethereal ambiance that heightened the sense of wonder at every turn.

One unforgettable encounter was coming face-to-face with a crocodile in its natural habitat. Witnessing this ancient predator gracefully gliding through the water was a humbling experience, a stark reminder of the raw power and beauty of nature.

Beyond the wildlife, Corcovado Park offers stunning landscapes, pristine beaches, and a sense of tranquility that is unparalleled. The lush rainforest and pristine coastline provide endless opportunities for exploration and adventure. The knowledgeable guides and rangers are passionate about their work, sharing their expertise and fostering a deep connection between visitors and the environment.

I highly recommend Corcovado Park to anyone seeking an extraordinary wildlife adventure. It is a place where you can immerse yourself in the beauty of nature, where every step reveals something extraordinary. The park's commitment to conservation and its remarkable biodiversity make it a true gem, deserving of recognition as one of the world's most remarkable natural wonders.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Es uno de los lugares mas increíbles del planeta lo unico malo es que el guardia de seguridad me quito el paquete de cigarrillos como en el instituto.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: What an unforgettable experience to step deep into untouched nature, teeming with amazing wildlife species of all kinds—birds, mammals, reptiles, insects, and more. No matter where you are coming from, it’s certainly a long journey to get here, but I’d say it’s definitely worth it.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: A faire seul ou avec un guide, plutôt à l'envers des groupes et tôt ou après-midi. Ne pas oublier son maillot de bain.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
The monkeys are fast, the sloths are... well, they’re trying their best.

Can’t believe how many animals we saw despite the crowds. Amazing experience!

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Very nice park ! A lot of wildlife, worth a visit!

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Le parc Corcovado est un endroit magique avec sa faune et sa flore!!

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Molto bello, lo consiglio

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Marvin was a great tourist guide with 12 years experience. He was caring and kind. He spotted and showed us the different animals and birds, etc. Through his scope we were able to see them close up and he took beautiful pictures through the scope. The meal was good. The boat rides to and from were very good, the staff attentive and helpful. The walk was easy even though long (I am 62 and did a hike the day before). The group experience was nice. I am glad that I got to experience the park. It costs, especially being a Canadian. With the exchange rate it was about $200. With Marvin and the beautiful pictures he took it was worth it. I would recommend the trip at least once.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Muy bonito recomendado totalmente

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Überwältigender Nationalpark. Mit einem guten Guide sind viele Tiersichtungen möglich. Transfer zum Nationalpark leider nur mit (ziemlich vollbesetzten und zusammeengedrängten) Boot mit Ein- und Ausstieg ohne Pier direkt ins Wasser - gewöhnungsbedürftig und nicht ganz ungefährlich. Im Nationalpark ist Mittagessen möglich (hat gut geschmeckt). Auch für Familien mit Kindern geeignet. Eine unbedingte Empfehlung!

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: A parte la bellezza di ciò che offre questo parco, inspiegabile, le guide José e Eli sono perfette per fare questa esperienza perché trasmettono passione e storia

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Vérifier la route, car il y a une route beaucoup moins accidentée pour se rendre. Prévoyez d’abord de l’argent car il y a un droit d’entrée pour la route en plus des frais du parc que vous devrez payer en colónes.

La randonnée des hot pots est très facile, même si il est mention d’intermédiaire. Vous pouvez y aller facilement avec d’enfants. Nous avons fait le tour en une heure en marchant très lentement. À l’entrée, il nous avait dit que ça allait prendre deux heures.

Les attractions sont biens.

Nous avons seulement fait ce parcours, car celui des chutes était fermé. Selon la personne à l’entrée, le parcours des chutes allait prendre six heures, donc si on se fie à cele des pots, c’était peut-être plus trois heures.

N’oubliez pas de vous inscrire en ligne pour payer vos frais d’entrée sur le site des parcs nationaux.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Absolutely beautiful with stunning flora and fauna!

Since tickets are limited, I highly recommend booking early — and note that visiting requires a guide. We arrived at the Sirena Ranger Station by boat from Puerto Jiménez, which takes about 1.5–2 hours. As there’s no dock, you arrive directly on the beach and walk over the stones to reach the station.

The trails are well marked and relatively easy to walk. We saw an incredible variety of wildlife: many birds and lizards, lots of monkeys, snakes, wild pigs, butterflies, a tapir, several agoutis, and even some fascinating bats.

Halfway through, we had lunch at the ranger station — it was very good and filling, and there was even a vegetarian option available.

I would highly recommend this experience!

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Es la definición de naturaleza salvaje en su estado más puro. Aquí el ser humano es claramente solo un invitado. Ver tapires caminando por la costa o guacamayas rojas volando en parejas es lo habitual. Es una caminata exigente debido al calor extremo y la humedad, pero es el mejor lugar del mundo para ver biodiversidad intensiva en un solo sitio geográfico.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Everyone was super hyped about this place, I don't understand it though. Rules are very strict; you can only follow the path. Yes you see wildlife but I really expected something out of the ordinary. It's quite expensive. 2 days, 1 night is 365$ which is really a lot and I don't think it's worth it.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: פארק פראי מעניין מאוד, לכל חובבי הטבע וגם לאלה שלא. מומלץ להגיע עם הדרכה

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Très décevant !  D'abord l'organisation : bouchon à l'entrée, les bateaux débarquent tous en même temps, au même endroit. On a eu du mal à trouver notre guide et la mise en route a été pénible. Résultat : tous les groupes agglutinés au même endroit,  sur le même sentier, les guides cherchant tous des animaux à montrer. Pourquoi ne pas s'éparpiller sur tous les sentiers ? Avec tout ce monde faisant du bruit forcément, peu d'animaux en vue et notre guide francophone n'avait pas grand chose à dire. On entendait les autres guides donner des informations sur la flore à défaut de faune. Pas le nôtre ! Durée minimale de 2h de visite guidée, peu d'infos, peu d'animaux. Rapport qualité/prix exorbitant ! Bien sûr il y a 1h30 de bateau pour y accéder.... J'ai regretté mes sous !

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Décevant lorsqu'on a déjà visité les parcs de Cahuita, Tortuguero ou Manuel Antonio.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Bohužel už se jedná jen o masovou turistiku. Ráno přijede ve stejný čas asi 400 lidí, chodíte okolo moře po vyšlapané cestě, pořád dokola a několik skupin se střídá u jednoho ptáka, či opice,  katastrofa a vyhozené peníze, je plno lepších parků skoro zdarma kde uvidíte více zvířat. Všechna zvířata z toho blázince již dávno utekla.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Wij hebben een bijzondere jungletour gehad. Ik vermoed dat je ervaring deels afhankelijk is van de gids die je krijgt toegewezen. Wij hadden geluk. Deels is het wilde natuur waar je geen invloed op hebt. Zorg ervoor dat je verwachtingen realistisch zijn. Trek wandelschoenen aan!

In en uit de boot stappen gaat op blote voeten, of neem sandalen mee. Er is geen aanlegsteiger. Hou er ook rekening mee dat plastic flessen en eigen voedsel niet zijn toegestaan. Sommige accomodaties geven je een herbruikbare fles water mee, of schaf vooraf 1 aan.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Der wahrscheinlich schönste Nationalpark in Costa Rica oder zumindest der mit den potentiell meisten Tiersichtungen. Hier unbedingt eine Tour von Puerto Jimenez oder Drake Bay buchen und sich alles zeigen lassen. Ich empfehle Corcovado Wild Tours aus Puerto Jimenez

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Un parc certes joli mais qui ne vaut pas du tout son prix pour y accéder. Ce lieu est le résultat d'un tourisme standardisé où la relation entre touristes et locaux (guides, rangers, personnel...) est purement transactionnelle. Les règles et fonctionnement y sont infantilisant et nous laisse avec une certaine amertume à la fin de la visite. Si vous avez un budget serré, passez cette étape, vous verrez tout autant d'animaux en vous perdant dans le peu d'endroits encore gratuit.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Me parece una estafa que te digan que es público, pero sólo te dejen acceder con guias locales. Deberían decir el precio real que implica desde el principio

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Die einstündige Bootsfahrt weckt Hoffnungen auf Wildnis, doch man landet in einer Massenabfertigung. Lange Schlangen, strenge Kontrollen und Gruppen, die per Walkie-Talkie zu schlafenden Tieren gejagt werden, die dort scheinbar seit Jahrzehnten festgewachsen sind. Man sieht auf jeder Autofahrt durch Costa Rica mehr Wildlife als hier.
​Der kulinarische und hygienische Tiefpunkt: Mittags gibt es Kantinen-Essen in der Massenhalle. Das Schlimmste: Man muss die Toiletten barfuß betreten und durch den Urin der Vorgänger waten. Ein grauenhaftes Erlebnis.
​Mein Tipp für Naturschützer:
Wer den Nationalpark finanziell unterstützen möchte, kann sich gerne eine Eintrittskarte kaufen – aber man sollte sie keinesfalls einlösen. Man ist an jedem anderen Ort in Costa Rica besser aufgehoben und spart sich den Frust.“

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Piękne miejsce,  dotrzeć najlepiej łodzią, codziennie inne trasy po parku,mnóstwo zwierząt, lunch  w cenie biletu,najbardziej dziki park z wielu na jakim byłem na Kostaryke, jednak i tak 200osob wpuszczany na dzień sprawia że jest dość tloczno ,możliwość kilkudniowe wycieczki -w stacji Sirena możliwość spania,zakaz plastiku oraz używania kosmetyków czy muggi na terenie parku ,plecaki sa kontrolowane, lepiej sie spryskaj przed wejściem

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Der Park ist nicht mal einen Stern wert.

Die Boote halten soweit draußen, ich bin beim Aussteigen von einer Welle erfasst worden und komplett untergetaucht, inkl. meiner elektronischen Ausstattung wie Handy, Powerbank etc. und ich war völlig durchnässt den ganzen Tag.

An der Sirena Station angekommen wird wieder (die 2.) Pause eingelegt mit dem Hinweis auf den Souvenirshop.

Irgendwann ging es los, knietiefer Matsch (hatte nur flache Sneakers an, bekam aber bei Sukia (Organisator der Tour) die Info, das sei völlig in Ordnung, musste man sich darauf konzentrieren nicht stecken zu bleiben, d.h. die Augen waren permanent auf den Boden gerichtet gesehen haben wir wenig, weit entfernt ein paar Affen, Spinnen, Vögel und einen schlafenden Tapir (es standen mind. 20 Leute Schlange) das wars.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Guía de naturaleza y amante de la aventura. Muestro Costa Rica a través de sus paisajes, fauna y experiencias auténticas. Cada recorrido es una historia por vivir.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: The world's paradise sanctuary for the wild, what an honour to have experienced this place. The sheer vastness of the landscape, the boat through the mangroves from Sierpe, the Whales along the shore, then the wildlife in the jungle. It just keeps getting better. Some tips: Have a massive meal before you go as no food allowed on the island apart from the ecological centre.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Corcovado was one of our highlights during our visit in Costa Rica. Nature at its best.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Magnifique parc. Que de bons souvenirs !

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Pacheco Tours, Guide Jose:
unsere Gruppe bestand aus 5 Personen, 3 davon deutsch und englisch sprechend, 2 spanisch und englisch sprechend. Der Guide hat sich immer öfter in spanisch mit den 2 spanischen jungen Frauen unterhalten und das dann auch nur teilweise und auf Aufforderung in englisch für die ganze Gruppe wiederholt. Auch mit den Bildern von den Tieren durch das Teleskop wurden die Spanierinnen deutlich bevorzugt. In unserer Gruppe war ein Biologe. Der hat mir gesagt, dass einige Äußerungen des Guides biologisch nicht haltbar waren. Außerdem hat der Guide gesagt, dass die Schreie der Dinos in Jurassic Park aus Schreien von Brüllaffen erzeugt worden seien (ich habs gegoogelt, da steht was anderes) und das Menschen von Außerirdischen abstammen. An Tag 2 habe ich die zweite Wanderung dann schon garnicht mehr mitgemacht weil mir das alles viel zu blöd war und mich die Sichtungen auch nicht vom Hocker gehauen haben.
Zum Corcovado an sich: meine Frau und ich waren vorher schon knapp 2 Wochen in Costa Rica unterwegs und hatten einige geführte Tages- und Nachtwanderungen. Das meiste was der Corcovado uns geboten hat, hatten wir vorher schon gesehen, teilweise auch einfach so ohne Führung, und wir hatten vorher spannendere Sachen gesehen. Meine Erwartungen wurden nicht erfüllt. Spitz ausgedrückt: Man läuft stundenlang durch wadentiefen Matsch um in einem Baumwipfel einen Vogel durch ein Teleskop zu sehen. Und zum Thema Matsch habe ich im Vorfeld auch nicht gelesen, dass man unbedingt hohe Gummistiefel benötigt. Gut, man kann sich dort für 2 Tage welche für 5 $ mieten, das ist ja ok, aber viele haben in hohen Gummistiefeln ohne hohe Socken Probleme und keiner hat hohe Socken dabei. Die kann man sich dort natürlich kaufen...zu überteuerten Preisen. Als ob nicht sowieso schon genug für diesen Trip bezahlt. Wenn man sich die Tageseinnahmen hochrechnet, habe ich nur noch Fragezeichen über dem Kopf.

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text:
Probably the best national park in Costa Rica for spotting wildlife in its natural habitat. Corcovado has several entrances with trails of varying difficulty levels but visitors must book in advance and be accompanied by a certified guide. . The park opens at 7 a.m.
Overnight stays are available at the Sirena and San Pedrillo stations. We had lunch at Sirena — the food was decent, and there's a small shop there (some snacks and souvenirs). Note that bringing your own food or single-use plastic bottles and bags is not allowed.
Wear closed or hiking shoes and quick-drying clothes. Also, keep your wallet/passport etc. protected from rain/humidity (especially if you arrive by boat).

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Ein beeindruckender Nationalpark

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Un parc national préservé en apparence (voir tous les commentaires positifs). Seulement en apparence car beaucoup de déchets plastiques s’amoncellent le long des zones de plages (provenant de l’océan) : pourquoi ne pas proposer aux visiteurs qui seraient volontaires de participer au ramassage au moment des visites ? Il ne faudrait fournir qu’un sac et une pince… pourquoi ne pas lier l’utile à l’agréable ?

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Wunderschöne Natur, hinreißende Optik von Strand und Wald. Dennoch finden wir den hohen Preis für die Führungen und den Hype um den Park übertrieben. Haben die Carate/Leonatour gemacht und keine Schlangen Faultiere, Frösche oder Tapire gesehen. Insgesamt eher wenig Tiere. Positiv: wir sagen einen Ameisenbär, Nasenbären, einen Tukan, drei Affenarten. Rund um Puerto Jimenez kann man so viele Tiere erleben, da muss man nicht in den Park; aber vielleicht sieht man an anderen Tagen oder bei einer Sirenatour mehr...

name: Corcovado National Park
review_text: Not recommend for 2 days package, you have to sleep at the station bed and there are bugs. My legs and fingers got bite by bugs , suffered several days even when I back home.

name: Corcovado National Park
