name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Resort is beautiful, food is great compared to other all inclusive places. Look up Spanglish Tours and book with Herson for a personalized experience outside the resort.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text:
How is this place still open and labeled as luxury? We spent $5000 on Carnival Cruise-level booze cruise atmosphere with inedible food.

We were greeted by a man that was our concierge . He told us to call him on his cell if we needed anything and to meet him here tomorrow to go over things. No number was exchanged. Weird, but okay.We saw champagne at the front but we’re not offered any.
We loaded into a van to check in at the premier desk. In the premier club there was a little bit of food, a small bar with “upscale” drinks and a jar of trail mix. The “high end” bourbon on the car was Jim beam. You weren’t able to get bottle service to the room so I wouldn’t know what the wine list looked like. I was told it was because people party too loud in the room with bottles.
We upgraded to an ocean front room. The room was to include chocolates and rum. Costa Rica is known for their chocolate, but peanut m&ms were the chocolates offered . It was so loud from drunken karaoke the first night that we couldn’t enjoy our beautiful patio and could hear it with all doors closed. After calling the desk to inquire, they said it will be over soon. It went until 10:30.
The next day we had breakfast where we ordered fresh juice twice and it just never came. We also saw the remnants of movie night, a big screen tv set into the back of an SUV with four dining chairs set up to enjoy. They had also set up a Jumbotron and bleacher seating, obstructing the view of the beautiful ocean. Mind you, this is the main area, where all the restaurants and main bar is. There is no escaping the noise of the game while you eat dinner. Romantic luxury it was not.
We then found our concierge, and thought he was going to sign us up for excursions. He asked us how our stay was and we explained that we had noise issues and were very nervous about the noise from the game late tonight. He apologized and then, for 30 minutes, tried to upsell us on a timeshare. He asked that we come back later for lunch to listen to another talk. At this point I was outraged.
They do have a nice spa setup, but cars squeal tires and rev engines outside, not super relaxing for the massage. The quiet side of the resort is lovely to look at, and overall the resort is beautiful. But lunch there was inedible, and I ended up getting food poising from breakfast, so we had to leave less than 24 hours after arriving. Save your money and run somewhere else.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Nice beach

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Es un resort de lujo con todo incluido, con una hermosa playa y aguas tranquilas, ideal para nadar. Ofrece varios restaurantes a la carta, bebidas premium y actividades de entretenimiento durante todo el día. Sin embargo, para un hotel de este nivel, necesita mantenimiento urgente !!!

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Overall great experience. Somethings would need a bit of an upgrade otherwise it was a nice resort. The food could also use some upgrading.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text:
The location was great—very peaceful and relaxing, which is exactly what we were looking for.

However, I think the Secrets brand is a bit below our tastes. It felt like a cruise ship on land. The cocktails were watery, with limited selection even in the preferred club bar. The preferred club is kind of silly. It gave us access to a six stool bar inside a small room by the business center. The cocktails were no better there.

While the food was edible, it was uninspired and lacked authentic Costa Rican flavors, except for breakfast.  Expect food quality along the lines of Red Lobster or Applebees.

The room itself was great, but our ocean view was initially blocked by overgrown vegetation. After we complained, they finally cleared it out on day five. We had critters visit the pool every morning. This did not bother us but some guests might be alarmed.

The property was spread out, a half mile from our room to the main resort. There were no walking paths and the roads were narrow and dangerous to walk on. You needed to call for a van when you wanted to get around the resort.  Inconvenient at best.

We found a groove at the spa pool and spent a great deal of time at our pool room, avoiding the cruise like atmosphere at the main resort.

They tried hard with evening entertainment, but it lacked finesse. Most of the entertainment was phone in stuff.

At checkout they fought us on the $100 house credit we received when we booked it, waving a discount coupon book we needed to use (and spend about $400) to redeem the  agreed to credit. We kept showing the confirm email and after about 15 minutes they relented.

They also pester you constantly to buy into their Hyatt all-inclusive 20 year travel club for about $25,000.  Since Secrets is considered one of their luxury all-inclusive brands I would expect the brands below it in the Hyatt chain to be even more sub standard.

Keep in mind that all inclusive resorts make money by skimping on the things that make a vacation great. If good cocktails and authentic food is what you want, skip the all-inclusive and spend for the best room with the best location.  You will pay for your drinks and food, but they will be much better.  If you want a lot of food and much to drink and don’t care about their quality then this place is perfectly fine.

We had a great time and are not disappointed. We just wouldn’t recommend it to friends or family and if we did we would not call it a 5 star resort. Maybe 3.5.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text:
Worst resort experience we’ve ever had.

This stay at Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica cost approximately $5,000 and was extremely disappointing. It did not meet even basic expectations of an all-inclusive or the Secrets brand.

Major issues:
•	Buffet closed multiple times for private events with no advance notice
•	Only on-site store closed repeatedly during posted hours
•	Everything shuts down early — no nightlife or evening activities
•	Resort is not walkable and internal transportation is slow and unreliable
•	Bartenders often did not know how to make basic drinks and asked guests what was in them
•	Promised birthday surprise never delivered
•	$500 Preferred Club upgrade canceled after lounge closed early and benefits were minimal
•	$299 couples massage lacked basic technique and professionalism

The only positive was room service — timely and friendly staff.

We escalated to Hyatt while still on property and received no response from resort management before checkout. This experience does not reflect the Secrets or Hyatt brand at all.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Beautiful resort with two pools, one quiet.  Staff is all very friendly and helpful. Yeilyn at the concierge desk was exceptionally kind and conscientious.The food is good. Some rooms are reached after a hilly walk. Our room did not have adequate lights. Overall an excellent experience.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: We had a great girls trip to Secrets. The food and drinks were excellent. What made it an even better time was the staff. Merari and her young staff were great, along with Christian. I don’t think the weather could’ve been better either, 90 degree sunny days with no rain for 6 days! Beautiful country and resort.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text:
Stayed in the hotel with “preferred” status. But honestly it was a battle - with sunbeds, pool floats, and especially cabanas!

I’ll explain:
- Sunbeds are generally “reserved” before breakfast (6am), but often unused for the majority of the day - and staff won’t move guests stuff (which I get to an extent)
- The free pool floats are taken by guests and kept for themselves next to the sun lounger but unused. Earlier I asked a float, but they were all taken and only 1 person in the pool!
- Lastly and worst, we were there for 3 nights and with their policy (need to reserve a cabana the day prior at 7am), we only had 1 opportunity to use a cabana for a full day, and they were all reserved by 07:10, we were put on a waiting list and were told; if they are unused by 11am, they are then free again. Having spoke to the preferred desk at 10:55, who enforced the policy, then Pool Services at 11:00, who said the very unused cabana (no.2) was being used. Even with us all looking at the empty cabana, not even any personal items were there! We later found out it was possible to book a cabana more than a day in advance - likely with a little bit of cash incentive.

To caveat, the staff try to be really friendly - but our frustration was that they don’t regulate the sun-beds like most “good” hotels and don’t enforce their own policy.

In addition to that, our suite was far from perfect - definitely doesn’t live to up the expectation of a Suite in a 5 star hotel and associated cost of it - it wasn’t even fully clean, we had issues with the noise of the fridge, the shower had a leak, housekeeping broke the sink plug and didn’t fix it and there was a leak in the toilet. But the suites are well located, which also has a downside - the noise from the nightly events, the bass genuinely make the room vibrate - we were in 104, which is one of the best rooms due to the ocean view!

We also booked a Dining with the Stars experience - which was horribly oversold! Thought it romantic way to end our honeymoon - but ended up being average food (the Japanese and Italian actually had better food), not under the stars, not on the beach - my recommendation would be to save the $175 and give it a miss!

Can see this becoming an American, spring break type of place in the coming years and not so much the luxury experience it’s sold to be. Even during our stay, there was a group of 60 Americans who significantly impacted our experience negatively! But we did meet some other lovely couples and small groups - which really saved this leg of our trip!

Hope this helps, my final piece of advice - Even in the Suite; I got more Premier Inn vibes, than Suite in a luxury 5 star hotel (for the non-brits reading this - Premier Inn is a back to basic 3 star hotel), but still at 5 star prices, avoid unless you love a 24/7 party, but don’t bother with Preferred or a Suite!

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: We had such a great stay at Secrets. My husband and I came for our honeymoon and I can’t say enough about the staff. Christian at Seaside Grill served us multiple times and he always had a smile on his face and treated us so kindly. (We never had a bad meal at ANY of the restaurants! Their food is amazing! Edward, Anderson, and Merari were all so fun with the activities and got us to participate in multiple different games. We had such a great experience and would come back a million times!

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text:
FRAUD WARNING: Credit Card skimming at Amstar office at the resort

I am writing this as a warning to all guests at Secrets Papagayo. On January 15th, I used my physical card at the Amstar desk to pay for a $315 tour. On January 19th, conveniently right after I returned home from Costa Rica, my card was hit with fraudulent charges at Walmart.com.

I used encrypted "Tap to Pay" (Apple Pay) for every other transaction during my trip. This was the ONLY time I swiped my physical card, and it led directly to fraud.

The interaction with the agent was a massive red flag:

After the first swipe for the charges for the tour that we booked, he claimed his screen was blank and it "failed." I showed him my bank app on my phone which clearly displayed a $315 "Pending" charge. He argued with me, insisting that "pending doesn't mean it went through, since you didn't get a notification" to force me into a second swipe. As someone who develops software for a living, it was clear to me that he is lying since a notification of transaction is a nice to have but in app activity is the source of truth.

The rep suggested to swipe again and worst case is a second charge which he can always return if that happens. For the second swipe, he manually entered $630 into his machine. When I challenged the amount, he gave a vague answer about "testing the system." After the swipe, his machine suddenly displayed $315 as successful and my app didn't show a second charge. I assumed it was indeed a glitch due to high trust on agents sitting at the resort. I believe that first swipe was the real machine and second made up swipe was a skimmer that picked up the information. Nothing explains that entry of $630 for the second swipe but I guess that doesn't matter if that's not a legit swipe to begin with.

It is clear the "system failure" was a social engineering tactic to capture my magnetic stripe data. Do not let them swipe your card. Stick to room charges or contactless tap-to-pay only.

Be warned—they also offer a significantly lower price if you pay in cash. Do not do this. This is likely an off-books scam where your tour isn't officially registered, and it's a huge red flag for the integrity of the operation.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: I cannot overstate this enough, DO NOT GO HERE. This was the worst resort experience we have ever had. We have visited many different Secrets resorts, and have had absolutely wonderful times and fantastic service. This resort could not be more of the opposite. The number 1 issue with this resort is the transport situation. This resort is odd, in that it is fairly small, but very spread out, so you have to rely on their transport system to be taken from your room to the main pool area and restaurants. We routinely would have to wait 15-20 minutes to be picked up, and estimated that almost 5 hours of our time during the week was just spent waiting for transport. Next, the service is very subpar. One of our favorite aspects of other Secret resorts is the poolside service and friendly staff. In a week’s time, I think we were approached by pool side service 3 times total, and all 3 times they never ended up bringing us our order. For the restaurants, especially for lunch, the average wait time for food was over 45 minutes. Which is a double issue, because the staff will actually take away your seats at the pool if they see you gone for more than 30 minutes. While this did not happen directly to us, a couple next to us had their stuff taken by an employee while at lunch, their seats given to someone else, and then the employee lost their stuff. After that, that couple had to spend the rest of their vacation going to lunch in shifts so that 1 person could stay at all times with their stuff at the pool. And speaking of the pool, there is a significant issue with the number of chairs available, and I’d guess on average there was about 30% of people who could not find anywhere to sit, and just had to lay their stuff against a wall while accessing the pool. The beach that the resort has access to isn’t actually part of the resort, it is just a public beach, with kids and families. So you could be sitting enjoying yourself at the pool, and 30 yards away are kids screaming playing on the beach. And there was little to no beach chairs to be able to sit down there and relax, because as stated, the beach was just a public beach, and not part of the resort. The restaurant selection is very limited compared to other Secrets. While the food was good, if you did not get there immediately when things opened, you likely would have to wait an hour or so for a seat, since there was only 2 main restaurants. They also were very inconsistent with their dress code rules. I have no issue with the rules, but there are sometimes they would enforce them, and sometimes they wouldn’t. I routinely saw men wearing cut off shirts at breakfast, so one day I wore a cut off shirt thinking it was fine, and they would not let me in. So I literally wore my wife’s swimsuit cover up and apparently that was better, while they let her go in with just her bikini. It made no sense. The rooms are definitely smaller than any other Secrets we have been too, and our little AC unit made his clicking noise all night that made it hard to sleep. To top it all off, the 2nd day we were there, my wife developed a bacterial infection on her foot from the pool, and within 12 hours went from absolutely nothing to a severe infection that needed to have a doctor come to our room to drain her foot, and spiked a 103.6 fever. It got so severe that we actually had to go to the emergency room at the hospital, as they were worried it was starting to be systemic and cause severe issues. For the next 5 days while she was unable to basically do anything at the resort due to this, not a single time did a single Secrets staff member check up on her.. The manager that was present when her foot was being drained even seemed to go out of her way to avoid us whenever we would be near her. I do want to highlight the probably lone bright spot of our trip, and that was the entertainment director, Merari. She was fantastic, and was the single person who seemed to go out of her way to make sure our stay at the resort was as good as possible.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: This is a stunning resort in a great location, and the staff were genuinely friendly and helpful. We loved the atmosphere and setting. While the hotel itself is beautiful, the food fell short of expectations and could definitely be improved.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text:
Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica es un lugar verdaderamente especial. Su mayor encanto está en su entorno: una playa tranquila rodeada de montañas, vegetación exuberante y fauna local que te hace sentir completamente desconectado del mundo. Es el tipo de destino donde la naturaleza se vive y se respira en cada rincón.

Los cuartos son bungalós pequeños pero muy acogedores, impecablemente limpios y pensados para el descanso total. Todo el complejo transmite calma, privacidad y una sensación constante de bienestar.

El servicio del personal es simplemente inolvidable. En la parte de actividades, Belén destaca por su acompañamiento cercano y siempre cordial, invitándonos a participar y disfrutar de las distintas experiencias dentro del complejo con una actitud cálida y entusiasta.

Asimismo, queremos resaltar la hospitalidad de Adriana, nuestra concierge, una persona sumamente amable, siempre sonriente y atenta. Agradecemos profundamente todas sus atenciones, ya que hizo que nuestra estancia fuera aún más cómoda y especial, cuidando cada detalle con una calidad humana excepcional.

La oferta gastronómica es excelente: el hotel cuenta con un restaurante de comida italiana, uno japonés y un buffet muy completo. Además, en una segunda zona del hotel hay otro restaurante donde puedes encontrar diferentes opciones de desayunos, comidas y brunches, todos muy bien preparados y con sabores memorables.

Es un lugar muy tranquilo, ideal para descansar, relajarse y desconectarse por completo. Sin duda, un destino excepcional que combina naturaleza, buen servicio y experiencias memorables. Un lugar al que definitivamente dan ganas de regresar.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Nice quiet relaxing place. 2 separate pool areas. One with a DJ and entertainment and one with silence. This allows guest to choose their level of fun. Food was great, the customer service was outstanding.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: A great resort. Super friendly staff. Several good restaurants to try and 2 different pools - 1 very busy, fun & loud, the other great for swimming & very peaceful

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Excelente servicio, nos hicieron sentir muy cómodos, ambiente relajado y de mucho disfrute. Instalaciones hermosas y la playa genial.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Overselling specialty wine and events. We were preferred package and had to go to one small bar. It was great but… Himitsu was great. Great staff and food. Others, Portifinio seaside etc could have been better. Did one dinner buffet and the steak was leather. Be careful where they put you. We were on the back area and so far away we asked to be moved.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Secrets Papagayo propriedade maravilhosa-lindos quartos e praia.Areas comuns e restaurantes deixam a desejar.Equipe super cordial

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: We spent the holidays at Secrets Papagayo (Christmas and New Year’s). Everything was amazing. The entertainment by the pool during the day was fun and energetic, and the evening shows were spectacular. The resort went above and beyond for Christmas and the fireworks on New Year’s Eve were wonderful (over 12 minutes of beauty in the sky). We loved the food and the staff. Can’t wait to return! Thank you for a wonderful holiday.

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: Excelente servicio, Ingrid tiene unas manos increíbles,  recomendado 100% el masaje dura lo que prometen, la terapeuta muy profesional, la música a buen volumen aunque me hubiera gustado que tengan la opción de elegir la música. Trabajó el cuerpo completo y en específico las zonas que pedí. Volvería sin pensarlo

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: This was a fun hotel in a beautiful location, that offers great entertainment and very friendly staff! It was a bit too expensive and we didn’t have a great dining experience for the one night we were there (the sushi restaurant), but overall it’s a good spot for families and those wanting all-inclusive convenience.  Beautiful area!

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text: La playa es pública .hay que pasar por el hotel .la chica de la entrada donde indica la información para los no buesped fue muy amable con nosotros anoche e incluso el chico de la moto nos indico el camino hasta la playa muy amable ..solo revista tu información personal y recibe un brazalete...llevamos a las 5:20 ingresamos y salimos a la 7:30 pm .no hay inconveniente para entrar ..30/12/25  no puedo dar la información del hotel en si ..porque solo fuimos a la playa..

name: Secrets Papagayo Costa Rica
review_text:
My husband and I recently spent three nights here during our honeymoon. Overall, the property is beautiful but everything is really concentrated in one central hub that makes it feel much smaller than other Secrets resorts we’ve been to. Given we were on our honeymoon, we appreciated the free champagne but the other free amenity they offered was a romantic breakfast in bed which was just normal room service.

The pros - as mentioned the location is stunning. The beach is beautiful with the rocks adding to the views and the black sand beach is easy to get to from the hotel beach. The resort offers a wide variety of free activities, we enjoyed kayaking while we were there. The service was great - we were there over the holidays and everyone was so friendly and kind. Esteban and Alonso were the best bartenders and Erick was our favorite server at the Seaside grille. The food and drinks were solid. Nothing to write home about.

For Christmas Eve, they did a big holiday show but you had to pay $200 extra for a special dinner that gave you front room access. They didn’t communicate what was going on that night well- only the buffet and Italian restaurant was open if you didn’t do the upgraded dinner. Thankfully we went to dinner early but many people were frustrated with how long the wait was for dinner given everything was closed. Would recommend they do a better job of communicating what the options are for holidays if they are going to close most of the options and cater mostly to those that choose to upgrade.

Cons - when we first arrived, our room wasn’t ready and they just left our bags in the open for hours. It felt a little unsafe like someone could just walk up and grab them. The main pool is very activity heavy with music and dancing and the other was just far enough away that we stayed at the one. It got very repetitive by day 3. Also the shuttles didn’t seem that frequent so we just walked everywhere. The room was nicely done but the shower did not drain well. Standing water even over night.

If you’re a beach person, beware that there are people selling activities and goods on the beach.

All in all, we have high standards and enjoyed our stay but it wouldn’t be the first secrets I would recommend to someone.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Me pregunto cuántos indígenas de Costa Rica pueden pagar una comida acá ☺️

Cuántos habitantes de los pueblos originarios lo pueden pagar?

Esto no suena a rescatar, esto es lucrar con una población vulnerable

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
套餐是$150
食物很鹹 風味組合雖然有故事但搭在一起也不好吃 過度調味 完全吃不到食物的味道
從來沒吃的這麼痛苦過 後面都只希望菜趕快上一上 完全沒有期待的感覺
甜點還不錯 一開始的生蠔也還可以
服務很好
酒/無酒精調飲也很普通 整體吃下來很失望
環境很吵

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Best meal I’ve had in a restaurant in CR!

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: great dinner . delicious 😋 chicken empanadas and res al carbón , Delicious cocktails and Sergio the server amazing. a very special night

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: The food was spectacular - new and familiar flavors. One of the best cocktails I’ve ever had. How special to get a taste of local, indigenous focused cuisine.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Had a fantastic meal while visiting San Jose for the first time. I tried several starters (the tortellini being my favorite) and the arroz con palmito - all of which were delicious and unique. The staff was welcoming, warm and friendly. I had a wonderful night and highly recommend.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Les saveurs des plats typiques costariciennes sont revisitées de façon gastronomique.  Tout étaient délicieux.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Uma experiência gastronômica inesquecível! Recomendo fortemente.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: What a delightful meal! Eric, my server, spoke fluent English and was able to explain the intricacies of the largely indigenous, and entirely local, ingredients that went into the tasting menu. Left feeling well cared for, entertained, and well fed! As a solo woman traveler, I pay a lot of attention to how places extend hospitality to me. This was an outstanding meal and service.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: This restaurant is ranked 43 on Top 50 Latin restaurants and it doesn't disappoint. They have an interesting way of pairing food together to create unique flavors where on paper they shouldn't go together but in reality, they're not bad. I had the empanadas for appetizers and they were delicious. I had the matambre with the licorice purée, in which I enjoyed the pork but did not like the purée. Then for dessert, I had the Buñuelos and that was soooo good. Overall, worth the visit!

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: el mejor restaurante de costa rica. único

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Gran experiencia! Recomendado

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Overrated!
We order several dishes and most of them were awful. A truly disappointing and disgusting experience. Don’t waste your money here!

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
this restaurant is a must try for Latin American food with a twist, their ingredients are top tier and the variations they have for vegetarians were really good for a couple of the people in the grouo. the service was superb, I visited with people from my team in states,  and our server was really attentive and spoke really fluent English which made the night  very easy for them , since he was able to explain the dishes perfectly.

we really like the matambre, montaditos de atún and their gazpacho, which is made of pejibaye in this case, their cocktails were good, I personally recommend the tomillo, and the dessert I would go with the miel de ayote , it was spectacular.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Been perhaps half of the 3 michelin starred restaurants in the world, comfortable saying this is one of the best restaurants i have ever been

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Visité el lugar un martes y no me gustó la experiencia. No hay parqueo disponible. La calidad de la comida que consumí no es acorde al precio que pagué por una cena para dos personas. Uno de los servicios sanitarios está fuera de servicio y pues para lo que cobran por un platillo y una botella de vino, bien les alcanzaría para tener dicho sanitario en buen estado. No me gustó la atención del mesero, estaba en otra cosa, hablando con los demás compañeros y a la salida tuve que esperar que me abrieran la puerta para retirarme.  No quedé satisfecho.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Comida espetacular. Como entrada de atum e tutano. Ambos maravilhososo. Porções boas e muito saborosas. De prato   principal comemos Assado Carbón com puré de feijão branco e cacao. Ótimo. Macio, saboroso.
Sérgio nos atendeu muito bem, com boas sugestões de comida e vinho.
Jantar muito bom com minha esposa.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
El sabor y presentación de la comida: 10/10, se nota que el chef es realmente un profesional de alto nivel.

Pagamos 61.000 colones por dos platos fuertes, 2 bebidas no alcohólicas, y dos postres.

El restaurante es bonito, pero bastante ruidoso.

La atención es buena, pese a que fuimos en San Valentín, considero que el tiempo de espera fue razonable.

Sin duda volvería para probar otros platillos.

Felicitaciones al chef 👌🏾

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Es la segunda vez que tenemos la suerte de visitar este hermoso restaurante.
Lugar íntimo, pensado en cada detalle. La comida y bebidas son excepcionales(lo mejor que tuvimos en costa rica), y la narrativa e investigacion de cada insumos y proceso, hacen de la experiencia aun mas gratificante.
Hazte un favor y visita Sikwa.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Dined at some of the best restaurants in the world and without any doubt, this is in my top 5. Every single bite of every single dish was a revelation, the whole experience was thoughtful and emotional, and it all felt like a great value. Do not miss this place- heck, we seriously discussed flying back to CR just to eat here again!

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Le falta sal.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: This restaurant was my top experience in Costa Rica, I absolutely loved the food. The tortellini will remain in my memory forever, and I loved my fish curry. The tastes of the bunelos were interesting and distinct, but this they did not appeal to my palate. You would be lucky to get a table here, you will suffer other hassles in San Jose, but Sikwa will make it worth it.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Muy lindo el lugar, buen servicio y muy buena la comida.
Nos gustó mucho los platillos, sólo en el matambre nos pareció que el sabor del puré de castaña opacaba mucho el matambre y el kimchi, pero individualmente el matambre y el kimchi sabían muy bien. Las bebidas también estaban muy buenas y en general nos gustó mucho la experiencia. Nos dieron unas trufas de cortesía por celebración de cumpleaños y están deliciosas.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Nous avons choisi ce restaurant pour terminer notre merveilleux trip de 2 semaines au Costa Rica... et waaaaw! Nous avons adoré les différents mélanges de saveurs, les mélanges parfois osés mais tellement délicieux !  Et cerise sur le gâteau, Sergio a bien pris son temps pour nous expliquer tous les mets et l'historique du pays. On ne pouvait terminer mieux notre voyage. Merci ! Pura vida 🥰

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Excellente expérience. Les plats étaient vraiment très bons, avec des goûts équilibrés et des associations à la fois originales et audacieuses. Le service s’est montré professionnel et attentionné tout au long du repas. La décoration est particulièrement jolie et l’ambiance musicale festive.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Une expérience extrêmement décevante, indigne du standing et de la réputation affichée.
Nous avons dîné dans ce restaurant le 24 décembre à 20h30, et malheureusement, tout au long du repas, l’expérience n’a cessé de se dégrader. Le service a été catastrophique : lent, désorganisé et sans aucune initiative. C’était presque comique, on a cru à une caméra cachée, et heureusement que nous sommes patients et de bonne composition.
Dès le début, une grande partie de la carte était indisponible mais personne ne nous a rien dit. Sur un menu déjà restreint, aucun plat de poisson n’était proposé, ce qui est pour le moins surprenant pour un établissement se voulant gastronomique. Nous avons également essuyé un refus concernant les tortellinis, sous prétexte que leur préparation prenait trop de temps… pour qu’ils nous soient finalement servis plus tard. Une incohérence totale, révélatrice d’un sérieux manque de coordination en cuisine.
Le plus choquant reste le fait que ce soit nous, clients, qui ayons dû suggérer au manager de nous proposer un plat à base de thon, alors même que du thon était disponible. Aucune force de proposition, aucune anticipation, aucune volonté de trouver des solutions adaptées.
Tout au long du dîner, nous avons été pris en charge par au moins quatre serveurs différents, à qui nous devions nous expliquer sans cesse.
Comme si cela ne suffisait pas, on a ensuite tenté de nous facturer un supplément pour le thon, alors que nous avons dû nous adapter et renoncer à certains plats initialement choisis, uniquement à cause du manque de prévision et d’organisation du restaurant. Faire payer un extra dans ces conditions est tout simplement inacceptable.
Les prix sont extrêmement élevés au regard de la prestation proposée. Niveau goût, nous avons apprécié les plats que nous avons pu obtenir mais rien ne justifie ni les tarifs pratiqués ni la réputation dont se prévaut l’établissement. On est clairement tombés dans le panneau du label « 50 best restaurants 2024 » donc on espère que ce commentaire vous évitera de faire la même erreur !
Passez votre chemin !

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
The food was very tasty and full of flavor, and the cocktails were also good. Highlight was the starter with tuna. The service is friendly and welcoming but still has some room for improvement, as it felt a bit chaotic at times.

When the restaurant is full, the noise level is unfortunately quite high and some sound-dampening would really help. Additionally, the air conditioning was a bit too cold for my liking.

Overall, it is definitely one of the very good restaurants in San José and worth a visit despite these small drawbacks.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
I am leaving this review after visiting the restaurant 5 times in the last 3 years - and unfortunately I’ve seen the quality slowly declining.
I used to love this restaurant and I insisted on bringing my family and friends visiting me in San José every time. In general for me the entrances have always been better than the main dishes, but I have always appreciated the creativity and uniqueness of the menu and the ingredients. Service is always impeccable, so I have nothing bad to say there.
However our last visit was marked by a massive disappointment. First by the atmosphere - since they changed the restaurant area and left the better part of the space for La Cantina, the restaurant is small, crammed, extremely noisy and I was feeling totally detached by the person I was sharing the dinner with - the windows were closed which also made it very unpleasant.
And second my companion couldn’t finish their main because part of it was extremely greasy and another part of it extremely salty.
I am really sorry to have to leave this review, I had to make these points in case you would like to improve your clients’ experience.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: The food was good, the service was bad. On a 10 item menu 4 plates were unavailable, we waited about an 40 minutes to get our entreés and another 40 for our main dishes. We got served the wrong drinks. Too expensive for the overall experience

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: El chef es un maestro, el uso de ingredientes tradicionales de Costa Rica es creativo y delicioso. En particular el plato fuerte Matambre y el postre Miel de Ayote estaba exquisitos.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Not as good as they think ( or hope) it is. Menú is predictable and not that original, cheap ingredients and service is just hectic (although they are fairly polite). Not a good value for the money. Expensive

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: El lugar esta bien, la atención es buena y la comida sabe bien, pero para estar entre el top 50 de Latinoamérica esperaba mucho más del restaurante, por lo general dan cortesías, explican la historia de los platos y atienden diferente, por lo que me decepcionó un poco y los precios son bastante altos para la experiencia real.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
La experiencia estuvo mal gestionada, no sé si por haber asistido un martes. Hubo una espera de casi una hora entre la entrada y el plato fuerte. El ambiente no resulta cálido en ningún sentido: la comida se enfrió con mucha rapidez, y da la impresión de que tanto el emplatado como la ambientación no favorecen que los platos se mantengan calientes o, al menos, tibios durante el consumo.

Además, pedí una copa de vino en tres ocasiones; finalmente llegó demasiado tarde para acompañar la entrada y demasiado pronto para el plato fuerte, lo que afectó aún más la experiencia.

Para cerrar la cena, solicité un café y me indicaron que no contaban con bebidas calientes. Un restaurante de esta categoría no debería carecer de una de las bebidas más emblemáticas de Costa Rica para acompañar la sobremesa después de la cena.

La comida estuvo bien; en términos generales, considero que la propuesta es muy interesante, pero está claramente mal gestionada.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
L’esperienza da Sikwa é notevole. Il lavoro che viene fatto qua dentro é di ricostruzione della memoria, prima che tecnico e di così detto ‘fine dining’. Cominciamo con il posto, che é bello ma senza sfarzi e lustri da poser. La cura sta nei piatti, senza sottovalutarne la presentazione (i piatti usati per le diverse portate sono bellissimi). Il servizio, in spagnolo, é super esplicativo, attento, e nel mio caso anche veloce perché il posto non era pieno (il cameriere che mi ha servito mi ha detto che di aver  ricevuto una cancellazione importante all’ultimo minuto). Ho optato per il menù degustazione (150$), con 6 portate più il pane, il pre dessert e il dessert. É un viaggio, in sapori a me, per una buona parte, sconosciuti, ma sempre ottimamente presentati e bilanciati. Mi fanno un po’ sorridere i commenti letti prima di arrivare di apprezzamento altalenante ai piatti: anche io ho vissuto un’altalena, alcuni piatti mi sono piaciuti meno di alti, ma non solo é normale, in questo posto, che ti racconte le storie di ingredienti locali e pratiche ancestrali lo straniamento che un piatto può dare é assolutamente benvenuto! Quindi, mio consiglio, godetevela, perché il viaggio é bello!!!

NB: l’accompagnamento con i vini é un po’ costoso (ma siamo in un paese che vino non ne produce). Alternativamente ai 75$ potete scegliere 2-3 cocktails, che sicuramente onorano la cena.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Le he dado 2 oportunidades pensando que tal vez fue mala suerte o un mal día. Pero definitivamente no veo el valor de su menú.
La primera vez que fui hace un tiempo pedí un pescado al vapor en hoja de plátano y estaba seriamente desabrido y fue decepcionante. Pensé que tal vez me equivoqué en mi elección.
La siguiente vez fui ya con el hype de su 50 Best y mejor restaurante de Costa Rica.
Pedí 2 entradas y un plato fuerte, pagué alrededor de 42000 Colones (84$) con un descuento.
Las entradas tartar de camarón que eran 3-4 camarones pequeños y una croqueta de papa.
Mini empanadas de gallina achiotada
En general bien, pero sumamente pequeños, especialmente por el precio alrededor de 20-25$ cada uno.
Plato principal vacío al carbón con mole de cacao, este plato tenía potencial pero la carne llegó tibia y el mole sabía solamente a clavo de olor y opacaba todo el sabor, estaba debajo de la carne así que era imposible evitarlo.
La bebida un agua de sapo de 8000(16$), en general más costoso que tragos en otros bares del estilo.
En la misma línea siento los precios en exceso inflados para la cantidad y calidad de los productos, habiendo visitado restaurantes similares.
El servicio empezó bien, conforme se llenó de extranjeros disminuyó la atención, sigo esperando la cerveza que pedí, misma razón por la que no me quejé, fue imposible tener atención después de que llegó la comida, más que para pedir la cuenta después de largo rato.
Había un descuento del plato principal 25% por pagar con una tarjeta específica, eso ayudó.
Al final me voy decepcionado; siento que es una trampa para turistas pintada de buen marketing.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
This place was amazing. When you hear about affordable fine dining, this is what I think of.

From the restaurant ambiance to the service to THE FOOD! Everything is great.

Started off with shrimp tartare and bone Marrow. Two of my favorites dishes I’ve had on the trip. The presentation was gorgeous and not too filling. That bone marrow with the beef crudo! Lawddddd. That was fire!

We moved on to our mains and got the chicken cashew and pork tenderloin dish. The squash purée was perfect and they added some texture with some nuts on top and the tenderloin was the perfect amount of saltiness. I said I wasn’t going to eat pork on my trip and that changed my mind right away!

Finished off with the Yol Tamal! It reminded me of this cereal called “Corn Pops”. Of course 20 times better but the horchata ice cream made it the perfect finish.

Definitely will come back here again.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: A comida é realmente excepcional com um cardápio autoral enxuto. O serviço por vezes é lento.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Fue una cena espactular, desde la entrada con ostras, el platillo de pescado, las bebidas, la atención del salonero, vale la pena darse la vuelta y explorar nuevos sabores

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Unfortunately, the food’s quality and taste made the price feel excessively high — about $150 before service charge — and it simply wasn’t tasty.

The service also needs more attention. Crumbs from the first dish were left on the table until the end, and utensils weren’t changed unless absolutely necessary. These small things added up and made the whole experience feel less.

Each dish comes with a “story,” often tied to indigenous culture, local ingredients and sometimes oppression :)))but when the food itself lacks flavor, technique, and balance, the storytelling starts to feel like a cover-up rather than respect. It’s hard to justify such a high price with just words and symbolism.

I kept questioning whether my view of “fine dining” should be challenged by this “grounded” experience — maybe ordinary food can express authenticity and worth the payment. But in the end, I couldn’t reconcile it. It didn’t feel like the chef showed genuine respect for the diner’s experience, and I felt the need to write a review. Selling stories without good food isn’t honoring culture — it’s just disappointing.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Lugares sobrevalorados y este… recomiendo buscar la experiencia del influencer Arturito, él dice la  verdad y nada más que la verdad.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
This review is being written by someone who lives in the United States and Costa Rica is underestimated as far as food experiences, there are true treasures in the most unexpected parts of Costa Rica. This restaurant is #25 on the list of Latin America's 50 best restaurants. The owner/chef of this restaurant is clearly a master at his craft.

- *Ambiance*: Unique bohemian atmosphere with cultural significance
- *Food*: Fresh seafood, creative dishes like ahí tuna with sushi rice bun, and expertly prepared robalo
- *Drinks*: Creative cocktails, strong drinks made with high-quality tequila
- *Service*: Attentive server & bartender who crafted a custom margarita
- *Value*: Prices fitting for the high standard of experience

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Food and cocktails were incredible.

It's a little tough to eat here if you only speak English but I would 100% still recommend that english speakers go if they are in San Jose, just research the menu better than I did ahead of time. The dishes are very filling so you can't really order a whole lot to get a good idea of the menu. For 2 people - we ordered 2 small plates, 1 entree, and 1 dessert - we were completely stuffed.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Another fine dining restaurant worth visiting when in San Jose.
It’s definitely an experience and worth a visit. The flavours are a mix of local and foreign flavours.. of course this doesn’t suit everyone, and there were a few highs and lows when i tried as much as i could eat. This doesn’t take away from the amazing effort put into the creation of these meals (its more what i like or don’t like). The food is presented in an immaculate way and the drinks (cocktails) are an amazing mix of flavour and definitely worth a visit. The staff are super friendly and setting is quiet and relaxed. I do recommend you reserve your table.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Bonito lugar, comida x

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
+ Buen servicio y atención.
+ Desde la recepción, cantina y restaurante, todos muy amables.
+ El menú es interesante, con opciónes para diferentes gustos. Arroz, carnes, entradas..
+ La decoración es super agradable, un ambiente acogedor.
- Precios bastante altos y los precios del menú no incluyen ni impuestos ni el servicio.
+ Los meseros hablan inglés.
- Es considerablemente ruidoso en el restaurante, al menos en mi visita las mesas aledañas generaban demasiado ruido y era difícil escucharse.
- La calidad de la comida, todo muy rico. La presentación de la comida, excelente.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
I visited Sikwa on a Saturday for a friends' night out. The food was absolutely marvelous. I made a reservation and wanted to impress my friends, and I succeeded. We followed the Chef's reccommendation to try different dishes as entrées and then main dishes. It worked well for us and all of us shared different flavours. I loved the explanations on how each dish was created and the origins and ingredients used.The service was superb and world-class! My only reccommendation was to elevate the customer's arrival as the hostess felt a little quiet and shy. I'd expect a confident greeting and for them to show us our table. Otherwise i'd just say I am definitely coming back.

Ps. We finished at the Cantina having chiliguaro shots and the music back there was groovy! Loved it!

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Cena para dos un viernes. Casa llena, muy ruidoso para mantener conversación a tono normal. Cocina abierta por lo que inevitablemente se impregnaba en la ropa todos los vapores de la misma y agrega ruido al ambiente, las mesas y las sillas normales. Ambiente casual.
Dos menú degustación, presentación de alta cocina con porciones de degustación. 8 tiempos y en realidad sobresalientes sólo dos: el postre y las ostras… los demás preparaciones sencillas y nada memorable… para los precios exorbitantes. Ya que no incluye el maridaje?!
Mención aparte la macarela con arroz cremoso donde la proteína “protagonista” pasó desapercibida y el arroz con crema de coco estaba rico pero nada espectacular…
Factura ₡215000 😱 casi $500 al día de hoy …. pues agregar el precio del vino al de por si costoso precio de degustación es demasiado…
Celebrando a mi madre en el mes de ella… sabor agridulce 🥴
SOBREVALORADO…a pesar de la propuesta

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
El plato de tuétano y el postre espectaculares.
La atención y recomendaciones estupendas.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Más que una restaurante una historia llena de magia

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Fui a Sikwa justamente ayer por la noche y tuve una experiencia increíble. Soy foodie y me encanta probar propuestas interesantes y Sikwa trae una propuesta innovadora inyectada de creatividad y amor en cada detalle. Además de eso, honra la comida ancestral- autóctona de nuestro país con un twist que tiene como integrantes ingredientes de la comida Brunca y Bribri, tratados con respeto y la más hermosa creatividad.
A veces las personas leen un menú y no tienen idea de la cantidad de horas de trabajo físico y mental que existe detrás de cada plato. Sikwa es una experiencia al paladar, es identidad de nuestro país, es Arte Culinario…

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Este lugar es simplemente espectacular. Si quieren comer comida costarricense de un alto nivel Sikwa es una parada obligatoria en San José.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
La comida, bien. La atención muy bien. 7/10 en general. Pero súper caro para todo lo que debería ser una experiencia culinaria de ese precio. Es mejor Silvestre y En Boca y más baratos.

En Boca el menú degustación cuesta ₡38.000~, 7 tiempos de comida.
Silvestre, ₡48.000~, 9 tiempos.

En Sikwa el menú degustación costaba $150 y sin maridaje. Estaban mejor los platos del menú principal que el degustación de todas maneras.

Cuando llegamos había gente fumando afuera del restaurante y el host no les dice nada. Fue la primera red flag.

El restaurante tiene un problema adentro con el manejo del humo. El extractor es de mala calidad o nada más no da abasto. Todas las personas de mi mesa, éramos 8, pasamos con dolor de cabeza y mi esposa con una alergia que 1 día después no se le ha quitado.

Lo dejaría como una última opción si tuviera que escoger ir a un restaurante de esa rama.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Este restaurante 100% tico logra la hazaña de ofrecer cocina gourmet con productos que generalmente se ofrecen en una típica soda campestre costarricense.

Esto, obviamente, conlleva precios de estrella Michelin sin alcanzar el mismo nivel de servicio.

Debemos reconocer a los propietarios que han logrado la máxima rentabilidad en el sector de alimentos y bebidas. Felicidades!

Un lugar ideal para ser invitado por tu amigo turista gringo que así descubrirá la cocina tica tradicional de una manera un poco más sofisticada, sin tener que sacar tu plata.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Una experiencia decepcionante. Lo único rescatable fue la atención del mesero, pero la actitud del chef Pablo fue nefasta. La comida decepcionante. Lo único “tico” en esos platillos es que la mayoría de los ingredientes son de Costa Rica. Así que lo de ser gastronomía tica elevada es una total mentira. Lo único rico fueron los postres, así que recomendaría a Sikwa como cafetería, más no como restaurante. La entrada que pedimos era excesivamente ácida, la carne al carbón sabía a carbón y no podías sentir el sabor de absolutamente nada más, el gazpacho de pejibaye fue un completo fracaso donde no había un balance de sabores y texturas.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Con respecto a la comida, me parece sobrevalorado, caro y los sabores no son buenos, unas mezclas no agradables, además, nos sirvieron una carne con sabor a quemado, estaba completamente quemada. En el lugar no se puede hablar porque la acústica es pésima.
Pablo es una persona demasiado arrogante, mejor que no interactúe con las personas, porque los meseros sí son buena gente y eso baja aún más puntos.
Lo único bueno fue el postre

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Bastante decepcionados del lugar y la comida. La tartaleta de hongos sabía poco y lo peor de todo fue el vacío al carbón. 18 mil colones y cuando nos trajeron el plato dijeron que usaron lomo porque no tenían vacío. Que falta de seriedad.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Los ingredientes y su combinacion suena espectacular pero es un robo la calidad vs experiencia vs la cantidad. Está más barato y de mayor calidad un restaurante como SEED en la Castellana en Madrid con estrella Michellin y más barato vs este restaurante… Mejor busquen otro restaurante. Es un restaurante con excelente marketing y con muchas áreas de mejora.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Best restaurante in town! Love how Cozy it is and Pablo is the best at his job! Everyone is so nice and charming

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Sequa is a great restaurant tucked away in a quiet part of San Jose. It’s a little bit on the pricier side, but the males are excellent. Everything I’ve had here, and I have had a lot, has ranged from good to great. This includes the drinks, and they have some very good juices

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: The good was not bad but we get the a la carte menu and we asjes for the tasting menu. The waiter told us thatcthis was the boy menu. At the end of the evening we asked what Some other people got . They Saïd it was the tasting menu and that our waiter didn’t understand. So we where dissapointed. I must say the tasting menulooked good. It was a communication problem

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: While very (!) expensive (matching fine dining in New York or London), Sikwa offers a one-of-a-kind experience that is a lot more than a dinner. The chef has meticulously studied regional indigenous dishes and ingredients and created a celebration of local flavours that exposes the diner to a plethora of unknown plants and cooking techniques through innovative and delicious dishes on tailor-made plates that are outright works of art. The staff goes at great length to explain everything and one gets a lovely booklet that even indicates the Latin (scientific) names of the ingredients.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
The food was amazing!!
The people were friendly.
It was perfect!
I would highly recommend this place.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: This restaurant uses ingredients sourced from indigenous farmers to create fantastic dishes.  Had mole for the first time.  Delicious but hard to articulate the flavor.  Dishes were beautifully presented.  Service was fast and friendly.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: First night dinner here, and it did NOT disappoint. Every dish was thoughtfully prepared, but the shrimp tartare stood out as my favorite appetizer—fresh, vibrant, and full of flavor. By the end of the trip, this meal remained the most elevated dining experience I had in San Jose. What truly set it apart was the service: each course was presented with care, and the staff took the time to explain the intricacies of every item—even how to enjoy it as the chef intended. A memorable start to the trip.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Primero que nada la atención de Sergio fue clave en la experiencia. Hiciemos un tasting de las entradas y lo mas rico fue el tartar de res y el tartar de camaron. Perate la empanada de gallina! Top!
Mi plato fuerte fue el gazpacho y la mezcla de sabores fue increíble.
El postre de miel de ayote fue una delicia.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Fuimos con expectativas altas por ser un restaurante que está en la lista de 50 best. Sin embargo, la experiencia general fue muy decepcionante.

El ambiente es incómodo, hubo mal servicio general y la comida (aunque la propuesta en menú) suena atractiva hay mala ejecución, poca técnica y calidad final.

Hay muchos otros lugares en costa rica con una calidad muy superior a este restaurante, uno de los principales que pedimos era con “vacío” y llegó con un corte blancuzco y mal preparado al consultar nos dijeron que era lomito.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Absolutely stunning dishes, creatively imagined and expertly prepared. It was a beautiful meal, and well worth the stop. The cocktails were also very good, but did not appear to be executed at the same level of care as the food. Mostly my disappointment came from the fact that 3 of the 11 signature cocktails on the menu were not available at 6:30pm on a Friday night, which seems far too early to be running out of anything.  We also received an outdated menu when we were seated, which caused a little confusion.

Overall though, absolutely excellent and definitely recommend.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: ¿Un 50 Best? Fuimos hoy a cenar 5 personas. Lo más desagradable fue que en el lugar en donde nos sentaron -una de las mesas del fondo con sillones- nos salió una cucaracha de entre los sillones, lo cual habla del cuidado de la higiene (fumigación y limpieza) que tiene el restaurante (pongo foto). Un amigo, que celebraba su cumpleaños, pidió de beber un cocktail de gin de ostras sugerido en la carta, y no tenían. Yo pedí una copa de tempranillo, y me dijeron que tampoco tenían ese vino, y me lo sustituyeron por uno más caro (casualmente). Al final, pese a los elevados precios del lugar, nos quisieron cobrar una copa de vino de más que hubieramos pagado de no ser porque revisamos la cuenta y vimos el error. Además, la acústica del lugar es terrible y termina siendo un sitio super escandaloso. Fueron muchos errores en una sola cena.  Es increíble que el lugar sea un 50 Best.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Espectacular, sí cumplió expectativas. Un menú muy creativo. Probé 3 platos fuertes y 3 postres (andaba con dos amigas) y todo estuvo muy bueno. Trago+plato fuerte + postre = 30 mil. Precio/calidad 10/10. Nos atendieron muy bonito, te explican cada plato.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Sikwa lived up to the hype. Got an Uber 30 minutes from the hotel to come try it out and it did not disappoint. If you’re a foodie this is a must try. The bar attached to the restaurant is also an amazing spot for a final final.😎

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
El lugar es pequeño y no tienen mesas privadas, nos sentaron muy cerca de la salida lo cual fue un poco incómodo ya que las personas entran y salen. La espera de la comida es un poco larga pero fue deliciosa el arroz con palomito fue delicioso. Los cócteles no son tan ricos pedimos uno con licor de banano y otro de mezcal.
El postre estuvo ok, sabía como a tres leches, no fue nada especial. Recomiendo mucho los platos principales, el puré de ayote es delicioso. Muy recomendado.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text:
Comida excelente, con atención de calidad
Ambiente un poco ruidoso

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Pasear en San José y encontrar este restaurante ha sido un verdadero placer. La comida, el lugar y la música hacen de este espacio una experiencia increíble.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Schönes Lokal mit wunderbar freundlichem und kompetenten Service und einzigartigen, leckeren Gerichten mit lokalen Zutaten. Wir waren auf ganzer Linie begeistert und können das Restaurant Sikwa wärmsten empfehlen.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Cerveza caliente, menu nada especial, comida con sabor regular a mediocre, precios altos para la calidad, no recomiendo

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: Increíblemente bueno! La comida espectacular! Todo es una experiencia, desde la entrada hasta el postre. Experiencia personalizada. Soy celíaca y con alergias alimenticias, pero aquí no tuve ningún inconveniente. La comida es increíble. La calificación de los mejores restaurantes de Latinoamérica lo tienen ganado con honores!

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: I came upon this restaurant because of the world's top 50 list. I went here my first night in Costa Rica. The service was dreadfully slow, though the staff was friendly. The food was tasty, but I think their overall menu is a little too sweet for me. It could be that I just combined too many sweet-leaning things, but my stomach was not happy with me the night that I ate here.

name: Sikwa Restaurante
review_text: This was an unforgettable dining experience. My party ordered almost everything on the menu and we were blown away by the quality and complexity of the food. Sergio, our waiter, was incredibly knowledgeable and guided us through the cuisine which elevated the experience even more. This was easily the experience that stood out the most during my time in Costa Rica

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
Very nice staff
Very nice place and food

Why not have lunch here?

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: That was the best meal we have had all week in Costa Rica. The Casado had amazing sides - pumpkin, spicy coleslaw, sweet plantain, and the best savory carrots ever. It’s a simple place without ambiance but really good, inexpensive food. We enjoyed it more than the fancy places we have been.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Excellent plat

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
Great little stop for food! Friendly staff, good food, great service.

We enjoyed the Casados very much! Highly recommend.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Fuimos a cenar y encontramos una soda bien agradable con personal muy amable. La comida estaba muy rica y el precio estaba bien. Intentamos ir a desayunar para repetir pero se encontraba cerrado a la mañana siguiente, una pena! Si volvemos a La Fortuna, volveríamos a esta soda

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
Sehr lecker und für Costa Rica nicht sehr teuer.
Hier habe ich an 4 Abenden gegessen.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
Comida normal, nada especial
El spaggeti pomodoro te lo puedes saltar…

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Cobran obligatoriamente 10% de más, cuando deberia ser optativo, la comida es normal y con este incremento el precio es igual a otros restaurantes de la zona q no cobran ese 10% extra. Crei q todos cobraban ese monto pero no, muchos no te cobran ese 10%

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
Family owned authentic Costa Rican food at a great value.
Restaurant is clean. Portions are large.
We are a family of 4, we each had a meal, we ordered 2 alcoholic beverages and 1 iced tea, and had dessert for about $65US dollars.

They take credit cards. They do not charge the 10% service charge so bring cash for tip!
This was a delicious meal. I highly recommend the fajitas!!

Street parking. No a/c but open air and fans. Service was great and workers very friendly.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: This was the first soda we tried in Costa Rica & we loved it 😌 great prices and food is delicious! Would recommend.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Todo muy rico y a buen precio

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
A very welcoming place with plenty of tables in a lovely environment.  A large and fresh mixed salad arrived first followed by a seafood soup with mussels, shellfish and crabs.

My friend enjoyed his Plato Casado with chicken, rice and beans.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Really good food for a great price. They also had great service.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Rated amongst my personal top 5 while staying a week in La Fortuna. Food, service, and atmosphere was all on point. Highly recommend, price tag is perfect

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Beste soda tot nu toe in Costa Rica. Snel, lekker, goedkoop en vriendelijke bediending

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Soooo good! They give out free samples of rice pudding as well

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Très bonne cuisine pour qualité prix

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Good food for good value! We were among a lot of tourists, but the food and establishment as a whole still felt relatively authentic. We were pleased with the Casada!

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Delicious local food! Great and fast service

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: good food, good prices and service is fast! thought the portions were a bit small, not too much chicken. the mango juice is a must if you go and bolognese is surprisingly good! would come again:) staff is nice

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Delicious, authentic Costa Rican food!  The fish casado was awesome and so were the mango batidos!! Service with many smiles was topped off with complimentary rice pudding - perfect ending for a wonderful meal

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: excellent

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: This place is wonderful for dinner, good food, large portions and huge drinks. They get you served fast because all the sides are ready to serve. The owner sometimes offers you a free dessert as well, or a shot to try! We have been here two years in a row now, same great food a service. Parking is always difficult, but that’s true for anywhere down in this area.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Dit is een erg drukke Soda, hierdoor voelt het meer als een fabriekje. De rijst was nog niet gaar gekookt, beef well done terwijl medium besteld, hamburger droog, arroz con pollo weinig en smaakloos (met doorgekookte groenten). Voor een ander leuk alternatief zie mijn review over een andere Soda in La fortuna, waar wij het lekkerst hebben gegeten gedurende onze vakantie.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Excelente comida y la soda más rica de La fortuna

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: I love garlic sea bass! That’s the only dish I go to eat at this place. People are nice and food is great!

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: great food in that region.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
Service tres sympa,
2 bouteille d'eau gratuite a notre arrivee
Tres bon nacho
Et petit dessert sont offert (pudding au riz)
Tout est parfait

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Service was nice, but food was average and bland

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Si pudiera darles menos de una estrella, lo haría. Esperamos una hora y media por nuestra comida, que resultó ser mediocre y con porciones más pequeñas que mi puño. Parece que no les importan los clientes gringos. Nos tuvimos que quejar para que nos cambiaran la comida. Para colmo, he estado vomitando y con mal de estómago durante tres días. Todo está mezclado, y no tienen aseo. La comida está mala o mal preparada.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Amazing Amazing sea bass. Best I’ve ever had. Lovely people quick service. Perfect value. Words don’t do justice. If you like happiness you’ll like Viquez.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Delicious food and affordable price. Service was quick and efficient. Will definitely go again next time we are in Costa Rica.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
The place is nice.
Same food has different taste on different days.
Staff is nice, polite and helpful.
Worthy for the price

name: Soda Víquez

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Súper recomendable. El servicio es maravilloso. La comida muy buena. Nosotros pedimos casados, y te pueden variar los ingredientes que hay en la carta sin problema (a mí no me gustan los frijoles y los cambiaron). La comida está muy bien hecha y el precio fenomenal, muy económico. El ambiente muy acogedor, está bastante lleno, que eso es buena señal. Está muy bien localizado al lado de la plaza principal de La Fortuna.

name: Soda Víquez

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Food was good. Also we got free plantain chips with bean dip and a free small dessert

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Una verdadera y auténtica Soda en La Fortuna. La mesera fue bien amable con nosotros en explicarnos el menú y ayudarnos a escoger. Pedimos casados igual con la sopa de hongos creo y estuvo riquísimo todo. Recomiendo esta soda en particular en visitar y explorar La Fortuna

name: Soda Víquez

name: Soda Víquez

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Excelente calidad y un precio buenisimo. Atención excelente y muy rápida. El fresco de mango deli!

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: My wife says this was her favorite meal of La Fortuna. Simple yet delicious. The Casado is generous and includes a squash puree that is fairly unique. We came after ziplining and probably could have eaten one of everything.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Es una soda bonita y con comida muy rica y trato muy amable. Solo que ahí mismo te dicen que el plato viene muy bien servido y no es el caso, el casado trae muy poca proteína.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text:
Great food.
The service was amazing. Highly recommend for some Costa Rica food.

name: Soda Víquez
review_text: Très bon Casado (avec du choix dans la protéine), bon rapport qualité prix. Belles quantités et beaucoup d’éléments dans le casado (riz, haricot, coleslaw, salade, purée de patate et de banane, plantain et protéine)  . Salle agréable et service très sympathique. Je recommande

name: Soda Víquez

name: Soda Víquez

name: Soda Víquez
