review_text: Views from the balcony of our 4th floor room where breath taking! When we were not enjoying the many pools, we spent a lot of time reading and enjoying drinks with views of Arenal volcano. At arrival we were greeted by Fabian who offered us a welcome beverage as well as a cool towel to refresh ourselves as he explained the hotel and property. Great customer service continued with Sebastian who helped us with our luggage and informed us of all the features in the room. The room has a slot near the door so when you come in the room and insert your key card all the lights and air-conditioning activate. When you leave, they automatically turn off for you to quickly and conveniently help conserve energy. The balcony has chairs and a table along with a huge floor to ceiling sliding glass door that also has a screen door. Reservations at the Lava Restaurant will take you to the 5th floor where you can enjoy great food and service in an intimate setting along with a fantastic view of Arenal. The Emperador is on the main floor and hosts an outstanding breakfast buffet with some daily changes and at night high-class dinner. Breakfast included a large variety of options from omelets made to order, build your own yogurt parfait, fruits, eggs, cheese and meats. Dinner here is incredible! I had cream of corn soup for the app, the special blackened seabass stole the show for dinner and chocolate temptation desert. 5-star experience all around. Thank you to servers Israel, Jose and Tatiana!!

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Amazing hotel experience. The drinks are very good and the pool is great. The sauna is nice too. Service was excellent. The rooms are very good and have a great view of the volcano. Would stay here again!

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
Our 4-day stay here was just so fantastic and perfect. I felt completely at ease. The staff is wonderful, the hotel facilities with the hot springs absolutely won me over and dinner at the Lava Restaurant was delicious.
I have to make a small deduction for the mattresses, which were a little too soft. Nevertheless, it more than deserves its 5 stars.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Excelente lugar para vacacionar, súper recomendado.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Perfect location for visiting LA Fortuna. Every attraction you want is 30 mins away. Upon arrival, you are greeted by Sebastian the bellhop, and he starts the experience. Valentina at the front desk will make sure your stay is perfect. The staff here are all 5 stars. 3 pools 4 saunas give you plenty of relaxation choices. Two very good restaurants to choose from with the one on the top floor great for views at evening time. I highly recommend this hotel.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Liked it,

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Initially, we were a bit disappointed by the decor of the lobby and ground floor restaurant as other places we stayed in Costa Rica were more tropical and cozy. The ground floor of the hotel could benefit from a bit more flowers (in our view). However, our opinion quickly changed, and we ended up loving our stay here. The location is excellent, and we thoroughly enjoyed the pool and hot springs. The staff was incredibly helpful and kind, making our stay even more enjoyable. The rooms were clean and comfortable, and the food at the Emperador restaurant was very good (try the steak!). Surprisingly, this restaurant had a totally different look than the rest of hotel. It was beautiful and cozy, with a stunning view on the Volcano.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Absolutely amazing experience!! Outstanding service, food and location. I was very impressed with the staff too. Very kind and professional people. I highly recommend this place!!❤️❤️

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Excelente el concepto  del hotel, atención muy buena, lo recomiendo, la comida y la pasa super bien uno.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Excelente servicio

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: IMPECABLE sería la palabra para describir el hotel, el personal atento, amable y efectivo, el servicio es excelente, la limpieza del hotel como las albercas es impecable, la vista de las habitaciones hermosas

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Excelente servicio, super recomendado, la atención y demás.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Enjoyed our stay here but this is a 4-star hotel. Wonderful staff. Easy parking. Great breakfast. The rooms were fine but not a 5-star hotel (for example the tv was old and barely worked). Thermal pools were cute but just pools not hot springs.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
Very unique, smaller hotel. 54 rooms total, all the pools are heated by the Arenal volcano. If you are looking for a hotel that is serene and nature inspired, this is it. One downside is that this hotel is located next to a major highway so while sitting in a beautiful jacuzzi you hear semis Jake braking and cars whizzing by.

The food is ok, not great ($$$). The drinks were great by the pool if made by Israel;) the drinks were very pricey (ranging from $10-$20/drink).

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: My partner & I booked the final night of our Babymoon at The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa and Resort; it was the cherry on top of our Costa Rica adventure. The staff kindly greeted us upon arrival and were soooo beyond nice and accommodating throughout our stay. The welcome drinks, cold face towels, and gorgeous room decorated to celebrate our baby on the way was delightful. The hot spring pools varied in temperature, and I was able to comfortably sit out of each space, when I needed to. We highly recommend staying at Royal Corin ❤️

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
This was my first time visiting Costa Rica and this hotel was wonderful! It is worth the 2 hour drive from LIR. The staff was friendly and helpful!  We had a beautiful view of Arenal Volcano from our balcony. Breakfast was included, very extensive, and we got a free welcome beverage! Yumm!

There are many thermal pools on the property. The spa was also an amazing experience with the skillful hands of Maria! There are saunas in the spa area as well.

We had snacks by the pool and dinner upstairs, lovely experience!

Our hotel is also close to La Fortuna Falls and Mistico Park! Definitely must go!

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: My husband and I stayed here for our honeymoon. We absolutely loved it. Hotel is gorgeous, staff is amazing and attentive. They did something special since we were on our honeymoon. Hotel was 10 minutes away from the La Fortuna waterfall which is beautiful. I definitely recommend this hotel

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Had a wonderful stay at this property. The staff was attentive and went above and beyond to make us welcome. The rooms were well appointed and clean. The pools and hot spring were wonderful, as well as the spa services.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: I spent a long weekend here and had the best time. The rooms are clean and updated, every employee is kind and attentive, and it is a close drive to multiple tours, excursions, downtown, etc. The food is delicious - unlike when you go to typical all inclusive resorts. There are multiple hot pools outside, one with a swim up bar where you can eat. It is quiet and relaxing. I would 100% come back.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
My wife and I loved this place!

The  Resort is nestled at the base of Arenal Volcano and is a true gem for those seeking an unforgettable experience. The moment you step into this tranquil haven, you'll be enveloped by the soothing ambiance and attentive staff, who are dedicated to ensuring your visit is nothing short of exceptional. We had a great view of the Arenal Volcano from our room.  You also get great views from the thermal pools area.

The thermal pools, filled with mineral-rich waters, provide a unique and rejuvenating experience that will leave you feeling refreshed and revitalized.

The Royal Corin has a fantastic restaurant, the Emperador, which serves up delicious and beautifully presented dishes using fresh, local ingredients. The dining experience here is truly exceptional, with friendly staff and a warm, welcoming atmosphere that perfectly complements the stunning surroundings.

Overall, the Royal Corin Spa is an absolute must-visit for anyone looking to unwind and indulge in some much-needed pampering with its stunning location, top-notch facilities, and exceptional service.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
Nice hotel, but lots of mosquitos. Flew from Florida. I stayed at this hotel for 3 days, unfortunately this hotel was the only place I got bit during my whole trip… no bites during ziplining, waterfalls, tours, beach in Manuel Antonio/Quepos. I’m not sure but seems like the mosquitos are really bad at this hotel. It was good hotel but hard to enjoy for me. I had 2 types of repellent on including DEET which seemed to help more than just 1 repellent, but then the mosquitos bit my face so I had to start putting repellent on my face. Mosquitos are all over the breakfast  area, reception, and outdoor hot springs. I was supposed to come back from my vacation glowing and rejuvenated but instead I am coming back  with mosquito bites and swollen forehead and eyelid :(

I think aside from the mosquitos this is a really good hotel, delicious breakfast, very fresh food and good service. Everyone here was very nice. Hot springs were very warm and relaxing. Absolutely perfect! There is also a bar in the hot springs which is fantastic! Great view of the Arenal Volcano as well

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Amazing hotel. Room was huge, clean and comfortable, great view of the volcano. The spa pools and jacuzzis were lovely and the luscious planting in the garden areas all around was a joy. There were plenty of seats and loungers to relax on, most were secluded, always something available.  The food in the downstairs restaurant was excellent. Only caveat was our one time in the upstairs restaurant - I finished my starter quickly, they swept the plate up and then delivered both the mains, literally pushing my wife's half-eaten starter to one side to make room for the main, which she didn't want yet! Other than that, all the staff were very friendly and helpful. For example - we were late for breakfast one morning, arrived less than five mins before closing. We were the only ones there - after a while, waiter asked if we would mind if they started closing down - they'd left everything open in case we wanted to return for refills. And there was a huge choice of food for breakfast. Another example - we'd had a tyre blowout with our hire car, I asked receptionist to contact them and she was amazing, really pushed them and we got it all sorted really quickly thanks to her.  I thoroughly recommend The Royal Corin.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Really amazing experience staying at the Royal Corin. The staff is so attentive and everyone we encountered was so nice and pleasant. Fabian at the front desk was truly outstanding. He helped plan some adventures for us and always checked in with us to make sure we were having a good time. We rented a car and we had somewhere to park the whole stay. The breakfast came with the room and the food changed daily and it was very delicious. They also make food to order. The bar in the hot spring is also cheap compared to US pricing standards for a drink (especially at a hotel). It was a little farther from town but if you rent a car it’s a 4 minute drive and also a 10 minute drive to the volcano. It was truly a luxurious experience and i recommend this hotel to anyone.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Äußerst freundlicher Empfang mit durchweg freundlichen Mitarbeitern. Auch für die Aktivitäten hatten wir eine sehr gute Beratung an einem großen Monitor. Es wurden auch Trips empfohlen die ohne Verkauf durch das Hotel war en da wir eigenes Auto hatten.|Wunderschön angelegte Thermalwasseranlage mit verschiedenen Pools und mehreren Jacuzzis.|Großzügiges Zimmer, wir hatten ein Zimmer, das behindertengerecht war, was aber von der Ästhetik her nicht zu sehen war |Wir hatten eine Paarmassage, die wir übereinstimmend als eine unserer besten Massage bewertet haben, die wir je hatten.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
A high end hotel with attentive and courteous staff. The thermal pools surround the main three pools. Of the main pools, the only cool pool is the one to the left. The center pool and the right pool are both around 38 Celsius.  The other pools vary by one or two degrees.  There is also a large steam sauna.

Padded loungers surround the pools and there are several canopied areas around the pools with outdoor sofas and chairs. There is also a sitting area out on the second floor.

The best restaurant is the Lava restaurant on the top floor. It provides a view of the pool with evening breezes sweeping in and filled with the sounds of birds.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Beautiful hot spring spa. Wonderful staff who really cared about your comfort and happiness. Food and ambiance in Lava restaurant very good. Plenty of choice for breakfast. Lovely warm pool area with swim up bar. Fabulous different temperature Jacuzzis.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: An amazing hotel. The staff is wonderful. Know you by name. Highly recommend! All from the rooms, food, location is perfect.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: We are so happy we chose to stay here for our honeymoon. It was absolutely beautiful and such a relaxing and tranquil place too. Every single staff member we met and got to know in our 5 day stay were extremely kind. They also went out of their way in many different ways to help us celebrate our honeymoon. The rooms were clean, updated and felt luxurious. Our spa treatments and the private spa area we got access to that day were also outstanding. The hot spring pools and hot tubs were absolutely stunning. The breakfasts were incredible too. We will absolutely visit again one day!

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
My wife and I had such an amazing stay at the Royal Corin.

The staff were super friendly and we appreciated the small details such as remembering our names, room number, and the last day of our staff.

The rooms were very clean and comfortable, as was the entire hotel.

The drinks were very high quality (e.g., using real blended fruit) and were quite affordable.

The breakfast was delicious - there was a lot of variety. It was extra special when we were able to sit outside (worth the wait).

If I were to change one thing, it would be the music at the pool - there was a lot of 80s music. I love a good 80s song, but some variety would have been nice.

If you are coming to LA Fortuna, I highly recommend staying at the Royal Corin.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Courtesy, professionalism and respect define this resort…. Thank you Royal Corin…👏

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Espectacular lugar y personal muy amable.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: We visited for a few days for our honeymoon and absolutely loved this hotel and staff. From our initial check in with Valentina, to Abril hosting us for our spa experience, their service is one of the best we’ve experienced. The whole staff truly wants to ensure you have the best vaction. We highly recommend staying here and will be back to visit.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
Had a lovely 4 night stay. What stands out about this property is the level of service. Each and every staff member we had communicated with were so helpful and kind. Javier in particular was above and beyond trying to make our stay one to remember.

The good: hot springs were not crowded at all, quiet, level of service, the NYE event the staff threw for the guests was lovely.

The bad: not walkable to centre of town but it’s 10 dollars for a cab. Maybe a shuttle service for guests would be nice?

The ugly: the beds weren’t the greatest (felt my husbands every move at night)
More attention to cleaning the rooms is required (found a few hairs and dust on the floor upon check in)

We would recommend.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Las personas todas en general muy atentas. Los clientes todos muy educados se vive un ambiente de mucha relajación. Los platos ejecutados por la cocina muy adecuados recomiendo este hotel.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
We stayed here for two nights and were immediately greeted with hospitality and friendliness, not to mention welcome drinks for our whole party. Rooms were spacious and well appointed—even had spare dental kits, vanity kits, extras of body gel/shampoo/conditioner/lotion, and a nice automatic espresso machine. Breakfast was fantastic in both selection and service. Dinner in both Lava and the Emperador restaurants were likewise excellent in variety of option, preparation, and service. (There honestly wasn’t much of a difference between the two, so not a big deal if you only eat at one). Finally the pools and the pool bar were super relaxing—even though it rained for much of the time we were there.

We also used the in-house booking options for two tours. One was great (Arenal waterfall) and another so-so (Rope Bridges) but no big deal. The transport options were timely and convenient for us.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Excellent all around! Amazing & attentive service. Quiet & peaceful.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text: Nous sommes restés 3 nuits ici avec nos deux jeunes adultes dans deux chambres.  Nous avons adorés notre séjour : Les chambres, les lits ultra confortables, la magnifique piscine donnant sur le bar, les bains thermaux, l’aménagement magnifique. Le personnel est très gentil et très serviable, spécifiquement au bar de la piscine.  Le petit déjeuner est très bon. Le spa est magnifique.  Seul petits bémols :  La rue est proche, et selon la chambre on peut entendre beaucoup la circulation.  Ensuite, nous avons pris la plupart de nos repas ici et avons été déçus par les plats de steak ( filet mignon avec du gras, et autres coupes avec aussi beaucoup de gras.). Mis à part ces plats spécifiques, la nourriture a été très bonne dans les deux restaurants.  La proximité de l’hôtel est parfaite également pour les activités prisées ( volcan, cascade, ponts suspendus..).  L’hôtel nous a rapidement organisé une superbe journée d’activités  où l’on nous prenait et ramenait directement à l’hôtel.  Bref, excellent séjour et souvenirs merveilleux de notre passage ici.

name: The Royal Corin Thermal Water Spa & Resort
review_text:
If you are looking for a luxury resort in La Fortuna, stop your search now and book the Royal Corin.

The staff are the highlight of this hotel: April (who runs the spa), was such a wonderful host and made sure we had everything we needed. Jesslyn, Valentina, and the wonderful folks at the front desk helped us with every need, big or small, including taxis, reservations and excursions (Steven, Fabian and many more helped us). As we were checking out, the staff double checked our rooms and found a few items we had left behind, which was so nice.

The hotel property itself is fabulous. It is only one of two properties in the area to actually have real hot spring water feeding into the pools. They have four jacuzzis, two cold pools, and one large hot spring on the property. There is a wet sauna as well, but it wasn't very hot when we visited.

The rooms are spacious and clean. Each room has a balcony with a volcano view, incredible.

Make sure to spend some time in the spa as well. Not only were the massages wonderful, but the spa area has additional hot spring pools, two dry saunas, two jacuzzis, a plunge pool and more. Gym is small and therefore requires a reservation, but no problem getting a slot.

Finally, the free breakfast (which we didn't even know about!) is bountiful and a real treat.

The hotel is immaculate and well-run.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: One of the most magical experiences I have ever had. This resort is exceptional on so many levels. From the very quick, efficient, and professional service from staff, to the very very tasteful and high quality food, to the INSANELY beautiful and relaxing hot tubs. The scenery is breathtaking, again it’s so magical being here.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: We did the day pass as we already booked our hotel but wanted the hot springs experience without the crazy crowds.. and this place was awesome! They give you a map showing the temps in the different pools, options for food and drinks are easy to find and delicious.. Definitely recommend!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: This place was truly unbelievable. The vibes were immaculate . The hot springs are unique and the resort offers a wide variety of activities for all ages . The overall service was very very good and the food was all very solid . Highly recommend if traveling to costa rica

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Gorgeous and the hot springs are the best part. Quite an expansive resort and service is amazing

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Could not recommend this resort enough. My friends and I stayed at the Springs for my birthday, and the staff made every effort to make it special. The property itself is gorgeous with plenty to do around the property. Special thank you to Rachel, Jose, and Antonio for going above and beyond.
The rooms are extremely spacious and spotless with breathtaking views of the volcano.

Thank you to all of the staff at the springs resort for making our stay so special!!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Think of this as the theme park version of Costa Rica. Sanitized, comfortable, and completely detached from the country surrounding it. For families who want to switch off and soak in beautiful pools without a second thought, it might be exactly what they're looking for. For us, it missed the mark.

**The good:** Staff are genuinely wonderful, warm and welcoming throughout. The pool complex is impressive for a property this size, with enough tucked-away spots that it never feels overcrowded. Food was decent. Club Rio activities were a highlight for the kids; we did river tubing and the night walk, both fun. Housekeeping was lovely, and complimentary self-service laundry was a nice touch.

**The not-so-good:** Rooms are surprisingly dated for the price point. Hard beds, tired finishes. I actually slept better on the pull-out couch than the main mattress. Prices are aggressively inflated: $13 USD for a 350ml IPA, $28 for a negroni, gift shop items marked up roughly 3x. We budgeted for expensive and the sticker shock still hit. A meal for four without drinks ran $100-125 USD. Getting to La Fortuna requires a $60 USD return shuttle, which we did once and saved a fortune on food and drinks for the day. Small things added up too: mini golf had two clubs for the entire course, so we had to wait for the family ahead to finish each hole before we could play.

One observation that's less a complaint and more a symptom: nearly everyone spoke fluent English with very little Spanish anywhere on the property. Convenient, but it added to the feeling that this place exists in a bubble rather than in Costa Rica.

We spent two weeks travelling the country through Manuel Antonio, Puntarenas, and Monteverde, and this was the only stop that felt transactional and inauthentic. A caricature of Costa Rica rather than the real thing. At this price point, you can do better.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: I just wrapped up an amazing four-day stay at this place! The rooms were fantastic, and the food was even better. The fine dining steak I had was honestly the best I’ve ever had. The entire team was incredibly hospitable, and the service was top-notch. I couldn’t have asked for a better experience!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
We had a good overall experience at The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal, mainly because the hot springs themselves were beautiful and relaxing. The property has a ton of hot springs to explore, and the service throughout the resort was friendly. It was also nice that they offer a shuttle down to additional hot springs on the property for even more exploring, although those were definitely less maintained than the main ones.

That said, for a hotel that markets itself as a luxury five-star resort, we expected a lot more in other areas. The biggest disappointment was definitely the food - extremely expensive while also being surprisingly low quality. The resort tries to offer a little bit of every cuisine, but none of it was very good, and most meals felt really underwhelming for the price.

The overall vibe also felt less upscale than expected at times. There were people vaping at the pools and loud partying happening in the middle of the afternoon, which took away from the relaxing luxury atmosphere we were hoping for.

Overall, we’re glad we experienced it for the hot springs, but outside of that, we’d probably choose somewhere else next time. If you do decide to stay here, I’d recommend doing just a one- or two-night stay to experience the hot springs, since you can explore most of them within a few hours, and then continue on to another hotel afterward.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: The hotel staff are truley wonderful and go out their way to make you comfortbale so that cannot be faulted, also the rooms are large and clean and very well maintained - you wont actually have a bad stay if you stay at this hotel and its perfectly pleasent. The volacano hike, the tour company used is lovely and the again very helpful, but its a walk near the volcano and honestly the view from the hotel is much better.||the reason the hotel only gets three stars instead of 5, is because the hotel itself feels a little man made and artifcal around such beautiful natural surroundings. The hot springs etc - this takes away that feel of luxury and almost makes it feel a little tacky - and probably not worth the premium price.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: It’s expensive but worth it

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Our experience at The Springs Resort & Spa exceeded every expectation. The property offers a perfect balance of luxury, comfort, adventure, and nature, making it one of the most impressive destinations we have visited in Costa Rica.||From the very first moment, Jose, Ely, and Bayron made us feel welcomed and valued thanks to their attention to detail and outstanding professionalism. The entire resort is beautifully designed, with spectacular thermal pools, peaceful spa areas, excellent restaurants, and breathtaking panoramic views of the rainforest and volcano surroundings. Every corner of the property feels exclusive and thoughtfully designed.||One thing that truly stood out was how immaculate the gardens, tropical plants, and green areas are throughout the resort. Considering how massive the property is, the maintenance team deserves special recognition for keeping everything in outstanding condition every single day. The perfectly maintained landscapes create a natural paradise that attracts an incredible variety of birds and wildlife, making the experience feel even more magical and connected to nature.||The kids absolutely loved the game room — they had so much fun playing ping pong, video games, puzzles, and enjoying the free popcorn. Special thanks to Yetty Rodriguez and Fabiola, who did an amazing job making everyone feel welcome. They were incredibly kind, attentive, and always smiling with both kids and adults.||The hiking trails around the resort are beautiful and relaxing, and the gym and entertainment areas offer something for everyone. Every meal we had was excellent, with fresh ingredients and high-quality presentation that elevated the entire experience.||Another unforgettable part of the trip was visiting Club Río, located below the resort and accessible by the internal shuttle through the rainforest. The ride itself is beautiful and gives guests an even deeper appreciation for the surrounding jungle.||The activities at Club Río were exceptional. The horseback riding was peaceful and scenic, the wildlife sanctuary was very interesting, and the river tubing adventure was one of the highlights of our trip. Huge thanks to the guides Sivianny , Gerald, and Erick for their professionalism, positive energy, and commitment to safety throughout the experience.||We also want to recognize Jefry Urbina, the resort photographer, for capturing such incredible memories during the tubing adventure. His friendly personality and charisma made the entire experience feel natural and enjoyable, and the photo presentation afterward with upbeat dance music created a very special ending to an already unforgettable day for our family.||This resort truly represents the beauty and hospitality of Costa Rica. Perfect for couples, families, or anyone looking for a luxurious experience surrounded by adventure and nature. We would absolutely return again.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: This was an incredible part of our honeymoon! this is one of the most beautiful resorts we have ever been to and was romantic and perfect! |the service is incredible, and every person we interacted created a sense of comfort and joy to our experience. |the food, the room, and the springs were all amazing! we thoroughly enjoyed the springs at club rio, the springs at the resort itself, and the views of arenal volcano. |and the food here is amazing! the breakfast buffet is wonderful, with local cuisine as well as fresh fruits, omelet bar, and fantastic pastries! we had dinner as tres cascadas which was one of our favorite meals, as well as las ventanas which was also delightful. We can't wait to return!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: My family had the best experience at The Springs Resort! All of the staff were so welcoming and kind. I’d like to especially mention Joe, Bayron, and Jefry. They were very attentive and helpful to with my handicap accessible needs. The food was incredible!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: My family had the best experience at The Springs Resort! All of the staff were so welcoming and kind. I’d like to especially mention Joe, Bayron, and Jefry. They were very attentive and helpful to with my handicap accessible needs. The food was incredible!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Buy for a best hotel and resort I’ve stayed at super luxurious attention to detail amazing views jungle experience, Hot Springs everywhere. Monkeys sloths two cans in your own backyard.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
This was our first visit to the property.  We arrived after a six-hour drive from Arenas Del Mar, looking forward to experiencing the resort. Because of the long journey, we were especially hoping for a timely check-in. Unfortunately, although we arrived around 5:00 p.m., our room was not ready until approximately 6:00 p.m. Considering that standard check-in is typically around 3:00 p.m., this delay meant we effectively lost most of the first evening.

The dining experience was also a major disappointment. My husband and children are vegan, so the quality and variety of food are particularly important to us. Unfortunately, the vegan options were limited and the quality of the food overall did not reflect what we would expect from a five-star resort. Even for me, as the only non-vegan member of the family, One evening I ordered shrimp sushi, and having traveled extensively and stayed at many resorts around the world, I can say that the quality was far below five-star standards. The sushi was unfortunately barely edible and quite difficult to eat.

The final evening was particularly difficult.

On the evening of 04/23/26, when we attempted to order a vegan dish, we were informed that there were only six portions available for the entire resort for that week, and that because we had ordered four the previous day, only two remained. As a family of six, this created an immediate problem. Rather than offering alternatives or helping us find a solution, the waiter responded in a way that felt abrupt and unhelpful. The interaction with the waiter, Joaquin Sanchez,
was very rude, and the situation with the limited food options created tension within the family rather than the relaxing evening we had hoped for. One of the managers, Byron Alvarado, did provide us with some potato dishes on the house, but when I wanted his full name, he disrespectfully pointed at his name and walked away disrespectfully. To make matters worse, my husband later became ill and began vomiting after eating, which turned what should have been a pleasant last night into a very distressing one.

The hot spring pools themselves were very nice, and we did appreciate that aspect of the property. However, a five-star experience is not defined only by the facilities. Hospitality, respectful service, and high-quality dining are essential parts of the overall experience, and unfortunately this is where we felt the resort fell short during our visit.

We did reach out to management twice,  but unfortunately there was no response.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
מקום מדהים כמי באגדות
ההגעה קצת קשה וחייב רכב כדי להתנייד

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
My sister and I spent 7 nights there and it was a really wonderful experience. All of the staff were very welcoming and friendly. I felt there was great communication amongst all the staff members and we felt very seen and acknowledged our entire stay.

I do have one complaint though, but not for the employees. The owners. I find it absolutely ridiculous how the front desk staff does not have a stool or chair to sit on. My sister and I would leave in the mornings, return in the afternoon to change and leave again and return and leave again for dinner in town and the same staff would still be standing upon our return. This was very disappointing to see because as people in the medical field we know how detrimental that can be for a person’s health. I urge the owners to do something about this issue, this should be illegal. Allowing them to sit will not take away from the “luxury” of this hotel or the service. Everyone was so kind there and they deserve to work in a healthy environment as well.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Ever since I saw the photos online, I dreamed of visiting this resort. Turns out it’s even better in person!
From the beauty of the grounds, to the relaxing private springs, and large rooms; this place was AMAZING!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Great place.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Positive: This hotel is magical!  he room is huge and a direct view of the volcano and we loved the patio with the hammock. The bathroom was large with a stand alone shower and a jacuzzi tub.  The various hot springs throughout the property are a must experience. Negative: The mattress made noises anytime we moved and the pillows were very hard

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: One of the best views from your room of the Arenal volcano.  The food was seriously lacking and should be improved. The hotsprings pool was small but nice. There is a nearby stream to visit for activities and restaurant. Great for the family, huge kids playroom. Overall good service and good experience.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
This is one of my favourite resorts in the world, it’s a treat anytime I get to visit. Lee Banks absolutely nailed it. There is no detail missed, from the interior design, feng shui, hot springs, views, food, and most importantly the staff - everyone is so genuine and committed to offering top notch hospitality.
I also want to give a specific shout-out to Emilio S. and Bismarck H. for going above and beyond to offer the best guest experience possible. Pura vida 🤙

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Solo pasamos por el day pass de las aguas termales, no nos hospedamos y fue muy bonita experiencia, incluye dos días lo que es súper y muchas piscinas con temperaturas diferentes, realmente te relajas bastante en las aguas después de un día lleno de caminatas

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: So many pools with different temperatures, there’s something for everyone. The place is huge, with a natural river and tons of activities. The breakfast buffet is epic (come hungry 😄). Staff is amazing, super friendly and always ready to help. They even have a coworking space if you need to get things done. Second time here… and not the last.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: We had a great stay at the resort. The food was amazing. We saw many animals on the property including sloths,monkeys, coatis and hundreds of frogs. Esteban was great and helped us throughout the trip.  Highly recommend this place. The view is spectacular and everyone was super friendly.  The springs are plentiful and amazing

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: We decided to splurge on this trip and pay a bit extra. This place is absolutely amazing. Gorgeous architecture, landscaping, and views. And incredibly friendly staff. This is a first-class resort all the way. You will not be disappointed.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Truly a beautiful place! Awesome views & people

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Top notch resort. Impeccable rooms and high quality service. Amazing views of Arenal Volcano. Expensive drinks but overall, worth it for the atmosphere and peace of mind at a great resort.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
מומלץ בחום מקום מושלם
באמת - הגענו לגן עדן
אן מילים לתאר את המקום

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: We were there only 2 1/2 day but loved loved the stay.  Food was great, staff learned our names the first day, on  and off site excursions were fantastic.  We enjoyed to many pools and would recommend this location.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Was wonderful overlooking the Arenal volcano.  Staff was beyond wonderful,  food was great, the on-site and off-site excursions were wonderflu.  Bit pricey but you get what you pay for.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
The resort itself was very nice. Well maintained and the staff was super friendly and attentive. It didn't really feel like costa rica. It felt like you were just in a really nice resort in America.

They also seemed to nickle and dime you for everything, food, adventures, and the stuff in your room. For as much as the place cost some of these things should probably be included in the price

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Property - 5 stars
Service - 1.5 stars
Food - Negative stars

This place is gorgeous. The Springs are great. The Club Rio was serene and beautiful. The volcano views unbelievable. But, it's all ruined by clear lack of any service training (after being ignored for 5 mins, I grabbed a bell boy cart myself for example) and the mostly garbage food and service at the restaurants. The food is priced how you would expect (too expensive but not outrageous comparatively) but the service and general quality of the food was abysmal. It's too bad this otherwise great hotel is ruined by these things. Luckily we had a car so went to La Fortuna for pretty much every meal we could after seeing how bad the food was, but had to still have a few meals on premise. Also, the kids room is stupidly staffed by someone sitting on their phone all day and they require parents there at all times. There is simply no service culture, with constant ignoring by the staff at restaurants.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: My family and I couldn’t be more happy that we chose this resort. This exceeded all expectations. We all have nothing but great things to say about our entire experience, from the obvious spectacular views/location, to the food, drinks, and service. Every single person working there was so welcoming and helpful with everything. Extremely accommodating. We highly recommend this resort to any and all people looking for a fantastic time in Costa Rica. A special thank you to the following people; Gabriel, Alejandra , Michael , Pamela, Jimena, Juan Carlos, Tony, Ely, Esteban, Fraviolo. I know I’m missing so many more wonderful people. There isn’t one employee here that wasn't friendly. My family and I thank everyone from the bottom of our hearts for making this a special vacation.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: The Springs is an amazing property… Gorgeous rooms, all with views of the volcano, all blended seamlessly into the surrounding rainforest. On-site dining was fantastic, breakfast service was incredible (the coffee, the gallo pinto, eggs made to order). The fitness center had plenty of machines, treadmills, free weights, plus a rower. The concierge was super helpful, it was easy to book excursions both on-site and off-site. We visited the property’s nature preserve and did an early bird watching tour, plus some off-site zip lining. And, of course, we took full advantage of the well-maintained hot springs! One thing that surprised me… Even in the middle of a rainforest, the rooms were impeccably clean. Honestly wish we had stayed one more night!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Did the day pass. Great pools. The tubing is not 1 hour, it's a 10 minute run done twice.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Paradise on Earth . My husband and I travel often and this must be one of our favorite resorts to stay in . Facilities are incredible, Rooms are clean spacious and comfortable. Service is 10/10

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Fuimos a las termas, preciosas para disfrutar y la carta de los restaurantes buenas y a buen precio. Una pena que nos cobraran 180$ por persona y que no haya opciones de esta riqueza del país, de forma gratuita.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: My family of 4 (kids aged 8 and 10) had such a magical time at the Springs (stayed 6 nights). We were in a Vista Grande Double and it was the perfect size. The views of Arenal, the hot springs and Club Rio really made our time at the Spring enjoyable. Concierge was so helpful and was always accommodating to all of our schedule changes and they booked all the top tier tours. We were able to see sloths and monkeys on the property. We loved the horseback riding, cat feeding and tubing.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Lugar muy acogedor. Lindas piscinas. Nos encantó!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
We visited The Springs Resort & Spa with a day pass that my partner booked as part of our anniversary trip. At $150 per person plus tax, we were expecting a premium, well-organized experience to match the price.

Unfortunately, the service fell short from the moment we arrived. Reception sent us to the spa, and the spa staff simply pointed us toward the pools without any welcome or explanation. There was no orientation at all!!! We had to repeatedly ask different staff members where to find basic amenities like towels and lockers, which made the experience feel disorganized and impersonal.

The pools themselves are enjoyable, but overall it didn’t justify the premium cost. It may be a much better experience for overnight guests, but as a day pass visitor, it simply didn’t feel worth it.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Nous sommes venues à 2 avec un Day Pass. C’était une expérience incroyable ! Les piscines chaudes sont très nombreuses, tout est magnifiquement bien entretenu, nous étions quasiment seules. Le restaurant était délicieux, le service adorable. Nous recommandons, cela vaut son prix !

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: My husband and I just returned from the Resort. The resort was just beautiful. The staff are fantastic. The property and rooms are very clean. I would recommend the Club Rio activities whole heatedly. Since it is the end if the dry season, we could not Kayak, but the horseback riding and Nature Preserve were incredible. We were able to do tubing but the water level was getting low. Our only comment is that we found the food was not up to a 5 star rating. 3/5 best. We did take a taxi into La Fortuna to a casual place one night and it was excellent and felt very safe. There were many families visiting the hotel and we agreed that they may not have been as picky (with children) regarding the food, but we were not wowed at all.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Amazing!!!
One of the best resorts we have ever stayed at.  Rooms were fantastic, the hot springs at the resort were perfect and the service was outstanding! We really enjoyed the activities and excursions - river tubing, horse back riding in the river, repelling waterfalls, zip lining, night nature walk, animal sanctuary. The whole resort is a bit pricey, but well worth it.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: This place is amazing as far as location and views. The pricing for the hot spots is 150 per person per day. The hubby and I drove up and the booked the day pass we did not book a room. I would advise to book the day pass so you can explore without rush in the am. We arrived later in the day and the person at the reservation desk did not advise us that the thermals close at 5 and you have to take a tram down. We had already paid when he decided to provide us specifics. I wish they had more attention to detail. We were staying in the area so we could have came back a different day. Also, if you aren’t a staying guest at the hotel, it is hard to get bar service. There were enough bar tenders for the 15 people at the bar but took a long time to get someone’s attention and not because we were not trying. The location is great the views are impressive. We did get a chance to see a variety of birds, and a momma sloth and baby sloth right above us at the thermals.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Place is big. A lot of hot spring pools and activities.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Lovely resort, beautiful location, so many things to do!  Service was wonderful, helpful, and friendly.  We enjoyed activities on and off property all scheduled through the concierge, but you could easily use viator for off property excursions too.  You absolutely must go zip lining off property, and try tubing and horseback riding at club rio.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: La Fortuna, Costa Rica is located at the base of the of the Arenal Volcano. Arenal Springs resort is located just 3 km from the Town center. With 7 hot pools you can soak your body after hiking to the waterfall, volcano or the hanging bridges, kayaking the Arenal River, or doing one of many fun day trips found in this area. In the morning you can fuel up with one of the best breakfasts buffets you’ll ever have and not only that, it’s complementary!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Great service, everyone very friendly and helpful, the food is amazing (3 main restaurants).
Very clean, able to book and do activities on site (for extra charge) but all worth it with good value.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Costa Rica

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Very nice and beautiful hotel, built with high-quality materials and a great design. However, my biggest disappointment was that a 5-star hotel did not provide complimentary water in the room, instead selling it for $4.50 per bottle. This is especially surprising in Costa Rica, considering that the room costs over $1000 per night.It feels quite disappointing and even greedy that they are unwilling to meet basic international standards of service. In my opinion, the management should take inspiration from hotels in Asia and the Middle East, where guest service is often much more thoughtful and complete.I felt that instead of providing small essentials as part of the service, they were trying to charge extra for everything, which negatively affected the overall experience.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Beautiful hot spring water pools 👍🏼

There should be more warnings not to put your head underneath the surface, as there is a risk of catching the very dangerous "brain-eating amoebas"!

There are no clocks in the pool bar area, so you might be lost in time 🕔

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Went here for hot springs day pass only. Amazing surroundings with multiple pools of varying temps. Expensive but worth it. Towels changing area showers and lockers provided.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: The room and whole resort was incredible. I had the 4 hands massage at the spa, I would fly to Costa Rica to get that again. They have lots of family games and an onsite excursion area.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Riesige Anlage malerisch in die Natur eingefügt ab 18:00 ist es in den Pools angenehm weil nimmer so viel los ist. Sportliche Wasserrutsche zum Abkühlen. Der Weg lohnt sich.!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Beautiful hotel and springs and spa ! Great for families . Something for everyone to enjoy!!! The food quality needs improvement  and the restaurants have a very corporate feel and everything is way overpriced for the warily they offer, not organic , no fresh juices or smoothies … . The hotel itself seems a bit outdated and there’s the smell of Laundry everywhere. The gift shop is also a very generic shop that you find in the airport and  again over priced . For the price of this hotel a night I did expect a bit more with the food/drink. Also no free breakfast buffet and that would have been nice , even just for the kids !!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Beautiful property, amazing service. Worth every penny imo.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: The most amazing trip our family has had. Location was absolutely beautiful, the service was second to none, every small detail thought of. The rooms were like palaces with so much space. The staff was the most friendly ever. Jeffry and Joe were the best golf cart drivers ever. We will definitely be back. Pura Vida Costa Rica!!!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Coolest resort ever! Beautiful views everywhere you go and definitely not lacking in the activities department! Great for families and for singles so much variety!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Muy bonito

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: This spa felt very natural

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: We came to the Springs resort and spa for our honeymoon! It was an incredible experience. There are so many hot springs and such beautiful views. The food is also amazing. The breakfast was buffet style, and I loved all of the options. For dinner, I had one of the best meals of my life, a Caribbean jerk chicken with a coconut plantain risotto. I am back home dreaming about it! We had a little mixup with a  room package that we had paid for. Initially, when my husband went to talk to the front desk about the issue, the lady he spoke to was not very helpful because she was focused on other people checking in. She was very dismissive to him even after explaining to her that his wife, me, was upset. When he came back to the room, I ended up calling and she told me they’d fix it. They did, and it turned out great. The rest of the staff was amazing. They acknowledged that we were celebrating our honeymoon and would congratulate us. They would always say hi to us when they were near. And they were very friendly and let my husband practice his Spanish 😅 Thank you for your hospitality Esteban and Juan Carlos!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
We just did the Day Pass and ate at Las Ventanas Restaurants. Everyone here was super friendly and accommodating. The food was incredibly delicious and so were the cocktails at the restaurant. The swim up bar was great with perfect water temperature but service is little bit slow. Enjoyed all the pools and had a great time tubing at Club Rio. I hope to come back some day.
Gorgeous property!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Wow.
Incredible.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: One of the best resort hotels I have been to.  Great scenic area.  Staff is super courteous and attentive.  Everything is well maintained within reasons. The on-site restaurants are very good.  One can get a highly refreshing and relaxing stay in this resort.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Precioso lugar, excelente servicio pero bastante over price

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
I can honestly say this place is priced on the higher end — but absolutely worth every penny.

From the moment you arrive, the service is on another level. The staff treats you like royalty — attentive, professional, and genuinely warm. Every request is handled immediately, and they go above and beyond to make your stay unforgettable.

The resort is impeccably clean. Rooms, pools, restaurants, walkways — everything is spotless and beautifully maintained. You can tell they take pride in every detail.

The hot springs are simply spectacular. Multiple pools at different temperatures, stunning views of Arenal Volcano, and plenty of space so it never feels overcrowded. Whether you want relaxation or a more social vibe, there’s a perfect spot for you.

What really makes it stand out is the variety of activities. From the wildlife sanctuary and kayaking to spa treatments, fitness center, hiking trails, and incredible dining options — there is always something to do. You could easily spend days there without ever leaving the property.

Yes, it’s a premium experience — but the quality of service, cleanliness, views, amenities, and overall atmosphere make it completely worth it.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Highly recommended for couples, families, or anyone wanting a luxury experience in Arenal.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Positive: Location… rooms Negative: ""

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
This is a beautiful resort with stunning views of Arenal Volcano. The property is well maintained, and there are numerous activities on site, although you do need to take a shuttle to access most of them. The rooms are a bit outdated for the price point, but comfortable. Most of the staff is friendly and everyone speaks English, which makes things easy for U.S. travelers.

That said, we found the food throughout our stay to be inconsistent and generally average for the price. Unfortunately, our experience took a serious turn on our second-to-last night when our son became violently ill after eating seafood at the resort. He spent the entire night vomiting and was completely unable to participate in our final full day — which happened to be my wife’s birthday.

We reported the situation to the concierge the next morning and were assured multiple times that management would follow up. No one ever did. When we checked out, the front desk had no knowledge of the incident, and we had to explain everything again before our dinner from that night was comped.

At roughly $2,000 per night, you expect a higher level of communication and service recovery when something serious happens. Illness can occur anywhere, but the lack of follow-up from management was extremely disappointing and changed our overall impression of the resort.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
WOW! We would go back here again in a heartbeat!! Our room was perfect! We were on the top (4th) floor so we could see above the treetops w/a spectacular view of the Arenal volcano. We would sit on our balcony in the morning and listen to the birds. The whole first day was spent in all 28 hot springs! They were amazing! Speaking of amazing—the staff! They all treated us as if we were family! We have NEVER been anywhere where the staff was this cordial, accommodating, and FUN!! You have hired the best! First was Eli, who gave us the welcome tour, we didn’t see him again until 4 days later when leaving and he called us by name! WOW! The golf cart driver we had the most often was Jose, I called him Carlos! He was sooo fun, always had a smile on his face, and always called us by name! There was a room attendant that I would always see in the hallway that had such a beautiful smile so I would comment to her on how beautiful her smile was as she was always smiling! The swim-up bar bartenders Jason and Anthony. Again, always smiling and having fun at their jobs. So fun and friendly! They always made time for each and every customer.  And lastly, Rachel. We would her in the kids room, even tho we had no kids there! We were enticed there by the popcorn! Then she was manning the Monkey Slide in one of the springs. We kept seeing her daily and she was a bright spot in that main building! When we were driving through the gates leaving the property for good, Rachel was there and our driver stopped so we could see and talk to her one last time as she had become a good friend of ours! They are ALL keepers and you have hired well!
Highly recommend going white water rafting with Wave Rafting company. Also, Mistico Park Hanging Bridgess Park tour. Also take a trip into LaFortuna and eat at LaFonda 506. Have the Shrinp Ceviche!

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
Gorgeous, amazing, views, hot springs were clean, beautiful everything i had hoped it would be without the hassle! Go early & stay all day. Drinks & food are amazing, swim up bar, plenty of seating areas with food & bars. A bus every 30 mins runs you to the lower park for experiences & the lower hot springs were went there first since those close at 5pm. We saw wildlife every where
There are lots of kids & a waterside. Parking is inside the grounds, bathrooms are clean & everything is well maintained
Check prices online (high season is one day pass, low season is 2 days for same price) food & drinks are appropriately priced for the area, spoke English & accepted USD

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
We recently spent a day at the hot springs resort and purchased a day pass, including access to the restaurant, and overall we had a really wonderful experience.

The atmosphere was absolutely beautiful — it honestly felt like being in a movie. Everything was peaceful, scenic, and very relaxing. Even though it was peak season, it didn’t feel overcrowded at all, which made the experience even more enjoyable. We truly had an amazing day and would definitely recommend it to others. We would even come back again ourselves.

That said, there are a couple of areas that really need improvement.

Unfortunately, some of the staff did not feel very welcoming or attentive. We had to wait quite a long time for towels, and there was no one actively distributing them. We also experienced some issues with the lockers. The first key we were given opened a locker that was already occupied, with someone else’s phone, money, and personal belongings inside. We immediately returned the key and were given another one — but that key didn’t work at all. In the end, I just left it at the counter and used my boyfriend’s locker instead. These situations felt disorganized and could have been handled more professionally.

Additionally, we were disappointed with the food at the restaurant. It was quite overpriced for the quality, and unfortunately we didn’t enjoy it very much.

All in all, it was still a great experience thanks to the incredible setting and overall ambiance. With some improvements in service organization and food quality, this place could truly be exceptional. We would recommend it — just with the hope that these areas improve in the future.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Our group of 10 just spent five incredible days at The Springs Resort & Spa and are already counting down until we can return. Five days honestly didn’t feel long enough.

The grounds are absolutely breathtaking. Everything was so lush, expansive, and immaculately maintained. Every turn feels like a postcard. The hot springs, views of Arenal, wildlife sightings, and endless activities kept both adults and kids fully entertained the entire time (game room is incredible and we felt totally safe letting them explore a bit away from us). It is truly the perfect blend of luxury and adventure.

We stayed in Villa Palacio and it exceeded every expectation. Spacious, beautifully designed, incredibly private, and ideal for families. Waking up to that view every morning was unreal.

The food was also a pleasant surprise. After reading mixed reviews, we weren’t sure what to expect but it was genuinely good and far better than anticipated. Delicious breakfasts, fantastic dinners, and some of the best lattes and cocktails we’ve had at a resort. Everything felt thoughtfully prepared and fresh.

What truly sets this property apart, though, is the staff. The service was exceptional from start to finish. Gustavo, Priscillo, Carolina, and both Randalls went above and beyond to make our stay special. They were warm, attentive, and wonderful with our kids and always anticipating needs before we even asked. The level of hospitality made the experience feel personal and unforgettable.

This resort nails the balance of luxury, comfort, adventure, and family-friendly fun. We left relaxed, happy, and already planning our next visit.

We will absolutely be back. 💛

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Bought a day pass, totally worth a day visit! Lots of fun, great pools. Loads of activities for all ages and abilities.

name: The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal
review_text: Resort gigante, muitas atividades. Bom café da manhã. Os quartos são enormes com camas bem confortáveis. A vista é linda do vulcão, as piscinas termais são uma delicias.
